title: Possible typos in the correction of position and forces for added charges in QM/MM link method
description:
Dear Group,

I found these possible typos in qmmm_links_methods.F, which corrects the position and force for added charges.

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/7d8e3fba28207e7f8beccaed646b9e347e911aa5/src/qmmm_links_methods.F#L151-L152
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/7d8e3fba28207e7f8beccaed646b9e347e911aa5/src/qmmm_links_methods.F#L178-L179

Lines 152 and 179 mistakenly assign `added_charges%add_env(i)%Index1` to `Index2`. Dr. Hutter agrees on the matter and recommends verifying and correcting the code.

Here is the link to the correspondence in Google Group: https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/Ein9ggGQdgg/m/yYuDuY1QAQAJ

Thank you very much!

Xianyuan

title: Improve Spack environment
description:
Follow-up to https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/3149. While I agree that using Spack environments and have everything defined in a YAML file is great, I think installing the dependencies one at a time is not the best way forward (but happy to be proven wrong).

## Rationale

While I agree that it is more explicit to list dependencies, I think it goes against Spack's philosophy. A single root spec in the environment (CP2K in this case) determines what we want to install, and Spack should take care of the dependencies. We shouldn't be worrying about the dependencies, since they are encoded in the Spack package. If some dependencies are not what we want, it likely means that we need to update the Spack package.

For example https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/3149 sets `+openmp` for the `fftw` dependency. But this is [already taken care by the CP2K Spack package](
https://github.com/spack/spack/blob/ce09642922f27e60dc47247a12f6455bd5aeb9b8/var/spack/repos/builtin/packages/cp2k/package.py#L164). So we are basically re-encoding a constraint that is already encoded in the Spack package, and if it wasn't we would probably want to improve the CP2K Spack package.

Additionally, using the CP2K Spack package instead of installing dependencies one-by-one also allows to realize that some constraint we want are not encoded. For example, https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/3149 rightfully set `cmake@3.22:`. I realized that this constraint was missing from the CP2K package, so I added it thus improving the package for everyone: https://github.com/spack/spack/pull/41542/files.

Additionally, setting multiple specs in an environment with variants might end up in unexpected concretizations. The concretizer can ignore/turn-on/turn-off some variants automatically, if it is the only way to satisfy some constraints. Using `requires:` to add constraints on the dependencies if we really want to is much stronger.

## PR Description

This PR does the following:
* Update the CP2K package to the one in Spack upstream
  * It is useful to have a package locally to avoid circular dependencies
  * If the package matches the upstream one closely, it will be easy to port changes upstream
* Use the local CP2K Spack repo (with the local CP2K Spack recipe)
* Define CP2K as a single root spec in the environment
* Use `spack install --only=dependencies` to automatically install the dependencies for CP2K
* Separates the definition of virtual providers from their constraints

## Next steps

* Unify `spack_env_toolchain` and `spack_toolchain`
* Use [Spack build caches](https://spack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/binary_caches.html#spack-buildcache) or other means to avoid re-building dependencies each time (libint is indeed particularly nasty)
* Speed-up Spack builds by [building multiple packages concurrently](https://spack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/environments.html#installing-an-environment) and other means

description:
I decided to use [Spack environments](https://spack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/environments.html) instead of a dummy package with `install --only=dependencies` because it's simpler and more explicit. Later we can add more environment files for other common configurations like CUDA.

One remaining major issue is the build time of libint. In the old toolchain we address this by [pre-generating](https://github.com/cp2k/libint-cp2k) it.

title: Abort run by default on SCF convergence failure
description: The new keyword IGNORE_CONVERGENCE_FAILURE can be set to obtain the old permissive behaviour. It replaces the keyword NOTCONV_STOPALL which enforced until now the strict behaviour which is now the default.

title: Enabling calculations with ECPs (libgrpp library)
description:
This PR introduces a new optional library in CP2K. `libgrpp` (https://github.com/aoleynichenko/libgrpp) is library for the calculation of ECP integrals with GTOs, with a convenient Fortran interface. The toolchain script was modified accordingly.

The bulk of the new code is in `core_ppl.F` and `libgrpp_integrals.F`. It enables energy calculations with ECPs, as is the standard in many quantum chemistry codes. This makes 1:1 comparisons easier. The code was carefully tested with a large array of molecules and ECPs, with results differing by only a few micro Ha from Orca. New regtests were also added.

Note that forces are not yet available, as the Fortran interface of `libgrpp` does not cover gradients as of yet.

title: Deep potential plugin for CP2K implemented from C interface
description:
Enhanced based on #1620 
For the implementation is fully changed, I think it would be better to just open a new PR instead of former old one.

> Deep Potential (DP) is a machine learning potential based on deep neural network, first proposed by Zhang L. et al. (Doi: [10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016)) By learning from coordinates as well as energy and forces of different structures from DFT calculations, a potential describing the whole system could be frozen to a file. By reading the file, program could yield energy, forces etc. from structures, and then MD simulation with DFT accuracy could be fastened greatly. This PR add Deep Potential support for CP2K, with potential file (e.g. graph.pb) supplied, CP2K could run MD simulation.

As mentioned in #1620 , it was not a good practice to introduce C++ flags in CP2K, while at that time DeePMD-kit does not supply C interface. But now everything has changed. Since v2.2.x, [DeePMD-kit C Interface](https://docs.deepmodeling.com/projects/deepmd/en/master/install/install-from-c-library.html) is directly released together with each DeePMD-kit release, making it no need to install `libtensorflow_cc` any more. In this way, C wrapper has been rewritten and now only necessary parameters are added to arch file. 

We also support download from toolchain. To enable the DeePMD-kit feature, `--with-deepmd` flag in toolchain should not be set to no. The following ways installing has been added.
- Please set `--deepmd-mode` to `gpu` if GPU support is needed, and load CUDA environment before installing.
- From exist DeePMD-kit C interface, by setting `--with-deepmd=/path/to/libdeepmd_c`.
- From source code and library from Github, by setting it to `install`. For now, DeePMD-kit v2.2.7 will be installed.
- From system, by adding the header files and library to where ld command could read directly.

title: Add experimental support for DLA-Future eigensolver
description:
This PR introduces [DLA-Future](https://github.com/eth-cscs/DLA-Future), a task-based GPU-enabled dense linear algebra library based on [pika]() (a C++ library for concurrency and parallelism), with focus on eigenvalue problems.

The library is still in development, with focus on performance improvements. Therefore, the integration should be considered experimental.

## Correctness

Modification to CP2K to use DLA-Future for all matrices of size 3x3 and larger while running the regression tests:
```diff
diff --git a/src/input_cp2k_global.F b/src/input_cp2k_global.F
index 146660c2f..fc8eaedb1 100644
--- a/src/input_cp2k_global.F
+++ b/src/input_cp2k_global.F
@@ -215,7 +215,7 @@ CONTAINS
                           "the DLA-Future library. The eigensolver from the ScaLAPACK library is used as fallback "// &
                           "for all smaller cases", &
                           usage="DLAF_NEIGVEC_MIN 512", &
-                          default_i_val=1024)
+                          default_i_val=3)
       CALL section_add_keyword(section, keyword)
       CALL keyword_release(keyword)
 #endif

``` 

CP2K regression tests:

```
mkdir build && cd build

cmake \
    -DCP2K_SCALAPACK_VENDOR=MKL \
    -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON \
    -DCP2K_ENABLE_REGTESTS=ON \
    -DCP2K_USE_MPI_F08=ON \
    -DCP2K_USE_DLAF=ON \
    ..

cd ..

export PATH=$(spack -e ~/git/my-spack/envs/local/cp2k-dlaf/mkl-mt-mpich location -i pika)/bin:$PATH
export CP2K_DATA_DIR=~/git/cp2k/data/

./tests/do_regtest.py local psmp \
    --mpiranks 1 --ompthreads 12 --num_gpus 0 \
    --mpiexec "mpiexec --bind-to core:12 pika-bind --print-bind --"
```

Results:

```
------------------------------- Summary --------------------------------
Number of FAILED  tests 1
Number of WRONG   tests 0
Number of CORRECT tests 2941
Total number of   tests 2942

Summary: correct: 2941 / 2942; failed: 1; 26min
Status: FAILED

*************************** Testing ended ******************************
```

The one failed test is `QS/regtest-pao-2/H2O_pao_equi.inp` but it appears to be a sporadic failure. Re running with `--restrictdir QS/regtest-pao-2` concluded successfully. This particular problem is tracked in https://github.com/RMeli/cp2k/issues/4, and will be further investigated.

## Performance

Performance optimisation of DLA-Future is still an ongoing effort and the library performance will improve with time. For this PR, I benchmarked version `0.3.1` of the library (with pika version `0.20.0`). I added comparisons with ELPA and ScaLAPACK, when available.

Benchmark results are reported for Daint (multi-core: `daint-mc`, P100: `daint-gpu`) and Alps (multi-core: `clariden-mc`, A100: `clariden-nvgpu`, Mi200: `clariden-amdgpu`).

Benchmarks: `QS/H2O-512.inp` and `QS/H2O-1024.inp`, modified to use standard diagonalization.

### Daint

Missing points are due to not enough memory being available for the particular configuration.

#### MC
![eigensolver_h2o-512_daint-mc](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/d0c4a2e2-1489-4548-8e1d-9181603d1e91)
![eigensolver_h2o-1024_daint-mc](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/542d04d6-5f29-4efd-8695-b5732e007543)

#### GPU
![eigensolver_h2o-512_daint-gpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/8f689363-3dff-4cba-8f32-2e1dea1fbf02)
![eigensolver_h2o-1024_daint-gpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/6e4e7f3e-72bf-4593-b8d9-beacd50e0085)


### Alps (Clariden)

Missing points are due to MPI (`cray-mpich`) issues we are encountering with large matrices on multiple nodes. We have seen the same issues on LUMI (with the DLA-Future miniapps) and we are currently investigating the issue. Unfortunately we don't have further details at this time.

```
Fatal error in PMPI_Sendrecv: Other MPI error, error stack:
PMPI_Sendrecv(249).............: MPI_Sendrecv(sbuf=0x149e80010080, scount=104857600, MPI_DOUBLE, dest=8, stag=0, rbuf=0x149eb2010080, rcount=104857600, MPI_DOUBLE, src=8, rtag=0, comm=0x84000005, status=0x7fffe40d4590) failed
MPID_Progress_wait(193)........: 
MPIDI_Progress_test(89)........: 
MPIDI_OFI_handle_cq_error(1062): OFI poll failed (ofi_events.c:1064:MPIDI_OFI_handle_cq_error:Input/output error - NO_TRANSLATION)
events.c:1064:MPIDI_OFI_handle_cq_error:Input/output error - NO_TRANSLATION)
```

Despite these issues, the experimental integration will facilitate further testing and development, as well as early feedback from users.

#### AMD GPUs
![eigensolver_h2o-512_clariden-amdgpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/fccf3db4-cad4-474b-837b-fd0ed48a33d0)
![eigensolver_h2o-1024_clariden-amdgpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/63212e6b-88aa-4d13-a2c9-27f18af34192)

#### NVidia GPUs
![eigensolver_h2o-512_clariden-nvgpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/6892cfb6-3b3b-4038-b437-2b587cedfd4c)
![eigensolver_h2o-1024_clariden-nvgpu](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/11348981/9380da5a-6c30-4ee3-813e-55a2d8de2cf9)

## Spack 

Support for `cp2k@master +dlaf build_system=cmake` is already included in Spack (see https://github.com/spack/spack/pull/40702).

Locally, I used this Spack environment to build CP2K and run the regression tests:
```yaml
spack:
  specs:
  - cp2k@master +libxc +libint +cosma +spglib +dlaf +mpi_f08 build_system=cmake
  packages:
    all:
      require:
        - any_of: [+mpi, "@:"]
        - any_of: [~cuda, "@:"]
        - any_of: [~rocm, "@:"]
    mpi:
      require:
        - mpich
    mpich:
      require:
        - "@4.1: device=ch3 netmod=tcp"
    blas:
      require:
        - intel-mkl
    lapack:
      require:
        - intel-mkl
    scalapack:
      require:
        - intel-mkl
    intel-mkl:
      require:
        - threads=openmp
    cosma:
      require: 
        - +scalapack 
        - +shared
    dbcsr:
      require:
        - +mpi_f08
    dla-future:
      require:
        - "@0.3.1"
    pika:
      require: 
        - "@0.20:"
  view: false
  concretizer:
    unify: true

```

---

## Credits

While not reflected in the commit history, @mtaillefumier and @msimberg contributed to this PR, especially with early integration of the Cholesky decomposition and general comments/suggestions/reviews.

title: Manual: Add keyword mentions and stars

description:
- Enable more fine control of the hubbard correction. It supports only the onsite correction (although sirius supports onsite and intersite) and can handle multiple orbitals per atom type. Hubbard correction involving for 2s - 2p onsite correction is not supported yet because the input format is unsettled (soon).
- Updated SIRIUS to the latest version. This version has major improvements and include new features.
- Updated the spack recipe as well, both mainstream and locally.

title: Initial guess: skip atomic calculation for ghost atoms
Manual: Fix code blocks and add default unit to keywords
[SIRIUS] Add on-site and intersite features.
description:
This PR contains changes allowing calculations of DFT+U and DFT+U+V in the plane wave domain and an updated version of SIRIUS accompanying it. It uses CP2K input files only and information are retrieved from the already present DFT+U input parameters. 

The U+V approximation has its own input section within the DFT section and only works in combination to SIRIUS as CP2K does not support this approximation at the time of writing.

To use the U+V section, include the following to the DFT section of the input file

```
&DFT_PLUS_U ON
      SUBSPACE_METHOD "full_orthogonalization"
&END DFT_PLUS_U
&DFT_U_PLUS_V ON
     & LINK
         ATOM_PAIR 1 2
         ANGULAR_MOMENTUM 1 2
         ATOMIC_LEVEL 2 3
         V 1.24532876
     &END LINK
&END DFT_U_PLUS_V
```
Each atom pair contributing the intersite hubbard coupling should be described with one `&LINK &END LINK` and all keywords within the `link`  section are mandatory.

the `&DFT_PLUS_U ON &END DFT_PLUS_U` controls how the hubbard subspace is orthogonalized. 

- full_orthogonalization : take all atomic orbitals found in the pseudo potentials to orthogonalize the hubbard subspace using the Lowin method
- orthogonalize : Orthogonalize the hubbard wavefunctions within themselves only
- normalize : normalize the hubbard wave functions
- none : take the bare wave functions.

Atoms are referenced by their indices as we have no simple mean to actually describe them without indicating their position. It means that the indexation should be the same all the time.

As the name suggests "intersite" means two distinct atoms. CP2K will exit if the two atoms referenced by the atom_pair parameter are the same and in the unit cell.

for instance 

```
ATOM_PAIR 1 1
TRANSLATION 1 0 0

```  
is allowed but 

```
ATOM_PAIR 1 1
TRANSLATION 0 0 0

```  
will cause an error. intersite should not be used to describe multi-orbitals onsite calculations....

title: Inconsistent result between builtin and LibXC B3LYP
description:
Using input below, builtin B3LYP gives inconsistent result compared to libxc HYB_GGA_XC_B3LYP or HYB_GGA_XC_B3LYP5.
The result is:

```
 builtin               -76.38085340418614 
 HYB_GGA_XC_B3LYP      -74.63525048993552 
 HYB_GGA_XC_B3LYP5     -74.59818843158433 
```

<details><summary>Builtin B3LYP Input</summary>

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD Quickstep
  &DFT
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME EMSL_BASIS_SETS
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL
    &MGRID
      CUTOFF 250
      REL_CUTOFF 50
    &END MGRID
    &QS
      METHOD GAPW
    &END QS
    &POISSON
      PERIODIC NONE
      PSOLVER MT
    &END
    &SCF
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-6
      SCF_GUESS ATOMIC

title: Problem with combining coordination numbers as one collective variable
description:
Dear CP2K developers,
  I'm trying to use the &COMBINE_COLVAR section in CP2K (version 2022.1) to combine two coordination numbers as one collective variable (CV), and output the Lagrange multipliers of this CV. However, I encounter problems in both the AIMD simulation and the Lagrange multipliers. I update an input file as an example to explain these problems. The input file is for an AIMD simulation of a simple system containing 64 water molecules. The difference between the O-H coordination numbers of the O atoms in two water molecules is chosen as the CV (by using the &COMBINE_COLVAR section). It is reasonable to set the difference to be zero, i.e., these two O atoms have same O-H coordination numbers.
  However, after each MD step, CP2K output several warnings with same text:
![image](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/62468272/26d576c7-803a-4a28-9456-904fc31d84e6)
More seriously, all outputted Lagrange multipliers (both Shake and Rattle) are equal to zero:
![image](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/assets/62468272/67089824-8dbf-4768-97a2-1b20296eee15)
I am wondering if there are any problems in my input file, or in CP2K? Thanks in advance!
Sincerely,
Xu

[64H2O_CV-CN.inp.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/13450816/64H2O_CV-CN.inp.txt)

In NEB calculations, each electronic state of a structure may have different multiplicities. Is it possible to change the MULTIPLICITY parameter setting for each step in CP2K's NEB calculation? If not, is it possible to modify the source code to achieve this? As far as I know, NEB calculations rely on stack operations of the force environment. So, is it possible to modify the multiplicity setting for each step?
With my respect, Thank you!

title: Explicitly initialize HIP/CUDA before MPI
description:
On our system, running CP2K on multiple nodes with HIP (version `5.2.3`) results in the following error:
```
ERROR: hipErrorNoDevice /users/rmeli/git/cp2k/src/offload/offload_library.c 51
```

Initializing HIP before MPI circumvents the issue. This PR adds `offload_init` to call `cuInit`/`hipInit` explicitly, before `mpi_world_init`.

I've been told this was an issue on LUMI as well (and apparently it is no longer), but I wasn't able to find additional information.

---

Credit to @msimberg for suggesting the workaround.

Fix Wannier localization when using LOW SPIN ROKS
description:
- Added the ability to calculate Wannier Сenters for the ROKS method (only LOCALIZE NONE and JACOBI).
- Added the error message when ROKS and LOCALIZE were used incorrectly (instead of Segmentation Fault).
- Replaced the output of two spins in ROKS by the output of spin 1 only, since the densities of spin_1/2 are the same.

I have previously raised this issue in the Google Group discussion https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/yJPJS2vUkBI

This Wannier centres analysis is added in analogy to the implementation in CPMD code. See https://github.com/CPMD-code/CPMD/blob/main/src/ddipo_utils.mod.F90, The fragment “ELSEIF (lspin2%tlse) THEN…” means that for singly occupied orbitals the Jacobi rotation is disabled. In this case, the number of orbitals to which the Jacobi rotation can be applied has been reduced by 2 since SOMO-1 and SOMO-2 orbitals should not rotate.

title: Wrong atom kinds with cif file generated by ase
description:
Usually the atom_info of a cif file (e.g. Material Studio) will follow the following order
```
_atom_site_label
_atom_site_type_symbol
_atom_site_fract_x
_atom_site_fract_y
_atom_site_fract_z
Ti0    Ti    0.00000   0.00000   0.00000
```
But cif file generated by ase may follow order like
```
_atom_site_type_symbol
_atom_site_label
_atom_site_fract_x
_atom_site_fract_y
_atom_site_fract_z
Ti  Ti1       0.00000  1.00000  0.02294 
```
which will cause subroutine `read_coordinate_cif` in topology_cif.F take `_atom_site_label` as the atom type. For example, the type of oxygen atom is O1, but the other oxygen atom is O2.
The result is that atom kinds is equal to the number of atoms, which may lead a HF funcitonal calculation cost several times the time. My solution now is turn `USE_ELEMENT_AS_KIND` on. But I still wish this bug can be fixed. The following are my modifications to the code, please let me know if there are any problems. With my respect.
```patch
diff --git a/src/topology_cif.F b/src/topology_cif.F
index 7eab185f1..164a59f47 100644
--- a/src/topology_cif.F
+++ b/src/topology_cif.F
@@ -161,15 +161,15 @@ CONTAINS
       IF (.NOT. found) &
          CPABORT("The field _atom_site_label was not found in CIF file! ")
       ifield = 0
-      ii = 1
-      ifield(ii) = 0
+      ii = 0
+      ! ifield(ii) = 0
       CALL parser_search_string(parser, "_atom_site_", ignore_case=.FALSE., found=found, &
                                 begin_line=.FALSE., search_from_begin_of_file=.TRUE.)
       DO WHILE (INDEX(parser%input_line, "_atom_site_") /= 0)
-         IF (INDEX(parser%input_line, "_atom_site_label") /= 0) THEN
-            CALL parser_get_next_line(parser, 1)
-            CYCLE
-         END IF
+         ! IF (INDEX(parser%input_line, "_atom_site_label") /= 0) THEN
+            ! CALL parser_get_next_line(parser, 1)
+            ! CYCLE
+         ! END IF
          ii = ii + 1
          CPASSERT(ii <= 20)
```

title: Active space: Implement ERI calculation using half-transformed integrals
description:
This PR implements several options which have already been available in the input but were not implemented:
- half-transformed integral calculation algorithm: borrowed from the GPW-MP2 method. It is a much fast algorithm to calculate the integrals, especially if all orbitals are correlated. This implementation is 2-3 faster than the legacy FULL_GPW algorithm.
- store_wfn: this option enables the on-the-fly calculation the orbital functions on a grid. It increases the costs by 40 % in case of half-transformed integrals (no idea in case of the FULL_GPW method but probably much more expensive than the with storing the orbitals). It allows to reduce the memory footprint significantly, especially in case of molecular systems with their larger number of grid points
- subgroups: As in the WFC module, the calculation of integrals is possible in subgroups trading memory for reduced communication. I measured a threefold improvement.
- The new implementation is GPU accelerated as it relies on DBCSR and the grid code for the integration of the potential (untested).
- A bunch of new regression tests (QS/regtest-as-2, QS/regtest-as-3)

This implementation works well with the GPW method but is inadequate if highly localised AOs are present (known issue of GPW). In these cases, a GAPW-like implementation would be advantageous but a matter of implementation.

description:
Time-dependent Field for EMD.

Note that many tests have been changed because of the last change but with a very few impact overall. This implementation should be more readable and maintenable as we use the already computed density matrix.

title: RFE: Need to be able to build and install multiple MPI implementations
description:
The Fedora cp2k package builds for multiple MPI implementations - currently openmpi and mpich.  However with cp2k's current cmake build systems the two builds write their executables to the same location.  Also, the fedora package currently suffixes the executables with the MPI names, e.g. `cp2k.psmp_openmpi`.  I'm not sure this is strictly necessary as they are installed (finally) into different directories (`/usr/lib64/{mpich,openmpi/}bin`), but might be one solution.

Another might be to able to define a suffix to the `__cp2k_cmake_name` variable.

title: Allow usage of wrapper script when running regression tests with MPI
description:
Currently, the `--mpiranks` option is simply appended after the `--mpiexec` option.

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/b1e573e5365af53cd5b64772cda048b47a415cd7/tests/do_regtest.py#L300-L301

This prevents the use of a wrapper scripts while spawning tests.

This PR moves the `-n RANKS` argument just after the first string in `--mpiexec`, so that a wrapper script can be used.

Example:
```bash
./tests/do_regtest.py local psmp \
    --mpiranks 2 --ompthreads 6 --num_gpus 0 \
    --mpiexec "mpiexec --bind-to core:6 wrapper.sh"
```

title: Bump werkzeug from 2.3.3 to 3.0.1 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [werkzeug](https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug) from 2.3.3 to 3.0.1.
Release notes

Sourced from werkzeug's releases.

3.0.1

This is a security release for the 3.0.x feature branch.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/changes/#version-3-0-1

3.0.0

This is a feature release, which includes new features, removes previously deprecated code, and adds new deprecations. The 3.0.x branch is now the supported fix branch, the 2.3.x branch will become a tag marking the end of support for that branch. We encourage everyone to upgrade, and to use a tool such as pip-tools to pin all dependencies and control upgrades. Test with warnings treated as errors to be able to adapt to deprecation warnings early.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/3.0.x/changes/#version-3-0-0
  • Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/21?closed=1

2.3.7

This is a fix release for the 2.3.x feature branch.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/2.3.x/changes/#version-2-3-7
  • Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/33?closed=1

2.3.6

This is a fix release for the 2.3.x feature branch.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/2.3.x/changes/#version-2-3-6
  • Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/32?closed=1

2.3.5

This is a fix release for the 2.3.x feature branch.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/2.3.x/changes/#version-2-3-5
  • Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/31?closed=1

2.3.4

This is a fix release for the 2.3.x release branch.

  • Changes: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/en/2.3.x/changes/#version-2-3-4
  • Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/milestone/30?closed=1
Changelog

Sourced from werkzeug's changelog.

Version 3.0.1

Released 2023-10-24

  • Fix slow multipart parsing for large parts potentially enabling DoS attacks. :cwe:CWE-407

Version 3.0.0

Released 2023-09-30

  • Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:2768
  • Deprecate the __version__ attribute. Use feature detection, or importlib.metadata.version("werkzeug"), instead. :issue:2770
  • generate_password_hash uses scrypt by default. :issue:2769
  • Add the "werkzeug.profiler" item to the WSGI environ dictionary passed to ProfilerMiddleware's filename_format function. It contains the elapsed and time values for the profiled request. :issue:2775
  • Explicitly marked the PathConverter as non path isolating. :pr:2784

Version 2.3.8

Unreleased

Version 2.3.7

Released 2023-08-14

  • Use flit_core instead of setuptools as build backend.
  • Fix parsing of multipart bodies. :issue:2734 Adjust index of last newline in data start. :issue:2761
  • Parsing ints from header values strips spacing first. :issue:2734
  • Fix empty file streaming when testing. :issue:2740
  • Clearer error message when URL rule does not start with slash. :pr:2750
  • Accept q value can be a float without a decimal part. :issue:2751

Version 2.3.6

Released 2023-06-08

  • FileStorage.content_length does not fail if the form data did not provide a value. :issue:2726

... (truncated)

  • b1916c0 Fix: slow multipart parsing for huge files with few CR/LF characters
  • 726eaa2 Release version 3.0.0

  • 4820d8c Provide elapsed and timestamp info to filename_format
  • 599993d Bump pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish from 1.8.8 to 1.8.10 (#2780)
  • a2394ed Bump slsa-framework/slsa-github-generator from 1.7.0 to 1.9.0 (#2779)
  • 1efd6f3 Bump actions/checkout from 3.5.3 to 3.6.0 (#2778)

title: Latex formulae in input description, remove 2 keywords
Prettification issue with INTERFACE and pre-processor directive
description:
If `#if defined` follows immediately after `END INTERFACE`, `make pretty` removes part of the interface.

```diff
diff --git a/pt-2.F b/pt-2.F
index a3a6a473a..fbd2d812b 100644
--- a/pt-2.F
+++ b/pt-2.F
@@ -17,15 +17,14 @@ MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
 
 CONTAINS
 
+! **************************************************************************************************
+!> \brief ...
+! **************************************************************************************************
    SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize()
-      INTERFACE
-         SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux() &
-            BIND(C, name='dlaf_init')
-         END SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux
       END INTERFACE
 
 #if defined(__DLAF)
       CALL dlaf_init_aux()
 #endif
-   END SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize
-END MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
+      END SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize
+   END MODULE cp_dlaf_utils

```

---

The following code snippet allows to reproduce the issue (placed on the root directory of the git repository):
```fortran
!--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------!
!   CP2K: A general program to perform molecular dynamics simulations                              !
!   Copyright 2000-2023 CP2K developers group <https://cp2k.org>                                   !
!                                                                                                  !
!   SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later                                                      !
!--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------!

MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
#include "src/base/base_uses.f90"
   IMPLICIT NONE

   PRIVATE

   CHARACTER(len=*), PARAMETER, PRIVATE :: moduleN = 'cp_dlaf_utils'

   PUBLIC:: cp_dlaf_initialize

CONTAINS

   SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize()
      INTERFACE
         SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux() &
            BIND(C, name='dlaf_init')
         END SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux
      END INTERFACE

#if defined(__DLAF)
      CALL dlaf_init_aux()
#endif
   END SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize
END MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
```

Adding code between `END INTERFACE` and `#if defined` doesn't seem to cause issues:
```fortran
!--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------!
!   CP2K: A general program to perform molecular dynamics simulations                              !
!   Copyright 2000-2023 CP2K developers group <https://cp2k.org>                                   !
!                                                                                                  !
!   SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-or-later                                                      !
!--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------!

MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
#include "src/base/base_uses.f90"
   IMPLICIT NONE

   PRIVATE

   CHARACTER(len=*), PARAMETER, PRIVATE :: moduleN = 'cp_dlaf_utils'

   PUBLIC:: cp_dlaf_initialize

CONTAINS

   SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize()
      INTERFACE
         SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux() &
            BIND(C, name='dlaf_init')
         END SUBROUTINE dlaf_init_aux
      END INTERFACE

      CALL something()

#if defined(__DLAF)
      CALL dlaf_init_aux()
#endif
   END SUBROUTINE cp_dlaf_initialize
END MODULE cp_dlaf_utils
```

`fprettify --diff FILES` does not have any issue with either file.

title: Use MKL implementation of fftw3 by default when mkl is found
description: Added the possibility to compile cp2k with the FFTW3 implementation found in MKL. It can be turned off with  -DCP2K_USE_FFTW3_WITH_MKL=ON

title: Fix documentation for ELPA_FORCE_REDISTRIBUTE
description: PR https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/1515 changed the default value of `ELPA_FORCE_REDISTRIBUTE` from `.FALSE.` to `.TRUE.`, but the corresponding documentation has not been updated.

title: Docs: Fix github link for section descriptions
Docker: Add trigger_path to cmake target
Fix CMake test command message
description: Use `${__cp2k_ext}` instead of `${__cp2k_cmake_exts}` and add `export CP2K_DATA_DIR` suggestion.

title: Actually fix MPI_F08 in CMake
description:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/3063 did not contain the full fix, my bad. I opened it directly from GitHub because the actual changes I had locally were on a messy branch. This PR has been tested locally.

I don't think it would have helped here, but is it possible to automatically trigger the CMake CI action, when CMake files are changed?

title: Docker: Add regtests to CMake test
Fix MPI_F08 in CMake
description: Related to https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/3044

title: Add description for CUDA enable containers
Manual: Improve formating of input description
description:
This PR adds support for:
- Unicode (utf8), which includes e.g. Greek letters and emojis.
- Latex formulas between $ signs, e.g. `$\sum_n \rho(x^n) ...$`
- [Markdown syntax](https://www.markdownguide.org/cheat-sheet/), which provides e.g. text emphasis, lists, tables, and code blocks.

title: Drop apptainer definition files and update docker readme file
Create .git-blame-ignore-revs
description:
I was trying to look for which commit/PR changed some reference values in the test suite, and I hit this roadblock: https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/bb17fff6cb5d3268353275b6fea7c5f276b83cc2.

This PR adds a `.git-blame-ignore-revs` file, which allows to [ignore commits in blame view](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/working-with-files/using-files/viewing-a-file#ignore-commits-in-the-blame-view). The goal is to ignore commits that mess up with the actually interesting commit history. 

I only added https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/bb17fff6cb5d3268353275b6fea7c5f276b83cc2 for the time being, but this file can be populated as needed.

title: RI-HFX| Some optimization
description: Some minor optimization to the RI-HFX code, based on the lessons learned doing KP-RI-HFX. Namely, greater sparsity can be achieved overall by considering the density matrix difference (wrt to previous step) to additively update the KS matrix. As the SCF progresses, the cost of each cycle goes down because the variation in the density is smaller.

H2O linear-scaling post-HF benchmarks; use existing HFX calculation
description:
From what I understand, these examples start the post-HF calculation from a set of PBE orbitals. They proceed on a fairly large number of GPU nodes, yet they begin with a very expensive HF SCF which makes no use of the GPUs and is enormously slow when executed using 1 rank / GPU.
Wouldn't it be more efficient to use the PBE orbitals for a HF SCF (performed with 1-4 cores per rank, on CPU-only nodes), and use the converged HF orbitals as input for the RI-MP2 part, on GPU nodes? This would avoid the GPU idling during calculation of HFX...

(I'm testing on LUMI-G, with the provided Easybuild installation.)

title: HOTFIX: EMD + Time Dependent Electric Field
description:
In the current version of the code, the electric field is applied to the electrons only at the first iteration of each EMD step. 

For RTP: 
The core Hamiltonian is built at each RTP step at the first iteration, see line 133 and following of emd/rt_propagation_methods%propagation_step. In particular, once qs_energies_init is called, the core Hamiltonian is updated with the dipole operator for time-dependent fields (or velocity one for VG).  qs_energies_init is then never called before the end of the RTP step: we loop over the ''good'' core Hamiltonian through one RTP step. 

For EMD: 
It follows the same procedure, but the forces are also computed in qs_force. There, qs_energies_init is called to compute the forces. Which also reset the core Hamiltonian: it should thus be updated with the dipole operator. If not, the electronic Hamiltonian applied for the next iteration of the EMD step is field-free. Yet, the electric field is still applied to the nuclei. This produces a dynamics where the field interacts explicitly with the nuclei but not with the electrons. 

I have performed some tests to confirm that. For instance: run EMD with field and show the total energy for an isolated neutral molecule. This total energy should be independent with respect to the position of the nuclei since the nuclei-field and electron-field energy add up. In today's implementation it is not the case: moving the nuclei position changes the total energy value. With this fix the total energy is indeed position independent. 

This is a problem I have induced in the commit 78ff48d from 24 Feb: RTP: Add velocity gauge. There I have moved the electric field - electron interaction from a grid-based implementation to a matrix one in the core Hamiltonian (in order to have a similar implementation as the velocity gauge case). Therefore, this issue has been a problem since 24 Feb for EMD + field. 

Hence, I have also modified the regtests relative to the EMD + field since they were no longer correct. The obtained values with this fix are almost the same as the ones before the 24 Feb: the error may be explained by the new way of computing the electron-field interaction using matrices instead of the grid.  
 

As this is a potentially important fix, I allow myself to call this PR HOTFIX.  Should we communicate about this issue concerning the previous version of the code? 

Ps: I will soon push EMD for the velocity gauge

title: Projection for EMD
description:
Extend the MO projection for EMD. 

Either the MO coeff is time-independent and the time-dependent (nuclei position dependent) overlap matrix is used for the projection. In this case, the reference MO is not the same in time because it depends on the nuclei position. This case is propagate_ref FALSE

Either, the MO coeff is propagated using the B matrix so that the reference wave function is time-independent. This case is propagate_ref TRUE. 

Regtest added

title: Warning message for shortcut and explicit XC functional usage
description:
Print a warning message if the user explicitly defines an XC functional and simultaneously requests a shortcut.

The strange behaviour:
- If a libxc functional is called together with a shortcut, they are combined, which yields a wrong result.
- If the shortcut is used with a cp2k-type functional, the shortcut is overwritten. If the shortcut and cp2k explicit functionals match, the result will be correct.

See issue #2978

title: [cmake] More modern ways to use cmake (partially)
description:
- Use message("" "") when multiple lines are printed instead of calling message() for each line.

- Use CMakePresents.json for defining default configurations. The options are identical to the old settings. To use them simply input 'cmake --preset MINIMAL,FULL,DEFAULT,SERIAL'. The user gets the default behavior (full control) if '--preset' is not set.

- Minor cleanups, alias definitions etc....

- Add dockerfiles (CPU, cuda, rocm) to create docker images of cp2k with spack. (openmpi is supported, adding mpich is trivial)

- move the compiler options to a json file so we can configure any compiler supported by cmake independently. Compiler versioning remain to be implemented.

 - Add a new way for mixing the MOs using an external .wfn file
 - Add the possibility to use WFN_MIX before an RTP run in the RESTART_WFN case 

Add some tests and update the one with the modified keyword

title: RI-HFX for K-points (with gradients and ADMM)
description:
This PR introduces a new method for RI-HFX with K-point sampling. The exact exchange matrix is calculated in real-space for all interacting periodic images, before it is Fourier transformed to reciprocal space. Then, the complex Hermitian KS matrix is built, and the eigenvalue problem solved for each K-point. Details of the method will be published in a paper in the near future.

The method relies on the DBT library for sparse-tensor algebra, and on the libint library for analytical 3- and 2-center ERI evaluation. Forces and stress tensor calculations are also implemented. The method can be sped-up with the ADMM approximation (ADMM2, ADMMP, ADMMQ and ADMMS).

Careful testing was conducted. Equivalence to Gamma-point supercell calculations was verified, as well as convergence with the number of k-points. DEBUG runs were systematically ran for forces and stress tensors. Four new regtest directories were added, covering KP-RI-HFX energy and forces, with and without ADMM.

@dev-zero was instrumental in helping develop the ADMM approximation with k-point sampling.

title: Experts Only Input
description:
At the last developer meeting, the idea was raised to make our manual more accessible by de-emphasizing less important sections and keywords. I would suggest we label these as  _experts only_.

Since this is a rather profound change, I wanted to ask for comments before diving into the implementation.

title: Regtesting Improvements
description:
I'd like to make a number of improvements to our regtesting script:

1. Give [test types](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/tests/TEST_TYPES) meaningful identifiers instead of just  numbers.

2. Allow to check also other outputs besides the stdout.

3. Allow for multiple checks after running an input file.

As a first step, I'd like to move the [do_regtest.py](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/tools/regtesting/do_regtest.py) script from `cp2k/tools/regtesting` to `cp2k/tests`.

Are there any comments or concerns?

EHRENFEST_DYN with EXT_RESTART and FIXED_ATOMS fails with CPASSERT
description:
Dear developers,

The restart of the EMD from an external file fails when using geometry constraints. It is working OK when there are no such constraints or a small number of atoms is fixed (1 or 2 fixed atoms didn't lead to the error). **Here is the input file I used for restarting**:

&GLOBAL
  PROJECT_NAME Ag6_N2
  RUN_TYPE EHRENFEST_DYN
  PRINT_LEVEL MEDIUM
&END GLOBAL

&EXT_RESTART
 RESTART_FILE_NAME ../Ag6_N2-1_10.restart
 RESTART_DEFAULT T
 RESTART_COUNTERS F
 RESTART_POS T
 RESTART_VEL T
&END EXT_RESTART

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD QS

  &DFT  
  !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL_UZH
    WFN_RESTART_FILE_NAME ../Ag6_N2-RESTART-1_10.rtpwfn

    &QS

    &SCF
      &PRINT

           BACKUP_COPIES 1

        &END RESTART
      &END PRINT
      SCF_GUESS ATOMIC
      MAX_SCF 300
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-7

      &DIAGONALIZATION T
         ALGORITHM STANDARD
      &END DIAGONALIZATION

      &END MIXING

      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
    &END XC

    &REAL_TIME_PROPAGATION
      INITIAL_WFN RT_RESTART

      &END
    &END REAL_TIME_PROPAGATION

    &EFIELD
      INTENSITY 0.175472379E+16
      POLARISATION 0 0 1 ! Polarisation vector of electric field
      PHASE 0 ! Phase offset of the cosine given in multiples of pi (0 in most papers on catalysis)
      WAVELENGTH [nm] 751.4 ! Wavelength of efield field (nm)
      ENVELOP GAUSSIAN ! Shape of the efield pulse used in real-time propagation (RTP) calculations.
      &GAUSSIAN_ENV
        SIGMA 3 ! Width of the gaussian in fs
        T0 10 ! Center of the gaussian envelop (maximum of the gaussian) in fs
      &END GAUSSIAN_ENV
    &END EFIELD
  &END DFT

  &SUBSYS
    &TOPOLOGY
       &CENTER_COORDINATES
       &END CENTER_COORDINATES
      COORD_FILE_NAME ../Ag6-N2-relaxed.xyz
      COORDINATE XYZ
    &END TOPOLOGY

    &CELL

    &END CELL
    
    &KIND Ag

&END FORCE_EVAL

&MOTION

  &END PRINT
  &MD

    TIMESTEP 0.002

       &TEMP_KIND ON
       &END
      &END
  &END MD
  &CONSTRAINT
    CONSTRAINT_INIT T
    &FIXED_ATOMS

&END MOTION

title: compilation

title: Fix initialization issue with GCC8 and GCC9.3
description: This PR partially rewinds #2979 to fix issues with GCC8 and GCC 9.3 (before 9.5, I do no whether 9.4 raises the same issue). As soon as we drop GCC8 and GCC9, we can remove the workaround.

title: Separate PW types
description:
This PR incorporates rank, type and space of the grid into the pw_type. The naming scheme of the new types is "pw_<r/c for real/complex array><1D/3D depending on rank>_<rs/gs for realspace/reciprocal space>_type. This is justified by rank, type and space being fixed during the lifetime of the PW grids.
Code duplication is prevented as much as possible by letting Fypp write the necessary code starting from templates.

Advantages:
- These changes allow the promotion of several runtime checks regarding correct space and data types to compile time checks.
- This PR fixes two minor bugs in the code which were apparently never hit by users.
- Test coverage of the respective module is improved because of the reuse of code fragments.

This PR requires the creation of several copies of the pw_poisson_solve function because function interfaces must be distinguishable if optional arguments are not set. In exchange, I made some simplifications to the different copies.

The pw_copy function now covers all transfers between grids in the same space although not all of them are implemented (and will probably never be used). The old pw_transfer function is dropped in favor of an interface binding the same functions it used to call (pw_copy, pw_gather, pw_scatter, fft_wrap_pw1pw2).

Left for further PRs:
- Further refactoring of common bits used on several occasions (prepares improvements regarding OpenMP)
- Introduction of real2complex and complex2real FFT capabilities of FFTW to save FLOPs
- Acceleration of certain routines (especially scatter and gather operations)
- Switch to pw_types in XC and CDFT modules to prepare OpenMP

description:

Before describing all the bugs that've run into I give you some of the specs of my machine and all the software that has been used in order to run the toolchain.

title: CMake project modernization
description:
I was looking at the cmake build process and I have a few comments about some common cmake standards that should be adopted, especially since `cmake_minimum_required()` is set to `3.22`:
- [ ] Use `FetchContent` with [`FETCHCONTENT_SOURCE_DIR_<uppercaseName>`](https://cmake.org/cmake/help/latest/module/FetchContent.html#variable:FETCHCONTENT_SOURCE_DIR_%3CuppercaseName%3E) pointing to the submodule if necessary. This allows for a native switch between `find_package`, `FetchContent` and `submodule` sources. `FIND_PACKAGE_ARGS` was only introduced in `3.24`, but it can be easily back-ported.
- [ ] Use `[===[` block command and messages instead of multiple `message()`
- [ ] Avoid bundling `FindPackage` unless it is a wrapper that checks for `find_package(CONFIG)` and the cmake bundled `FindPackage` modules. The bundled modules will always have precedence and it is best to first check with the ones maintained on cmake which can have better support.
- [ ] Fortran module files should be installed in a separate directory. E.g. [fortran-std approach](https://github.com/fortran-lang/stdlib/blob/82f35a57063f5db39ac8c469867d560327ff7cd0/config/CMakeLists.txt#L3-L11)
- [ ] Common and useful options like `CP2K_TESTS`, `CP2K_INSTALL` and `CP2K_SHARED_LIBS`, with defaults based on `PROJECT_IS_TOP_LEVEL`. This will make it more friendly to use with `FetchContent`
- [ ] Missing namespaced `ALIAS` libraries/executables, and propagation of variables like `cp2k_VERSION`. This is to make the project equivalent if it's imported via `find_package` or via `FetchContent`.
- [ ] Instead of tests like `if(${cp2k_build_options_up} STREQUAL "FULL")` bundle cmake presets

Also, feel free to copy the test-suite from `spglib` to enable github actions across multiple compiler families

Hope these tips are helpful

title: Docs: Outline methods section
description:
This PR adds an initial outline for our documentation. I've keep the file hierarchy flat to allow for easy restructuring.

The section pages are intended as navigation aid. Besides a brief introduction they should explain how the individual methods relate and compare to each other. 

The leaf pages cover individual methods. For many methods we already have great wiki pages. I'll migrate those over the coming weeks.

Later we could harmonize the method pages so that they each contain the four categories of the [documentation system](https://documentation.divio.com/).

title: MD: Refactor REFTRAJ / EVAL_ENERGY_FORCES keyword
description:
This PR replaces the two confusing logical keywords [EVAL_ENERGY_FORCES](https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/MOTION/MD/REFTRAJ.html#CP2K_INPUT.MOTION.MD.REFTRAJ.EVAL_ENERGY_FORCES) and [EVAL_FORCES](https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/MOTION/MD/REFTRAJ.html#CP2K_INPUT.MOTION.MD.REFTRAJ.EVAL_FORCES) with a single enum keyword that has three options:

- `NONE`: Evaluate nothing
- `ENERGY`: Evaluate only the energy
- `ENERGY_FORCES`: Evaluate energy and forces

The old keywords are marked as deprecated, but still work to retain backwards compatibility.

See also #2974, #894, and https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/40BM0xIXak8/m/NtJ9iYb7BAAJ.

title: Add missing initialisations detected by the Intel compiler
Possible warning message for double calling a XC functional
description:
I recently made the mistake of calling the PBE0 functional in the following way:

      &XC_FUNCTIONAL **PBE**
         &GGA_C_PBE
         &END GGA_C_PBE
         &GGA_X_PBE
            SCALE 0.75
         &END GGA_X_PBE
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL

This certainly gave wrong results and it was not trivial to spot the error in the input. I was thinking some warning can be printed if a keyword and a section are used together. I wanted to do this but I am not sure if the warning should be printed when parsing the input or right before the calculation of the functional starts.

Create two new tests for Velocity Gauge: starting from SCF calculation (vector potential = 0) and restarting from RTP (vector potential != 0).

It is now used in [qs_active_space_utils.F](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/qs_active_space_utils.F#L127-L128). Either the GCC 8 regression test has to be dropped or `FINDLOC` has to be replaced by an equivalent piece of code (function).

title: Bug: Errors into the the toolchain for cp2k version 9.1
description:
Hi everyone , I've been working with cp2k in order to compile the version 9.1 and I think I've fond some bugs into the toolchain. Before describing all the bugs that've run into I give you some of the specs of my machine and all the software that has been used in order to run the toolchain.

## Hw specifics

CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Silver 4210 CPU @ 2.20GHz
Core(s): 20
Socket(s): 2
Core(s) per socket: 10
Thread(s) per core:  1 (Hyperthreading is disabled)
GPU: 4 x Nvidia Tesla V100-sxm2-16gb

## Sw specifics

OS: Rocky Linux release 8.8 (Green Obsidian)
GCC Compiler: gcc-8.5.0 --> OS package
CMAKE: cmake-3.20.2 --> OS package
OpenMPI: ompi-4.1.4 --> Compiled with: gcc-8.5.0 + cuda-12.0.0 + hwloc-2.9.0 + pmix-4.2.2 + gdrcopy-2.3.1 + ucx-1.14.1 + ucc-1.2.0
FFTW: fftw-3.3.10 --> Compiled with: gcc-8.5.0 (single precision)
Plumed: plumed-2.9.0 --> Compiled with: gcc-8.5.0 + ompi-4.1.4 (The verison above) + libtorch2.0.1noabi
CUDA: cuda-12.0.0
Nvidia driver version: 525.60.13

## Command to build the Toolchain

```bash
./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh -j 20 --no-check-certificate --mpi-mode=openmpi --math-mode=openblas --gpu-ver=V100 --libint-lmax=5 --enable-cuda=yes --enable-hip=no --with-gcc=system --with-cmake=system --with-openmpi=system --with-libxc=install --with-fftw=system --with-openblas=install --with-libint=install --with-libxsmm=install --with-elpa=install --with-pexsi=install --with-ptscotch=install --with-superlu=install --with-quip=install --with-plumed=system --with-cosma=install | tee install-cp2k-9.1.0-out.txt
```

## Bugs

1. Building Superlu: If I launch the command like this without say anything about CFLAGS or other vars I get an error while compiling Superlu, the complete error log is pretty long so I will resume it like this:

```bash
undefined reference to `MPI::Comm::Comm()'
...
Other undefined references
...
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make: *** [Makefile:326: amr3d] Error 1
```

(Let me know if you need the complete log)

The error goes away if I use these variables:

```bash
export OMPI_CC=gcc
export OMPI_CXX=g++ 
export OMPI_FC=gfortran
export CC=mpicc
export CXX=mpic++
export FC=mpif90
```

I don't know why the toolchain fails to pass these definitions in order to compile correctly.

2. Stage 7 - Build spglib

The build fails immediately because it tries to download the spglib from the wrong git url, the change I've made is in the script "cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage7/install_spglib.sh" line 37, in particular I've done this substitution:

```bash
# From this:
https://github.com/atztogo/spglib/archive/v${spglib_ver}.tar.gz \

# To this:
https://github.com/spglib/spglib/archive/refs/tags/v${spglib_ver}.tar.gz \
```

3. Stage 8 - Build spfft & sirius

Even if in the file toolchain.env I see that these variable are correctly setted (except for FFTW_ROOT):

```bash
declare -x FFTW3_INCLUDES="-I'/fftw-3.3.10/include'"
declare -x FFTW3_LIBS="IF_MPI(-lfftw3_mpi|) -lfftw3 -lfftw3_omp"
declare -x FFTW_CFLAGS="-I'/fftw-3.3.10/include'"
declare -x FFTW_LDFLAGS="-L'/fftw-3.3.10/lib' -Wl,-rpath='/fftw-3.3.10/lib'"
declare -x FFTW_LIBS="IF_MPI(-lfftw3_mpi|) -lfftw3 -lfftw3_omp"
declare -x FFTW_ROOT=""
```

I had to set in both the installation script for both the build (cpu and cuda) the cmake variables:

```bash
-DFFTW_LIBRARIES=/fftw-3.3.10/lib \
-DFFTW_INCLUDE_DIRS=/fftw-3.3.10/include \
```

In order to make the builds work correctly.

## Extra:

How can I also tell to the installer to install cp2k in a custom path and not in the same directory? Like the `--prefix=/my/custom/path` for the cmake (I've tried using the variable `CURRENT_DIR` but it didn't work)

title: Fix the error that the allocatable attribute globenv%gaussian_rng_stream is not allocated
description: When compiling cp2k with compile options **_-fno-realloc-lhs_** from the [cp2k-debugging](https://www.cp2k.org/dev:debugging) ```shell FCFLAGS = -O1 -fstrict-aliasing -g -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-realloc-lhs \ -fcheck=bounds,do,recursion,pointer -ffree-form $(DFLAGS) ``` An error occurred when executing cp2k: The globenv%gaussian_rng_stream has an ALLOCATABLE attribue ```Fortran TYPE global_environment_type INTEGER :: ref_count = 0 TYPE(rng_stream_type), ALLOCATABLE :: gaussian_rng_stream CHARACTER(LEN=default_string_length) :: diag_library = "ScaLAPACK" ``` The globenv%gaussian_rng_stream should be allocated in subroutine globenv_create ```Fortran SUBROUTINE globenv_create(globenv) TYPE(global_environment_type), POINTER :: globenv CPASSERT(.NOT. ASSOCIATED(globenv)) ALLOCATE (globenv) ALLOCATE (globenv%gaussian_rng_stream) globenv%ref_count = 1 globenv%cp2k_start_time = m_walltime() END SUBROUTINE globenv_create ```

cp2k 2023.2 build failure
description:

title: GPU performance cp2k
description:
Hello everyone,

I have been trying to get cp2k with CUDA working and I partly succeed, but also partly fail. 

I have two systems in which I have tried compilling cp2k with CUDA. my home PC with consumer CPU and GPU (ryzen 5600X and RTX3060ti) and on a supercomputer which has a Intel Xeon Platinum 8360Y and a A100. 

Let me start with the issues of my home PC. I installed the latest CUDA version 12.2 and that is working fine. When I use the toolchain to build cp2k with ELPA however I get some weird error messages (home_PC_toolchain_ELPA.txt). Although the toolchain does not say that an error has occured. However when I continue with the usual steps and try to build cp2k I do get errors (home_PC_build_ELPA1.txt). SOme of these errors are related to ELPA and an other one is related to the LIBS -lz flag in the ARCH file. Removing that flag removes these errors, however the ELPA errors remain (home_PC_build_ELPA2.txt). So I am not able to get a working cp2k version with ELPA installed together with CUDA. I did get a cp2k version version with ELPA working without CUDA support.

Next I tried a toolchain run without ELPA but with cusolvermp. The toolchain ended without error, but during building I did get an error (home_PC_build_cusolvermp.txt). 

Since I cannot get a build working with ELPA or cusolvermp I made a build without them. I then get to the next small issue. within the ARCH files local.psmp and local_CUDA.psmp there is a -lz flag for LIBS which gives an error while building. I solved this by simpling removing said flag, but I do not know what kind of effects this has on cp2k, but the build at least finished without errors. 

Finally this brings me to the performance issues with GPU acceleration. I used the benchmarks/QS/H2O-64.inp as a benchmark and tried with and without GPU to see the difference in speed.  When using a single core I did see a speedup of 2x (H2O-32_single_core_no_GPU.out and H2O-32_single_core_GPU.out). While if I use 4 cores I see roughly a 2x speed decrease! (H2O-32_parallel_4_1.out and H2O-32_GPU_4_1.out) I expected way better GPU performance. Is this just the kind of performance I should expect from cp2k or is there something wrong with the installation? Would EPLA or cusolvermp help with this? I can also imagine that part is due to my weaker and commercial GPU instead of a strong professional GPU.
I just now also noticed that the difference between 1 core with GPU and 4 cores with GPU is more or less nothing. Is that the expected bahviour?

On the supercomputer we do have top end hardware, Intel Xeon Platinum 8360Y and a A100. However, I have less control about the installation of cp2k and therefore I do not know the exact details of how it was installed. On top off that I can only make comparisons between calculations on different nodes. This will change the results a bit, but I still find the GPU performance to be quite lacking. 16 cores without GPU vs 18 cores with a GPU gives roughly a 2x speed decrease! (no_gpu.out and GPU.out) When using a single core instead we see a factor 4 speedup. (no_gpu_single_core.out and GPU_single_core.out)
Now that I have a closer look I see that the difference between 1 core and 16 cores with GPU is almost nothing only like 25%. SO in this case we use top of the line hardware, but we see similair issues with the GPU performance when using multiple cores. Is that what is expected? or is there soemthing wrong with?

Would anybody be able to help me with these issues?

Kind regards,
Roel v/d Ven

[files.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/12438825/files.zip)

title: Build error with Spack
description:
Hi,

I am trying to build cp2k on a RHEL8 cluster. I first tried installing with the `./install_cp2k_toolchain` script, but the script failed multiple times for many different reasons. Moving on, I attempted the cmake installation. It kept failing, as it `could not find 'dbcsr' as a git submodule`. I tried cloning the git repo instead of working with the `.tar.bz2` release, but still hit major roadblocks building `dbcsr`. Finally, I resorted to using spack. Although every dependency builds and installs, `dbcsr` continues to break. Here is the error that I am getting with spack:


```

            decompress status for section .debug_info
  >> 364    /short_path/usernameadmin/spack/opt/spack/linux-rhel8-zen/gcc-8.4.1/libxsmm-1.17-yjtkazarpbo7tnxd6wadbjvirixyr7mh/lib/libxsmmf.a: error adding symbols: File format not recognized
  >> 365    collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
     366    ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.

See build log for details:
  /lsf_tmp/200189397.tmpdir/usernameadmin/spack-stage/spack-stage-dbcsr-2.6.0-3z74th46kzsrztcedwlxc3k3kzjywifq/spack-build-out.txt

==> Warning: Skipping build of cp2k-master-td2wppwjgvdolewl7ssnvyodf57eahvn since dbcsr-2.6.0-3z74th46kzsrztcedwlxc3k3kzjywifq failed
==> Error: cp2k-master-td2wppwjgvdolewl7ssnvyodf57eahvn: Package was not installed

Cheers,
Walid

It seems that there is a case sensitivity bug for the reference potential for the vdw potential you choose in &PAIR_POTENTIAL.

title: Long-range quantum computing (WF) in short-range DFT embedding
description:
This PR revamps the active space module and implements an embedding approach based on range-separated DFT.

An active space Hamiltonian describing a few electrons in a few orbitals embedded in the field of all remaining electrons is solved iteratively with external software.
The current implementation is interfaced to qiskit-nature, which enables the solution of the active space problem on classical and quantum hardware alike.

title: Fix convention: WRITE with hardcoded unit number
Docs: Fix cross-referencing
description:
This change allows us to link to keywords from anywhere within our Sphinx docs and have them checked at build time.

Cross-references can be inserted like this: `[](MOTION.TMC.PRESSURE)` or like this: `[PRESSURE](MOTION.TMC.PRESSURE)`.

Thanks @mkrack for realizing that we can just use simple HTML anchors to preserve old links!

title: Initial commit of scalable, low-memory periodic GW
description:
- low-memory of tensors achieved by repeated calculation of 3-center integrals; memory can be brought down to almost zero by more often calculating 3-center integrals

- scalability should be excellent because every costly operation can be executed in small subgroups; only very little communication between subgroups is needed at the end of tensor operations

- suggestion for easy, user-friendly input:

  &PROPERTIES
    &BANDSTRUCTURE
      &GW
        NUM_TIME_FREQ_POINTS 6
      &END
    &END
  &END

Inside the &BANDSTRUCTURE section, one could also include SOC shifts of electronic levels (in collab. with Anna Hehn), local density of states, projected density of states, semi-empirical GW, etc.

Any ideas for improvement will be welcome!

- still many todos: flexible subgroup sizes, subgroups for kpoint diagonalization, scaling tests on very large unit cells, test of code on GPUs

title: Post-HF calculations on systems with Zr: generating ccTZ/ccQZ basis set
description:
I'd like to run RI-RPA calculations on a system involving Zr, C, O, H. The `BASIS_RI_cc-TZ` does not contain either TZ or QZ basis sets for zirconium, so I'm guessing that this means I will have to parameterize them myself. How should I go about this?
The `BASIS_def2_QZVP_RI_ALL` does contain a QZVP basis for Zr but it appears to be overcomplete because the PBE SCFs often do not converge...

title: XAS_TDP| Fix bug leading to crashes when n_ranks >>> nex_atom
description: When doing XAS_TDP calculations, atomic grids are distributed over the MPI ranks. When the number of ranks is much larger than the number of excited atoms, the grids are over distributed and the code crashes. This PR introduces an upper limit to how many ranks work on a single atomic grid and fixes the issue.

Careful DEBUG calculations were run to insure the forces are now correct in all possible combinations of ADMMS, ADMMQ and ADMMP (GPW, GAPW, LSD, pure HF, hybrid functionals, etc.).

The old regtests concerning these methods were replaced by new ones, which should now catch any mistake in the forces.

title: Issues about the diagonal values of stress tensor by CP2K
description: I tried to use cp2k to calculate stress tensor of liquid water. The test configuration was selected from the DeePMD library (https://www.aissquare.com/datasets/detail?pageType=datasets&name=H2O-Phase-Diagram) I found that the diagonal values of stress tensor calculated by CP2K differ from those calculated by VASP, while the off-diagonal values are similar. It seems that there is a overall offset for the diagonal values of stress tensor. In both cases, the SCAN functional were used. I used TZV2PX-MOLOPT-GTH and GTH-SCAN-POTENTIAL in CP2K. Input and ouput files: [cp2k.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/12190373/cp2k.zip)

description:
Hello,

I am trying to build the latest version of CP2K on LUMI and experienced an error related to hip. I can't tell if it is a problem due to the libraries/modules used or it is related to a feature that was introduced recently (see https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/337eb80bc75522ee88d994c513b00003debc09d2#diff-222702f28550f55c721d63d3f7a72952c48da6f11e7e6a42ac91d2c70f59d45c ).

We get first a warning in src/offload/offload_runtime.h:
 ```
warning: implicit declaration of function 'hipDeviceSetLimit'; did you mean 'hipDeviceGetLimit'? [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
  344 |     OFFLOAD_CHECK(hipDeviceSetLimit(hipLimitMallocHeapSize, required_size));
```

And then the error:
```
offload_runtime.h:344:19: error: use of undeclared identifier 'hipDeviceSetLimit'; did you mean 'hipDeviceGetLimit'?
    OFFLOAD_CHECK(hipDeviceSetLimit(hipLimitMallocHeapSize, required_size));
                  ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
                  hipDeviceGetLimit
```

We did successfully compile and run an older version [commit 9d075a87ba6edda76ea5a496699dead09fac0ad2](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/9d075a87ba6edda76ea5a496699dead09fac0ad2).



In case it can be helpful, here's the list of modules that are loaded:
```
Currently Loaded Modules:
  1) libfabric/1.15.2.0                      5) ModuleLabel/label (S)   9) cray-dsmml/0.2.2       13) cray-fftw/3.3.10.3       17) craype-hugepages2M
  2) craype-network-ofi                      6) lumi-tools/23.04  (S)  10) cray-libsci/22.12.1.1  14) cray-python/3.9.13.1     18) gcc/11.2.0
  3) perftools-base/22.12.0                  7) init-lumi/0.2     (S)  11) PrgEnv-gnu/8.3.3       15) rocm/5.2.3               19) cray-mpich/8.1.23
  4) xpmem/2.5.2-2.4_3.47__gd0f7936.shasta   8) craype/2.7.19          12) craype-x86-trento      16) craype-accel-amd-gfx90a
```

Looking at `/opt/rocm-5.2.3/include/hip/` on LUMI it seems that `hipDeviceSetLimit` is defined only for `nvidia_detail/nvidia_hip_runtime_api.h` but not for the AMD architecture, whereas `hipDeviceGetLimit` is found in `hip/amd_detail/hip_prof_str.h`.

The toolchain ran fine (without sirius) and using the latest cosma version built from source

./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh         --enable-cray          --enable-hip=yes         --gpu-ver=Mi250         --with-cosma=/users/toccigab/scratch/CP2K_v0723/cosma/build/install --with-sirius=no  

Arch file from toolchain without -Werror flags.
[local_hip.psmp.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/12095214/local_hip.psmp.txt).

If anybody has some ideas on what might be going wrong it would be great. Thanks.
Gabriele

title: Help with CP2K docker install
description:
Hi,

I am trying to build a docker container for CP2k which will suit podman builds later on. Currently I am trying to build the following recipe with docker
```
FROM ubuntu:22.04
WORKDIR /opt
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive

RUN \
    apt-get update        &&   \
    apt-get install --yes      \
        build-essential        \
        autoconf               \
        cmake                  \
        flex                   \
        bison                  \
        zlib1g-dev             \
        fftw-dev               \
        fftw3                  \
        intel-mkl              \
        apbs                   \
        libicu-dev             \
        libbz2-dev             \
        libboost-all-dev       \
        libgmp-dev             \
        bc                     \
        libfftw3-dev           \
        automake               \
        lsb-core               \
        libxc-dev              \
        libgsl-dev             \
        libblas-dev            \
        liblapack-dev          \
        git                    \
        unzip                  \
        wget              &&   \
    apt-get clean all
ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

#install miniconda
ENV installer=Miniconda3-py39_4.12.0-Linux-x86_64.sh

RUN wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/$installer && \
    /bin/bash $installer -b -p /opt/miniconda3          && \
    rm -rf $installer

ENV PATH=/opt/miniconda3/bin:$PATH
RUN conda install -y numpy scipy matplotlib setuptools mpi4py


ARG mpich=4.0.3
ARG mpich_prefix=mpich-$mpich

RUN \
    wget https://www.mpich.org/static/downloads/$mpich/$mpich_prefix.tar.gz && \
    tar xvzf $mpich_prefix.tar.gz                                           && \
    cd $mpich_prefix                                                        && \
    ./configure FFLAGS=-fallow-argument-mismatch FCFLAGS=-fallow-argument-mismatch && \
    make -j 16                                                              && \
    make install                                                            && \
    make clean                                                              && \
    cd ..                                                                   && \
    rm -rf $mpich_prefix
RUN /sbin/ldconfig


#Installing CP2K
WORKDIR /opt
RUN cd /opt
RUN git clone -b support/v2023.1 --recursive https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k.git cp2k

# Install requirements for the toolchain.
RUN mkdir cp2k-toolchain
WORKDIR /opt/cp2k-toolchain

# Install the toolchain.
RUN mkdir scripts
RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/VERSION \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/parse_if.py \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/tool_kit.sh \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/common_vars.sh \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/signal_trap.sh \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/get_openblas_arch.sh \
     ./scripts/
RUN cp /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install_cp2k_toolchain.sh .
RUN ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh \
    --install-all \
    --with-gcc=system \
    --with-mpich=system \
    --target-cpu=native \
    --with-cmake=install \ 
    --with-libxc=install \
    --with-libint=install \
    --with-fftw=install \
    --with-scalapack=install \
    --with-libxsmm=install \
    --with-elpa=install \
    --with-pexsi=install \
    --with-plumed=install \
    --with-superlu=install \
    --with-quip=install \
    --with-plumed=install \
    --with-sirius=install \
    --with-gsl=system \
    --with-libvdwxc=install \
    --with-spglib=install \
    --with-hdf5=install \
    --with-spfft=install \
    --with-spla=install \
    --with-cosma=install \
    --with-libvori=install \
    --with-libtorch=install \
    --dry-run

# Dry-run leaves behind config files for the followup install scripts.
# This breaks up the lengthy installation into smaller docker build steps.
RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage0/ ./scripts/stage0/
RUN  ./scripts/stage0/install_stage0.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage1/ ./scripts/stage1/
RUN  ./scripts/stage1/install_stage1.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage2/ ./scripts/stage2/
RUN  ./scripts/stage2/install_stage2.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage3/ ./scripts/stage3/
RUN  ./scripts/stage3/install_stage3.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage4/ ./scripts/stage4/
RUN  ./scripts/stage4/install_stage4.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/ ./scripts/stage5/
RUN  ./scripts/stage5/install_stage5.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage6/ ./scripts/stage6/
RUN  ./scripts/stage6/install_stage6.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage7/ ./scripts/stage7/
RUN  ./scripts/stage7/install_stage7.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage8/ ./scripts/stage8/
RUN  ./scripts/stage8/install_stage8.sh && rm -rf ./build

RUN cp -r /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/arch_base.tmpl \
     /opt/cp2k/tools/toolchain/scripts/generate_arch_files.sh \
     ./scripts/
RUN ./scripts/generate_arch_files.sh && rm -rf ./build

# Install CP2K using local.psmp.
WORKDIR /opt/cp2k
ARG GIT_COMMIT_SHA
RUN /bin/bash -c " \
    if [ -n "${GIT_COMMIT_SHA}" ] ; then echo "git:\${GIT_COMMIT_SHA::7}" > REVISION; fi && \
    mkdir -p arch && \
    ln -vs /opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/arch/local.psmp ./arch/ && \
    echo 'Compiling cp2k...' && \
    source /opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/setup && \
    make -j ARCH=local VERSION=psmp && \
    ln -sf ./cp2k.psmp ./exe/local/cp2k && \
    ln -sf ./cp2k_shell.psmp ./exe/local/cp2k_shell && \
    ln -sf ./graph.psmp ./exe/local/graph && \
    ln -sf ./dumpdcd.psmp ./exe/local/dumpdcd && \
    ln -sf ./xyz2dcd.psmp ./exe/local/xyz2dcd && \
    rm -rf lib obj exe/local/libcp2k_unittest.psmp && \
    ( [ ! -f ./exe/local/cp2k.psmp ] || ldd ./exe/local/cp2k.psmp | grep -q libmpi )"

# Run regression tests.
ARG TESTOPTS
RUN /bin/bash -c " \
    source /opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/setup && \
    ./tools/regtesting/do_regtest.py 'local' 'psmp' "${TESTOPTS}" |& tee regtests.log && \
    rm -rf regtesting"

# Setup entry point for production.
ENV PATH="$PATH:/opt/cp2k/exe/local"
ENV PATH=/opt/cp2k/exe/local:$PATH
ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:/opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/plumed-2.8.1/lib
WORKDIR /mnt
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/bash", "/opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/setup"]
CMD ["cp2k", "--help"]
```

While the compilation is sucessful, when I pull the image on NERSC Perlmutter and run the cp2k executable, I end with 'illegal instruction' output. When running the following example, I get no prompt/error messages on the screen other than 'illegal instruction'. May I please request your help with this?

Thank you!

title: Fix tests failing because of use_ref_cell
description: 4 tests failed with GCC 13.1.0 and OpenMPI 4.1.x, because use_ref_cell was not set correctly. For these tests, the consideration of the reference cell has a significant impact on the applied cutoff causing large deviations in the total energy.

title: fm: Add experimental support for cuSOLVERMp
SOC absorption for TDDFPT
description: We, @annahehn and I, have implemented a probational SOC correction for the tddfpt2 module. Basically, we changed the sums used in the xas_tdp module to use all Orbitals. We checked our implementation against a benchmark used by Christel M. Marian et al. (https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.6b01122) with reasonable deviations for 11 out of 14 small molecules. For the remaining three, dithiin, furan and quinoxaline, the corresponding excitations could not be identified.

```
