title: Unable to build successfully with HIP backends
description:
When I try to include the flags `-D__OFFLOAD_HIP`, my compilation fails. The current build system is very confusing and the following are big concerns in my mind:
- Now we are including all HIP or CUDA backends by default and are having to exclude different backends if we choose to instead of following the general practice of choosing to include only the backends we want to have. This results in files not needed getting included in builds (see examples below).
- When I build for AMD GPUs, the build system seems to include the CUDA backend also? This could be a lack of understanding on my part, but this is causing problems.

Here is an example of building with `-D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP`. Note that `src/dbm/*.cu` and `src/grid/gpu/*.cu` files are being built by hipcc. I also see the `src/grid/hip/*` files being built with hipcc, but there is no problem there. I am able to exclude the dbm backend using `__NO_OFFLOAD_DBM` but the problem with the `grid/gpu` files remains.
```
[2022-10-12T00:08:26.459Z] hipcc -c -D__FFTW3 -D__parallel -D__COSMA -D__SCALAPACK -D__LIBXSMM -D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -I/opt/fftw/include  -D__LIBINT -D__MAX_CONTR=4 -D__LIBXC --amdgpu-target=gfx90a,gfx908,gfx906 -O3 -fdenormal-fp-math=ieee -fcuda-flush-denormals-to-zero -munsafe-fp-atomics -fPIC --std=c++11 /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu
[2022-10-12T00:08:26.962Z] hipcc -c -D__FFTW3 -D__parallel -D__COSMA -D__SCALAPACK -D__LIBXSMM -D__NO_OFFLOAD_PW -D__OFFLOAD_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -I/opt/fftw/include  -D__LIBINT -D__MAX_CONTR=4 -D__LIBXC --amdgpu-target=gfx90a,gfx908,gfx906 -O3 -fdenormal-fp-math=ieee -fcuda-flush-denormals-to-zero -munsafe-fp-atomics -fPIC --std=c++11 /opt/cp2k/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.cu
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [91mIn file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu:15:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] In file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.h:15:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] In file included from /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_internal.h:12:
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] /opt/cp2k/src/dbm/dbm_shard.h:10:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] #include <omp.h>
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z]          ^~~~~~~
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [0m[91m1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] [0m[91mmake[3]: *** [/opt/cp2k/Makefile:521: dbm_multiply_gpu.o] Error 1
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.213Z] make[3]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] [0m[91m/opt/cp2k/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.cu:12:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] #include <omp.h>
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z]          ^~~~~~~
[2022-10-12T00:08:27.465Z] [0m[91m1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
```
These `*.cu` files have some openmp pragmas for which we need to include the `-fopenmp` flag when compiling them. There is a problem with including `-fopenmp` in files compiled with `hipcc` because `hipcc` pulls in the openmp library found in llvm during linking. And since I use GNU compilers for everything else, we end up with `libomp.so` and `libgomp.so` in the final linked binary and performance drops.

If we can remove those openmp pragmas in these files or separate device code and host code into different translation units (compiled with different compilers), that would be one solution. I'd rather not build these files if they are not needed anyway.

title: Is hybrid functional available in CP2K/SIRIUS?

Dear developers,

Hi, I'm trying to calculate PW-only hybrid calculation using SIRIUS in CP2K environment.
But seems like DFA part is working but not the HF part.
Below is my input file and thank you for the help.

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD SIRIUS
  &PW_DFT
    &CONTROL
       PROCESSING_UNIT cpu
       STD_EVP_SOLVER_NAME lapack
       GEN_EVP_SOLVER_NAME lapack
       VERBOSITY 1
    &END CONTROL
    &PARAMETERS
       ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD  pseudopotential
       SMEARING_WIDTH 0.01
       USE_SYMMETRY true
       NUM_MAG_DIMS 0
       GK_CUTOFF 5.0
       PW_CUTOFF 20.00
       ENERGY_TOL 1e-10

    &END PARAMETERS

       NUM_STEPS 20
       SUBSPACE_SIZE 4
       TYPE davidson
       CONVERGE_BY_ENERGY 1
    &END ITERATIVE_SOLVER
    &MIXER
       TYPE broyden2
       MAX_HISTORY 8
    &END MIXER
  &END PW_DFT
  &DFT
    &XC

       &END GGA_C_PBE
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
      &HF
        FRACTION 0.25

        &INTERACTION_POTENTIAL

          CUTOFF_RADIUS  7

        &END
        &MEMORY
          MAX_MEMORY 1000
          EPS_STORAGE_SCALING 0.1
        &END
      &END HF
    &END XC
  &END DFT
  &SUBSYS
    &CELL
A 16 0 0
B 0 16 0
C 0 0 16
    &END CELL
    &COORD
H     7.0    6.0    6.0
H     6.0    7.0    6.0
O     6.2   6.0    6.0

    &KIND H

title: RTP gauges
description:
RTP: Velocity gauge and magnetic delta pulse.
- Refactoring of the delta pulse routine
- Imaginary part of density matrix and KS matrix as kpoint_transitional_type

title: RI-HFX| more consistent GPU memory usage SCF/force
description: The 3-center tensors involved in the force calculations with RI-HFX are typically denser than their SCF counterpart. To insure that the memory usage is consistent between SCF and forces (especially for GPU), an additional layer of batching was added to the latter. This is already done in low-scaling post-HF methods.

title: Continue refactoring of pw_type
description:
This PR
- removes POINTER attributes from many pw_type
- converts (POINTER of) pw_p_type to (POINTER of) pw_type
- partially converts arrays of pw_p_type to arrays of pw_type (currently, we miss densities, XC potentials and derivatives of the Hartree potential)
- makes sporadic use of the BLOCK construct to restrict the scope of variables (should reduce the requirements on the stack)

title: WFC: Add the option to set gap and range externally for RPA/SOS-MP2
description:
These quantities will be written to the restart input file, too, in order to ensure compatible runs of geometry optimizations or MDs.

Misc: Fixes subsequent calculations of RPA/SOS-MP2 with gradients: In case of quartically scaling RPA/SOS-MP2, the integration grids were recalculated although they were supposed to be fixed within a run. The errors should be rather small if the number of integration points is large enough (6-8). Single runs were not affected.

With [GCC 13](https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=105131) we will eventually be able to detect this type of issue ourselves.

title: Remove null pointers if they are passed as optional arguments
description:
Passing NULL() as an optional argument is standard conform to indicate an absent actual argument but does not work with some compilers under special circumstances. My last PR #2294 has triggered them (not on Dashboard) in the context of the eigensolvers. This PR removes the null pointers such that the optional arguments are omitted makes the calls to the eigensolver routines more explicit.

@juerghutter Does this PR fix your issue?

I was testing the consistence in total energies with different version of CP2K with a 5-atom metalic system.
I kept the input the same and grep the total energy from the output, as listed below:

title: DBM: Add HIP support to standalone Makefile
hang with Intel LLVM-based compiler
description:
Hello,

I am on the Intel Fortran compiler team and work on ifx. (I was also a heavy user of CP2K during my physics post-doc back in the day.)

We would really like CP2K to be able to run correctly with ifx. At the moment, it is hanging soon after start-up with the H2O benchmarks. I have been able to trace the issue back to the timers in the timeset routine in `src/common/timings.F`:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L228-L229
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L280-L281
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L308-L309
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/common/timings.F#L383-L384

You have in two places this timeset routines that look like this:
```
!$OMP MASTER
!$OMP CRITICAL(time_setstop)
...
!$OMP END CRITICAL(time_setstop)
!$OMP END MASTER
```

The CRITICAL inside the MASTER is redundant and unfortunately causing issues with Intel OpenMP (based on the LLVM OpenMP). MASTER means only the MASTER thread will execute, so there is no need to have a nested CRITICAL.

The simple solution is to get rid of the !$OMP CRITICAL and !$OMP END CRITICAL sections statements in the lines that I have indicated. Is the CP2K team willing and able to do this?

Thanks in advance,
Nick Romero

description: Useful for debugging if there is not enough memory.

title: Removal of never used parameter and process maps
description:
More cleanup of the code
- print_count of cp_(c)fm_type
- process maps in WFC code (rewritten with modulo)

title: Refactoring of the fm module
description: This PR makes the local_data component of the cp_fm_type and cp_cfm_type CONTIGUOUS which prevents accidental deallocations or reassociation to non-contiguous data. Contiguity is required for the underlying libraries. Further, some POINTER attributes are removed in the fm directory where possible.

title: Reduce communication of degenerate part of MP2
description:
This PR tackles the high communication costs of the degenerate part of MP2. In case of geometry optimizations or systems of high symmetry like molecular crystals, there is a large number of nearly degenerate occupied orbital pairs which have to be treated explicitly. This PR
- introduces a blocking scheme similar to the one of the original code
- refactors the code
- adds an estimate of the left computation time

title: Low-scaling post-HF| fix bug with sparsity pattern of density matrices
description: In some rare cases, the low-scaling RPA/SOS-MP2 forces would crash in the response calculation. This was due to density matrices with the wrong sparsity pattern, and subroutine using them with the assumption that they had the sparsity of the overlap matrix. This condition is now enforced.

title: xTB and sTDA cutoff for short range Coulomb term
Add RPA gradients
description:
This PR adds gradients of the quartically scaling RPA kernel. The code was tested locally and on Piz Daint but not yet on LUMI-G. The code was optimized to reduce the memory footprint on CP2K side. It reuses as much of the SOS-MP2 and MP2-gradient code as possible.

The code employs COSMA but might require significantly more memory than before. Local DGEMM calls make use of SpLA. 

To my experience, the memory requirements increase significantly with COSMA (64 water molecules run well, larger systems don't because of COSMA). I do not know how SpLA increases the requirements. Thus, some more tuning might be required. Probably, machines with more memory like LUMI-G might not be affected.

This PR replaces the SIZE_INTEG_FREQ_GROUP keywords by the NUM_INTEG_GROUP keywords because of easier use.

title: [SIRIUS] libvdwxc functionality

title: Store 3c derivative tensors in compressed format
description: In RI-HFX and low-scaling RPA/SOS-MP2, the 3-center tensors containing the derivatives are now stored in a compressed form, the same way that integrals are. This should reduce the memory footprint of such calculations.

title: Building v2022.1 with HIP: hipcc cannot find 'omp.h'
description:
Hi!

I am trying building CP2K with HIP (ROCm ver. 5.0):

After building the toolchain

``./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --with-mpich --with-elpa=no --with-cosma=no --enable-hip --gpu-ver=Mi50``

I do the make

``make -j 8 ARCH=local_hip VERSION=psmp``

but it fails with

```
/opt/rocm//hip/bin/hipcc -c -fopenmp -fPIE -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -g --offload-arch=gfx906 -O3 --std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -D__LIBXSMM   -D__MKL -D__FFTW3   -D__LIBINT -D__LIBXC    -D__SPGLIB -D__LIBVORI  -D__OFFLOAD_GEMM       -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -D__OFFLOAD_HIP   -D__DBCSR_ACC -I/opt/rocm//include /mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu
In file included from /mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.cu:15:
In file included from /mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_gpu.h:15:
In file included from /mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/dbm/dbm_multiply_internal.h:11:
/mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/dbm/dbm_shard.h:10:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
#include <omp.h>
         ^~~~~~~
1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
```

If I add ``-D__NO_OFFLOAD_DBM`` it fails too

```
/opt/rocm//hip/bin/hipcc -c -fPIE -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -g --offload-arch=gfx906 -O3 --std=c++11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -D__LIBXSMM   -D__MKL -D__FFTW3   -D__LIBINT -D__LIBXC    -D__SPGLIB -D__LIBVORI  -D__OFFLOAD_GEMM       -D__HIP_PLATFORM_AMD__ -D__OFFLOAD_HIP   -D__DBCSR_ACC -D__NO_OFFLOAD_DBM -I/opt/rocm//include /mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_collocate.cu
/mnt/storage/home/vvsteg/repos/cp2k/cp2k-2022.1/src/grid/gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.cu:12:10: fatal error: 'omp.h' file not found
#include <omp.h>
         ^~~~~~~
1 error generated when compiling for gfx906.
```

And only if I add ``-D__NO_OFFLOAD_GRID`` the building is successful.

As far as I understand, the problem is connected with the general problem  that ``hipcc`` (clang) does not see ``omp.h``

Please let me know if it is possible to make a full HIP build without switching off those 2 GPU acceleration options?

NB: Building for CUDA (``--enable-cuda``) does not show such a problem.

Best regards,
Vladimir

title: RI-RPA| Allow for XC correction in ADMM RI-RPA
description:
This PR is a general upgrade of RPA when ADMM is used. There are 3 main aspects:

1) Previously, any usage of ADMM when doing RPA calculations (switched on with `RI_RPA%ADMM`) ignored the GGA exchange correction. This simply meant doing HFX in a smaller basis, which is a rather strong approximation. This is now fixed, also for GW.

2) Because ADMM operations in RPA rely on the `qs_env%admm_env`, it was not possible to do ADMM RPA@PBE, which is arguably the most efficient way of doing RPA. This is now possible (with XC correction) thanks to a small refactoring of general ADMM. This also means that RPA specific `xc_section_aux` and `xc_section_primary` sections are created and stored.

3) The  `RI_RPA%HF` input section governing EXX calculations is, in most cases, identical to the `DFT%XC%HF` section with the exception of the FRACTION keyword. Therefore, efficiency and memory usage can be improved by reusing the HFX data from the `qs_env` and rescaling. This is now automatically done if the two HF section only differ by the FRACTION keyword (this is also true without ADMM).

New regtests for ADMM RPA@PBE were added and results for ADMM RPA@PBE0 were updated.

A more standard --prefix or DESTDIR option would make the build steps a little more consistent, but in absence of that just including the step(s) in the documentation would be helpful.

title: Pexsi does not work with mpi error
description:
I've built cp2k.ssmp 2022.1 with Cygwin on Windows 10.The built binary cannot process pexsi regtest inputs with the following mpi error.

``
*** The MPI_Comm_f2c() function was called before MPI_INIT was invoked. 
*** This is disallowed by the MPI standard.  
*** Your MPI job will now abort. 
Local abort before MPI_INIT completed completed successfully, but am not able to aggregate error messages, and not able to guarantee that all other processes were killed!
``

The process aborted after writing the Linear scaling SCF section on the output.

````
------------------------------ Linear scaling SCF -----------------------------
 eps_scf:                                                              0.100E-07
 eps_filter:                                                           0.000E+00
 Density mixing in g-space:                                       BROYDEN_MIXING
 max_scf:                                                                     50
 fixed chemical potential (mu)                                                 F
 has unit metric:                                                              F
 Computing inv(S):                                                             F
 Computing sqrt(S):                                                            F
 Computing S preconditioner                                                    F
 S sqrt method:                                                     NEWTONSCHULZ
 S sqrt order:                                                                 3
 Extrapolation order:                                                          4
 Use single precision matrices                                                 F
 S preconditioner type                                                      NONE
 Polarized Atomic Orbitals (PAO)                                               F
 Purification method                                             PEXSI
 Cluster type                                                             ATOMIC
 Computing Chebyshev                                                       FALSE
 Using PAO                                                                     F
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
blank after this
````

cp2k 9.1 has the same problem, cp2k 8.1 and 8.2 with pexsi v1.2.0 has no problem.

Pexsi prerequisites (parmetis,scotch,upcxx,sympack and superlu_dist) were re-compiled, but this had no effect.

Any idea?

title: Add SOS-MP2 gradients
description:
This PR adds the kernels for SOS-MP2 gradients. Common infrastructure was used as much as possible.

With this PR, the WF_CORRELATION and RI_MP2 sections change slightly to prevent doubly set sections (especially with the still to be added RPA gradients) and complicated code paths. The CPHF section and the keywords EPS_CANONICAL and FREE_HFX_BUFFER are moved into their one CANONICAL_GRADIENTS section.

For reasons of efficiency of dot products, I interchanged rows and columns of the fm_mat_S matrix used in the whole RPA code base.

description:
Can one of the admins add the new tarball (https://github.com/eth-cscs/COSMA/releases/download/v2.6.0/COSMA-v2.6.0.tar.gz) ?

Questions/Comments for the GPU-specialists:
1. The new release enables GPU2GPU communication which requires to set a new variable -DCOSMA_WITH_GPU_AWARE_MPI for COSMA. As far as I know, some people are working on this kind of transfers in other parts of the code. Would it be better to have an additional flag for the whole toolchain or for COSMA only? Probably, we cannot hope that all MPI implementations are GPU aware, can we?
2. Related to question 1, COSMA can use the NCCL and RCCL libraries for enhanced communication.
3. There is now support for AMD GPUs.

title: Restore version number for development branch
description:
Essentially a rollback of #2188 because tools like AiiDA rely on the version number for dealing with output format changes.

Since we switched the versioning scheme to `<year>.<number>` and most tools assume that the version can be cast to float, there is no good way to assign development versions. Hence, we'll simply show the latest release version with the disclaimer `(Development Version)`.

See also https://github.com/aiidateam/aiida-cp2k/issues/163.

title: RI_HFX| fix RI_FLAVOR test with smearing
description: An erroneous test prevented RI_HFX to run with smearing whatever the RI_FLAVOR, whereas it should have allowed it for RI_FLAVOR RHO.

title: Refactoring of the ADMM environment
description:
This is a low-level refactoring of the ADMM environment. There were many ADMM related quantities (`aux_fit` densities, `aux_fit` KS and overlap matrices, `aux_fit` task lists, `aux_fit` MO coefficients, etc.) scattered across the code. All of these are now gathered together in the `admm_env`, itself part of the `qs_env`.

Moreover, the initialization of these quantities was also generally done all over the code. This is not the case anymore, as all these were moved to the `hfx_admm_init` routine. The same was done for the calculation of other ADMM related quantities, which were moved to new routines in the `admm_methods.F` module (`calc_admm_mo_derivatives`, `calc_admm_ovlp_forces`, `admm_projection_derivative`).

There is no visible change for the users of CP2K. This is solely an effort to improve the code.

title: Stop the user from using non-implemented TDDFT kernels
description: There was no safeguard preventing users from defining some non-implemented TDDFT kernels. In particular, calculations using a different `HF` section in the definition of the kernel and in the main `DFT%XC` could go ahead. Since TDDFT relies on the `DFT%XC%HF` section exclusively, results would have been wrong (without warning). This is not the case anymore thanks to a `CPABORT`. Note that the newly defined `compare_hfx_sections` function might soon have other uses.

DBM: Fix array bounds of per_thread_counters
description: This bug only triggered with >64 threads. Great find @mkrack!

title: Fix 2nd order GAPW properties
description: This PR fixes incorrect 2nd order properties of GAPW calculations introduced in #1974 .

title: Remove version number from development branch
Minor sparse tensor copy optimization
description: A very minor performance enhancement. Wherever possible, tensor copies made between tensor with the same distribution to save on communication. Affects RI-HFX and low-scaling RPA/SOS-MP2 forces.

title: Feature request: neural network potential training input printout
description:
Now CP2K already supports NNP, which is based on BPNN. 
Input see https://manual.cp2k.org/cp2k-9_1-branch/CP2K_INPUT/FORCE_EVAL/NNP.html

It will be nice to have the printout section, which can be directly used as a training dataset.
This part could be added in the section %MOTION%PRINT%NNP

The format is shown below and all units are in a.u. 

begin
comment ( which can be the title from xyz printout)
lattice  AX AY AZ
lattice  BX BY BZ
lattice  CX CY CY
atom  rx  rx rz   atom_kind   0   0   fx fy fz   
.
.
energy etot
charge 0
end

title: Optionally print peak memory usage after each MD/Opt step
description: A new keyword, `MEMORY_INFO`,  was introduced in `&MOTION%PRINT`. If set to .TRUE. (its default value), peak memory usage is sampled and printed at each MD/Optimization step. This should help identify the presence of memory leaks when jobs crash randomly.

title: Print input selection for PERIODIC_EFIELD in output
toolchain scripts ignore --with-mkl=no if MKLROOT variable is set for some packages
description:
As the title indicates I was trying to install the current git version of cp2k on some of our local clusters that doesn't have the `c++` bindings for mkl installed. I.e. I can only compile fortran applications with MKL and therefore I have to run `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --install-all --with-mkl=no` to get a clean install of the toolchain libraries. That doesn't work for some libraries however, `COSMO` being the first that fails. Since I guess that cmake automatically detects my local mkl install if I have set the environment variable `MKLROOT` to point to the MKL directory.

I was eventually able to build the toolchain and cp2k successfully after unsetting this variable. So I was wondering if this is the intended behaviour, or if `--with-mkl=no` should somehow make sure that a local version of mkl is not used by accident.

title: Fix name collision due to dbm_tests
description: Follow up to #2173.

title: Voronoi Update
description:
(-) Added more checks and diagnostic output to the Voronoi integration.
(-) Added JITTER option to mitigate issues with highly symmetric structures.
(-) Fixed issue where SKIP_FIRST did not work.
This update requires libvori version 220621, which can be found on
https://brehm-research.de/files/libvori-220621.tar.gz

title: Read cell parameters from REFTRAJ file generated with dumpdcd
description:
- Cell parameters written by dumpdcd to the XMOL comment line are automatically read during a CP2K REFTRAJ run.
- The CP2K cell file is ignored in this case even if present, because the coordinates and the cell dumped from a CP2K DCD trajectory file written with DCD_ALIGNED_CELL are different.
- Improved checks and error messages for REFTRAJ runs.

title: MP2: Improve communication and memory management
description:
This PR
 - extends the idea of partial replication and blocking to open-shell systems
 - implements a reasonable automatic determination of block sizes
 - adjusts the regtests accordingly
 - changes the communication buffer to a central one to prevent OOM from too many memory (de-)allocation steps and the use of pointers to access the required parts of this buffer
 - improves the description of a few keywords

Some comments:
 - The employed memory estimates are very conservative, i.e. although we allocate a central buffer to support all required communication steps, we still consider them separately in our memory model. In that fashion, the MPI communication should not be affected too much as MPI may use its own buffers.
 - The commits will be squashed later

title: fix HFX summation over neighboring cells for 1d and 2d periodic case
description:
Previous status: In case of 1d or 2d periodic systems, 4-center HFX integrals have been computed by a sum over all 3d-periodic neighboring cells. In a practical 1d- and 2d-periodic calculation, the arising issue could be circumvented by choosing a large cell size in the non-periodic direction. However, this is not practical in some cases, for example for a 2d-periodic unit cell that is large in the two periodic directions, but small in the non-periodic direction. 

Fix: Sum for 4-center HFX integrals only running over neighboring cells in periodic directions; do not sum over neighboring cells in non-periodic directions. 

Some benchmark data is collected in a github repository on the 2d-periodic MoS2 monolayer:

https://github.com/JWilhelm/MoS2_HFX_CP2K

I would be happy about comments or opinions to correct and/or further improve the PR.

title: PRINT_LEVEL influences the multigrid analysis
description:
I'm doing the "converging_cutoff" exercise in https://www.cp2k.org/howto:converging_cutoff , I find that changing the PRINT_LEVEL option from MEDIUM to HIGH, I get different multigrid analysis result, see the picture below. Do you know what's the reason?
![cp2k](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/61447011/170643435-dc82bfaf-395d-4679-8e90-fba163821076.png)

title: Added Mulliken and Hirshfeld charges to the results framework
description: As mentioned in #2130 I have added the` [MULLIKEN-CHARGES]` and `[HIRSHFELD-CHARGES]` to the fortran/C++ interface for CP2K.

title: DBT: Fix array bounds in dbt_reshape_ops
description: Follow up to #2132.

title: Further optimize OpenMP performance of tensor code
RPA| Low-scaling gradients
description: Forces and stress tensors were implemented for the low-scaling RPA method. Most of the routines written for the low-scaling Laplace-SOS-MP2 gradients (PR #2031) were reused, as the equation differ only marginally. This was thoroughly tested with debug runs, and regtests were added. As for SOS-MP2, most (all ?) necessary features (ADMM, double hybrids, tau functionals, etc.) are covered.

title: Getting Hirshfeld / Mulliken Charges from the LIBCP2K Interface
description:
As the title suggests I would like to be able to get the Hirshfeld or Mulliken charges using the general get results interface provided in `src/start/libcp2k.F`.
As far as I can tell implementing something that would enable one to be able to use the following `call get_result_r1(env, '[HIRSHFELD-CHARGE]', natoms, charges, ierr)` would be rather straight forward using the methods provided in `src/common/cp_result_methods.F`.

title: Consider converting the build system to fpm, fortran package manager
description: I have been hearing a lot of success stories around using fpm to build applications.  Although I have not tried converting a project, it is my understanding that it is fairly easy to do and powerful.  I will try to explore this a little, but those most familiar with the dependencies will know best.

title: make psmp test and fail to the tests because of QS/regtest-rma

title: Add OpenMP to most of the tensor code
description:
This PR adds OpenMP to most of the tensor code. A notable exception is `dbm_multiply` which will get its own PR.

I've added asserts to ensure that `dbm_reserve_blocks` and `dbm_iterator_start` are always called from within an OpenMP parallel region.

title: CDFT Hirshfeld forces convention fix
description: This update fixes a convention violation recently reported at https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/2113 for https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/2111

title: Update CDFT howto for new Hirshfeld force implementation
description:
I would like to update the CDFT howto (https://www.cp2k.org/howto:cdft) to reflect the newly implemented Hirshfeld CDFT forces (https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/2111). 

Attached is a folder containing: pdf of the suggested changes, as well as a .html file with these changes. 
[howto_cdft.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/8709266/howto_cdft.zip)

title: CDFT forces based on Hirshfeld partitioning of the electron density
description:
This update allows for CDFT force calculations to be performed with Hirshfeld partitioning of the electron density, which provides a more robust description of atomic charges than Becke partitioning. A paper based on this implementation has recently been accepted in the Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation: Ahart, C. S., Rosso, K. M. & Blumberger, J. Implementation and validation of constrained density functional theory forces in the CP2K package. Accepted (2022).

The changes are to the files: input_cp2k_dft.F, qs_cdft_types.F, qs_cdft_utils.F and qs_cdft_methods.F. I have added a new regtest for the force implementation in regtest-cdft-hirshfeld-3.

title: Toolchain: Link MPI dynamically
description: Fixes #2058.

title: Incorrect warning particle_methods.F
description:
Dear cp2k Team

In a simulation with an asymmetric .xyz file, I get a warning that a few atoms are too close but they are on opposite sides of the y-axis. My guess is that there is a bug in indexing.

```
...
16>>> Au      -5.10000000     -10.20000000     -3.06000000
...
21>>>Au      -5.10000000      10.20000000     -3.06000000
...
```

` *** WARNING in particle_methods.F:684 :: The distance between the atoms  ***
 *** 16 and 21 is only 0.400 angstrom and thus smaller than the threshold ***
 *** of 0.500 angstrom                                                    ***
`

The .inp and .xyz files are attached. Note that this occurs on a local Haswell machine with 2 sockets with 8 cores each as well as at cscs.ch Piz Daint gpu node.
[cp2k-particle_methods-bug.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/8630222/cp2k-particle_methods-bug.zip)

description:
In commit 1b077fe44c713442c7e325725758c8dee54eeced, `whittaker_c0a` was
introduced to replace `whittaker_c0`. This function evaluates the
integral along with 1/r^(l2+1) to remove noise when r is extremely
small. However, the documentation was partly copied from that of
`whittaker_c0` and therefore the description does not match the purpose
of the function. Update the description to match it.

title: Compiled PSMP too slow and fails the tests
description:
I have installed cp2k v9.1 recently using the toolchain method, with openmpi v4.1 and without sirius. Previously, I have also tried to use `--with-openmpi=install`, but my local openmpi is of v2.1, which leads to some illegal instruction error when compiling.

Here is the exact command I used to build the toolchain:
`/bin/bash install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --with-libxsmm=install --with-fftw=install  --with-openmpi=install --with-sirius=no --with-elpa=no`

After `source ./install/setup`, I compile cp2k using the following command:
`make -j 32 ARCH=local VERSION="ssmp psmp"`

The compiled ssmp version successfully passed all the tests, but the psmp version fails due to lots of timeouts and wrong results. The machine I used has Intel Xeon Gold 6226 Processor, and I utilized 32 cores and 128 GB output. Following #2048, I used the following command:
`make ARCH=local VERSION="psmp" test TESTOPTS="--maxtasks 8"`. Changing the maxtasks option to a larger number, say 32, doesn't makes the test any faster. 

Here is a summary of the output:

```

......
------------------------------- Timings --------------------------------

It seems like the mpi ranks, workers and threads are not following my command. How can I control the number of processes and threads to use for cp2k? What is potentially going wrong here? title: Should we remove GCC from the Toolchain?
description:
I'm contemplating to remove GCC from our Toolchain. It takes 10 minutes to build in the CI and adds 655MB to our Docker images.

When GCC was originally added to the Toolchain we were still suffering from numerous compiler bugs around modern Fortran. Furthermore, we were experimenting with Thread Sanitizer. Both is not the case anymore.

So, it seems nowadays we could just always use the GCC provided by the Linux distribution.

title: Docker image compatibility with shifter
description:
The docker image cp2k/cp2k:9.1 has at least two compatibility issues with [shifter](https://www.nersc.gov/research-and-development/user-defined-images/) (one of the popular ways of running docker images on HPC systems).

1. The use of `--disable-shared` in the build of MPICH results in a static link of `libmpi` to the executables. This means one can not bind mount the system MPI library (relying on MPICH ABI compatibility) in order to run with MPI on multiple nodes with good performance.

2. The ENTRYPOINT method tries to write files within the image after it is created. With shifter the image is squashed making it read-only. As a work-around one could bind mount in a directory that could be written to, but the `cp2k` links are being put into non-empty directories which means a writeable overlayfs needed which shifter doesn't support. Usually `/tmp` is a writeable by default directory available in these systems. Is there a good reason to do this environment setup in the entrypoint as opposed to putting the binaries in default PATH's?

2 is probably specific to shifter and might not be an issue with other things like podman or singularity, but 1 might be an issue for other HPC container runtimes.

title: Fix MP2 I/O
description:
There was a problem with the I/O in GPW MP2. Each time GPW integrals are to be computed, the `prepare_gpw` routine is called on each MPI sub-communicator. This leads to PW grid info to be printed to the output, by each sub-communicator.

This was a massive mess, as the default is on sub-communicator per MPI rank. Moreover, most of the GRID info would be printed from the beginning of the output file, therefore gradually overwriting the rest of the info (especially annoying during a MD run). As a fix, I disabled I/O if the call is made from a sub-communicator. I believe no info is lost since PW_CUTOFF and other GPW parameters are printed to the output anyways.

I also added a new keyword to control the printing of DGEMM performance info to the output file. Currently, every single DGEMM call of the MP2 code leads to 3 lines being printed. This results in some 1000s of lines per MD step, which kind of pollutes the output file. The new default is to only print the average DGEMM flop rate per MPI rank, but the full output can be recovered by setting the new `PRINT_DGEMM_INFO` keyword.

title: Possible Static Initialization Order Fiasco in COSMA
description:
Hi, 
After successfully installing cp2k version 9.1, running cp2k.psmp produces a Segmentation Fault.
Do you have any ideas?
cp2k.ssmp works properly.

Thanks in advanced for helping me.

**System details:**
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009
NVIDIA Tesla V100
gcc version 9.3.1
openmpi version 4.1.1
cuda 11.1
cmake 3.18.4 (also tried using --with-cmake=install)

**Installation steps:**
```
conda create -n cp2k-9.1.0
source activate cp2k-9.1.0
conda install pip
pip install ford
pip install fypp
pip install numpy

export VERSION=9.1.0

cd $CURRENT_DIR

git clone --branch support/v9.1 --recursive https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k.git cp2k-${VERSION}

cd cp2k-${VERSION}/tools/toolchain

export OMPI_CC=gcc
export OMPI_CXX=g++ 
export OMPI_FC=gfortran
export CC=mpicc
export CXX=mpic++
export FC=mpif90

export CUDA_PATH=/apps/cuda/cuda-11.1/
export CUDA_HOME=/apps/cuda/cuda-11.1/

export OMP_NUM_THREADS=10
 
./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --mpi-mode=openmpi --with-gcc=system --with-openmpi=system --with-cmake=system --no-check-certificate \
--with-fftw=install --with-openblas=install --with-ptscotch=install  --with-pexsi=install --with-superlu=install \
--with-plumed=install  --with-quip=install \
--enable-cuda=yes --gpu-ver=V100 -j 10


cp $CURRENT_DIR/cp2k-${VERSION}/tools/toolchain/install/arch/* $CURRENT_DIR/cp2k-${VERSION}/arch
source $CURRENT_DIR/cp2k-${VERSION}/tools/toolchain/install/setup

cd  $CURRENT_DIR/cp2k-${VERSION}

make -j 10 ARCH=local_cuda VERSION="ssmp psmp" 
```

title: CDFT (and LDA+U) k-point sampling support?
description:
CDFT and LDA+U seem not to have k-point sampling support. Is there any source that I can learn from to understand the challenge behind it? (i.e., the challenge to generalize the Gamma-point KS potential to each k-point KS potential.)

I am especially curious about the k-point implementation in CP2K, (for example, how is the real-space density matrix obtained?)
https://aip.scitation.org/doi/10.1063/5.0007045 says:
> An extension of Eq. (9) to include a k-point sampling within the first Brillouin zone is also available in CP2K.

but left without any references. Maybe there are some unpublished developers' notes?

title: Forces evaluation for mixed force calculations is printed in the same file if you use sopt or ssmp versions
description:
Dear CP2K developers,
I noticed interesting bug:
If you use cp2k.sopt or cp2k.ssmp for mixed calculations, your forces evaluation output will be printed in the same file (*-r-1.out). It doesn't effect restart or wavefunctions printing - only messes up with output.
I detected it by running example_multiple_force_eval.py from aiida-cp2k plugin.
Best regards,
Vasyl
[multiple_force_eval.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/8388405/multiple_force_eval.zip)

title: Wrong program name in `tools/toolchain/scripts/stage4/install_libxsmm.sh`
description:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/aa9c8d9962e810d991202283608a233847e566fc/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage4/install_libxsmm.sh#L92

The actual name should be `libxsmm_gemm_generator`, not `libxsmm_generator` as in the script.

title: Typo in `tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/install_elpa.sh`
description:
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/aa9c8d9962e810d991202283608a233847e566fc/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/install_elpa.sh#L106

Instead of
```bash
grep '\bavx512v1\b' /proc/cpuinfo 1> /dev/null && AVX512_flags+=" -mavx512v1"
```
it should be
```bash
grep '\bavx512vl\b' /proc/cpuinfo 1> /dev/null && AVX512_flags+=" -mavx512vl"
```
(Two letter `l`s were typed as number `1`s)

title: Add stress tensor terms with 2nd order Laplace functionals
description: Plus: Add higher order finite difference schemes

title: DBM: improved miniapp makefile
description:
* Introduced realclean target.
* Introduced NVARCH variable.
* Link MKL library if present.

title: Fix work array size
description:
The work space query for lwork does not return always sufficiently large values. This looks like a ScaLAPACK bug.
Let's add some margin as workaround to avoid crashes.

title: Offload: Move includes to fix __OFFLOAD.
description: This is a follow up to #2027.

title: Implementation of low-scaling SOS-Laplace MP2 forces
description:
This PR brings low-scaling SOS-Laplace MP2 gradients into CP2K. The new code is an extension of the energy code for low-scaling RPA and exploits the same machinery: sparse tensor contractions. The implementation covers the following areas:
- Double hybrid (with same-spin MP2 only)
- ADMM
- Meta (tau) functionals
- Stress tensor
- Spin polarized systems
- GPW and MME for the 2-center periodic integrals and derivatives
- Libint for all other integrals
- Option to keep the Laplace quadrature constant over a MD

The code was carefully tested with many debug runs. New regtests covering all the above have been added.

title: Add wb97x2 (Anna Hehn)
description: This PR adds XC sections of the wB97X-2 functional. A few parameters can be set externally as documented by the new regtests.

title: ARCH: Migrate CRAY-XC50-gnu.psmp to -D__OFFLOAD_CUDA
description: This is a follow up to #2027.

title: Do not use PW_CUDA
description: The performance tests indicate a slowdown after PW_CUDA was activated

title: Bump minimist from 1.2.5 to 1.2.6 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [minimist](https://github.com/substack/minimist) from 1.2.5 to 1.2.6.
Commits
  • 7efb22a 1.2.6
  • ef88b93 security notice for additional prototype pollution issue
  • c2b9819 isConstructorOrProto adapted from PR
  • bc8ecee test from prototype pollution PR
  • See full diff in compare view

title: RI_HFX| much better sparsity management in RHO flavor forces
description: This re-implementation of the forces in the RHO flavor of RI-HFX manages sparsity in a better way than before. In particular, the order of the many 3-center tensor contractions is changed such that densification happens at the last possible step, thus preserving sparsity for longer. This is partly made possible thanks to #1984.

title: Patching to work with MPItrampoline
description:
I'm trying to help patch CP2K so that it can work with [MPItrampoline](https://github.com/eschnett/MPItrampoline) (by @eschnett, it provides a stable MPI ABI so that you can use different implementations without recompiling the code). This requires patching of CP2K so that MPI constants are only used at runtime rather than compile time (for example).

Unfortunately,  Fortran is not my strong point and I struggled to help write the patch (a gist of the current version for CP2K 8.2 is at https://gist.github.com/ocaisa/49aa4aa6ac1bf43b36cde1716e369824).  One issue was it was unclear where exactly the initialisation of the constants should take place, we have something now that seems to work on that front but some advice on the "correct" place for this would be useful. The other problem is that some variables (like `fft_scratch_type%cart_sub_comm`) are initialised, and we seem to be failing to hack this initialisation when this type is used. Finally, the patch is causing a segfault right at the end of the application and I don't fully know how to track this down (though I suspect it is related to the second issue):
```

Support for other GPU models (A6000, 3080)
description:
Hi,

I'm attempting to set up CP2K on a couple of machines, one has a 3080 and the other has an A6000 (both are  `ARCH_NUM=86`).

I can't see direct support for these in the `--gpu-ver` flag, with the closest being the A100 (`ARCH_NUM=80`).
From what I can see, the `SM_80` binary will run on `SM_86`, but won't utilise the full FP32 throughput.

Just wondered if there's something I'm missing, or if targeting the A100 is the best way forward.

I also have several laptops with `SM_75` GPUs. For these should I use V100?

Cheers,
Zach

title: Improve support for metaGGA functionals
description:
This PR enables:
- forces and stress tensors with tau-dependent double-hybrid functionals
- forces with tau-dependent Harris functionals
- stress tensors with Laplace-dependent functionals (without double-hybrids and Harris)
- forces with Laplace-dependent double-hybrid and Harris functionals

Misc:
Refactorings in the XC, linres, MP2 code

title: Add LibXC equivalents to ADMM exchange potentials
description:
This commit adds the LibXC equivalents to the already available exchange correction functionals for ADMM. Because LibXC does not have a truncated-Coulomb-based functional (see [here](https://gitlab.com/libxc/libxc/-/issues/324)), the corresponding options still use the CP2K versions.
The main use of the new functionality is the availability of analytical 2nd derivatives (Double Hybrids, Harris functional, TDDF(P)T) with ADMM for most operators (apart from truncated Coulomb and mixed truncated Coulomb/longrange Coulomb).

Fixes #1960

description: Hi, I noticed that if user sets `ADMM_PURIFICATION_METHOD` to values except `MO_DIAG` and `NONE`, geometry optimisation will fail but the log file does not provide an error message. Inspecting the `admm_methods.F` at line 2033 shows the source of error as forces are not implemented otherwise. https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/d40d240e9e932ae2deae73189bc2060db0eb963a/src/admm_methods.F#L2026-L2034 but the `CPABORT` does not contain error message. If it helps I can open a PR adding these error messages. Best regards, Pezhman

ELPA GPU hangs or crashes
description:
Hi, a CSCS user on the Cray XC50 reported a problem with ELPA GPU linked by CP2K 9.1: in a test case that runs in few minutes on 6 nodes, the job hangs when starting the diagonalization with the default `ELPA_KERNEL AUTO` or it crashes with `CPASSERT failed` in `cp_fm_diag_elpa_base` when using `ELPA_KERNEL  NVIDIA_GPU` in the input file. 
The job works instead when setting manually `ELPA_KERNEL GENERIC`, therefore we have added a note on the [CP2K page of the CSCS User Portal](https://user.cscs.ch/computing/applications/cp2k): if you can access Piz Daint, the input file, the coordinate file and the Slurm submission script are in `/scratch/snx3000/lucamar/cp2k/aiida`.
Are you aware of other issues with ELPA GPU within CP2K? We have built CP2K 9.1 based on a [recent architecture file](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/arch/CRAY-XC50-gnu.psmp) with  `-D__ELPA -D__ELPA_NVIDIA_GPU` linking ELPA GPU enabled, but I have the impression that it is safer to continue linking the CPU version of the library only. Thanks for your advice.

title: Use SPLA for offloading dgemm on GPUs in the mp2 module
description:
dgemm in the mp2 module are offloaded to GPU using SPLA which already has this feature implemented. It is similar to what libsci_acc would do but it is now vendor agnostic and will support any blas vendor implementation.

- SPLA update to the latest.
- SPLA will offload the dgemm when the product 2 m n k is above a given threshold. The value is hardcoded but we can add an extra input option to set it up manually. I Fixed it to 2 * (128 ^ 3) but it can be increased further. 
- SPLA is not a hard dependency.

They are several direction for improvement. 
- Some of the matrices can be loaded only one time for instance on GPU, etc... 
- Dgemms can also be batched. But the prize to pay is code cluttering in absence of a generic interface.
- maybe use some of spla functionalities to handle mpi communication.

title: Error when config libvdwxc in toolchain
description:
Hello!

I tried to install cp2k 8.2.0 on my cluster, which uses CentOS 7.5, gcc 7.3.0, cmake 3.14.6, python 3.6.1, intel mpi and mkl 2018 update 3. 

I used the script to install tools:

```sh
 ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --mpi-mode=intelmpi --with-cmake=system --with-mkl=system \
--with-openblas=no --with-scalapack=no \
--with-libint=install --with-quip=install \
--with-plumed=install --with-sirius=install
```

It gave me an error when config libvdwxc:
```sh
libvdwxc-0.4.0.tar.gz is found
Installing from scratch into /opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/libvdwxc-0.4.0
ERROR: (./scripts/stage7/install_libvdwxc.sh, line 83) Non-zero exit code detected.
```

The error part in `build/libvdwxc-0.4.0/config.log` is:

```sh
configure:16998: checking whether the serial FFTW3 libraries work
configure:17017: mpicc -o conftest -O2 -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -march=native -mtune=native  -I'/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include'  conftest.c -lfftw3_mpi -lfftw3 -lfftw3_omp -lm  >&5
conftest.c: In function 'main':
conftest.c:41:26: warning: passing argument 1 of 'fftw_execute_dft' from incompatible pointer type [-Wincompatible-pointer-types]
         fftw_execute_dft(plan, a1, a2);
                          ^~~~
In file included from conftest.c:33:0:
/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include/fftw3.h:66:46: note: expected 'fftw_plan {aka struct fftw_plan_s * const}' but argument is of type 'struct fftw_plan_s **'
 #define FFTW_MANGLE_DOUBLE(name) FFTW_CONCAT(fftw_, name)
                                              ^
/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include/fftw3.h:65:35: note: in definition of macro 'FFTW_CONCAT'
 #define FFTW_CONCAT(prefix, name) prefix ## name
                                   ^~~~~~
/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include/fftw3.h:196:12: note: in expansion of macro 'FFTW_MANGLE_DOUBLE'
 FFTW_CDECL X(execute_dft)(const X(plan) p, C *in, C *out);              \
            ^
/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include/fftw3.h:457:1: note: in expansion of macro 'FFTW_DEFINE_API'
 FFTW_DEFINE_API(FFTW_MANGLE_DOUBLE, double, fftw_complex)
 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
/opt/src/cp2k/8.2/cp2k-8.2.0/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/lib/libfftw3_mpi.so: undefined reference to `fftw_ialignment_of'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
configure:17017: $? = 1
configure: failed program was:
| /* confdefs.h */
| #define PACKAGE_NAME "libvdwxc"
| #define PACKAGE_TARNAME "libvdwxc"
| #define PACKAGE_VERSION "0.4.0"
| #define PACKAGE_STRING "libvdwxc 0.4.0"
| #define PACKAGE_BUGREPORT "asklarsen@gmail.com"
| #define PACKAGE_URL ""
| #define PACKAGE "libvdwxc"
| #define VERSION "0.4.0"
| #define STDC_HEADERS 1
| #define HAVE_SYS_TYPES_H 1
| #define HAVE_SYS_STAT_H 1
| #define HAVE_STDLIB_H 1
| #define HAVE_STRING_H 1
| #define HAVE_MEMORY_H 1
| #define HAVE_STRINGS_H 1
| #define HAVE_INTTYPES_H 1
| #define HAVE_STDINT_H 1
| #define HAVE_UNISTD_H 1
| #define HAVE_ASSERT_H 1
| #define HAVE_COMPLEX_H 1
| #define HAVE_MATH_H 1

|         fftw_complex *a1, *a2;
|         fftw_execute_dft(plan, a1, a2);
|
|   ;
|   return 0;
| }
```
It seems there are two problems: One is the variable type of `plan` in `fftw_plan *plan;` may not be a pointer, and the other is the compiler can not find the function `fftw_ialignment_of`;

I did a little research and found out if  I changed `fftw_plan *plan;` to `fftw_plan paln;` in the `conftest.c`, the first problem can be solved; and if I remove `-lfftw_mpi` in the compile command, the second problem will also be solved.

title: [How it works? ]function ortho_cx_to_grid has many vectorized assembly versions
description:
Hey guys, when I looked at the gcc assembly code of `ortho_cx_to_grid_scalar`, in` src/grid/ref/grid_ref_collint.h` , I found a very interesting phenomenon, it has many different versions of vectorized loops, which seems to be good for running performance?
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17926010/153837634-fb37a466-5310-4619-ac3d-9f2ed01499a9.png)


And `icc` even has its own special assembly version code
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17926010/153837801-140709bb-a489-42fb-9b26-3cd0ded8f9c8.png)

This seems to be related to pragma unroll? Was cp2k designed with this scenario in mind? How does it work?

title: Segmentation fault with latest Intel compiler
description:
I tried to compile CP2K with the latest oneAPI container image. My Docker file is the following:

```
FROM intel/oneapi-hpckit:devel-ubuntu18.04

COPY USE_SERVER.lic /opt/intel/license/USE_SERVER.lic

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get upgrade -y \
    && apt-get install git wget cmake autoconf automake libtool -y \
    && apt-get clean

# Install libxc
RUN wget https://www.cp2k.org/static/downloads/libxc-5.2.2.tar.gz \
    && tar -zxvf libxc-5.2.2.tar.gz \
    && rm libxc-5.2.2.tar.gz \
    && cd libxc-5.2.2 \
    && ./configure CC=icc FC=ifort CFLAGS="-fPIC -O3 -march=skylake-avx512 -mtune=skylake-avx512 -mfma" --prefix=/usr/local/libxc -enable-shared \
    && make -j 64 \
    && make install \
    && cd .. \
    && rm -rf libxc-5.2.2
ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/libxc/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-c"]
RUN ln -s /opt/intel/oneapi/mkl/latest /opt/intel/mkl
COPY Linux-x86-64-oneapi.psmp Linux-x86-64-oneapi.psmp

RUN git clone --recursive https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k.git \
    && cd cp2k \
    && git checkout v9.1.0 \
    && git submodule update --recursive \
    && cd tools/toolchain/ \
    && ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --help \
    && CC=mpiicc FC=mpiifort FF=mpiifort CXX=mpiicpc LD=mpiifort CFLAGS="-O0 -fopenmp" FCFLAGS="-O0 -fallow-argument-mismatch -fopenmp" CXXFLAGS="-O0 -fopenmp" ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --with-intelmpi=system --with-mkl=/opt/intel/mkl --with-scalapack=no --with-cmake=system --with-cosma=no --with-sirius=no --with-libint=no --with-hdf5=no --with-fftw=no --with-elpa=no --with-libvori=no --with-spglib=install --with-libxc=system --with-openblas=no --with-libsmm=no --with-libxsmm=install \
    && cd ../.. \
    && cp ./tools/toolchain/install/arch/* ./arch/ \
    && mv ../Linux-x86-64-oneapi.psmp ./arch/Linux-x86-64-oneapi.psmp \
    && cat ./arch/local.psmp \
    && source ./tools/toolchain/install/setup \
    && make -j 64 ARCH=Linux-x86-64-oneapi VERSION="psmp" \
    && sed -i "s/source \"\/cp2k\/tools\/toolchain\/scripts\/tool_kit.sh\"//" ./tools/toolchain/install/setup \
    && rm -rf tools/toolchain/build/ \
    && rm -rf src/ \
    && rm -rf obj/ \
    && rm -rf tests/
    
ENV PATH=/cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi:$PATH
```

I use the following arch file (Linux-x86-64-oneapi.psmp):

```
CC        = mpiicc
FC        = mpiifort
CXX       = mpiicpc
LD        = mpiifort
AR        = ar -r

# Only one test input (CH4-rsLDA.inp) gives wrong results with inlining enabled
CFLAGS    = -O0 -fopenmp -I/opt/intel/mkl/include -I/opt/intel/mkl/include/fftw -I'/usr/local/libxc/include' -I'/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.17/include' -I/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/spglib-1.16.2/include

DFLAGS    = -D__FFTW3
DFLAGS   += -D__MAX_CONTR=4
DFLAGS   += -D__MKL
DFLAGS   += -D__MPI_VERSION=3
DFLAGS   += -D__parallel
DFLAGS   += -D__SCALAPACK
DFLAGS   += -D__LIBXSMM
DFLAGS   += -D__LIBXC
DFLAGS   += -D__SPGLIB

FCFLAGS   = $(CFLAGS) $(DFLAGS)
FCFLAGS  += -diag-disable=8291
FCFLAGS  += -diag-disable=8293
FCFLAGS  += -fpp
FCFLAGS  += -free

LDFLAGS   = $(FCFLAGS) -static-intel -static_mpi -L'/usr/local/libxc/lib' -Wl,-rpath='/usr/local/libxc/lib' -L'/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.17/lib' -Wl,-rpath='/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.17/lib' -L'/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/spglib-1.16.2/lib' -Wl,-rpath='/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/spglib-1.16.2/lib'
LDFLAGS_C = -nofor-main

MKL_LIB   = $(MKLROOT)/lib/intel64

LIBS      = -lsymspg
LIBS     += $(MKL_LIB)/libmkl_scalapack_lp64.so
LIBS     += -Wl,--start-group
LIBS     += $(MKL_LIB)/libmkl_intel_lp64.so
LIBS     += $(MKL_LIB)/libmkl_sequential.so
LIBS     += $(MKL_LIB)/libmkl_core.so
LIBS     += $(MKL_LIB)/libmkl_blacs_intelmpi_lp64.so
LIBS     += -Wl,--end-group
LIBS     += -lxsmmf -lxsmm -lxcf03 -lxc -lstdc++ 

# Required due to memory leak that occurs if high optimisations are used
mp2_optimize_ri_basis.o: mp2_optimize_ri_basis.F
	$(FC) -c $(subst O2,O0,$(FCFLAGS)) $<
```

I used to have higher optimization settings, which I removed for debug purposes. The error is the following:

```
forrtl: severe (174): SIGSEGV, segmentation fault occurred
Image              PC                Routine            Line        Source             
cp2k.psmp          0000000005DF657A  for__signal_handl     Unknown  Unknown
libpthread-2.27.s  00007F8156C01980  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown
libshm-fi.so       00007F81516EBC2C  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown
Unknown            00007FFE35A414DC  Unknown               Unknown  Unknown
```

I get the following valgrind traceback:

```
1 errors in context 1 of 1:
==118== Conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value(s)
==118==    at 0x5ED3511: __intel_sse2_strcpy (in /cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi/cp2k.psmp)
==118==    by 0x5E0613E: for__add_to_lf_table (in /cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi/cp2k.psmp)
==118==    by 0x5E6084B: for__open_proc (in /cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi/cp2k.psmp)
==118==    by 0x5E0D2D4: for__open_default (in /cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi/cp2k.psmp)
==118==    by 0x5E353F6: for_write_seq_fmt (in /cp2k/exe/Linux-x86-64-oneapi/cp2k.psmp)

==118==

Segmentation fault (core dumped)

Does anyone have an idea how I could fix this? Is this possibly a compiler bug? I didn't have this issue with older versions of the Intel compilers. Thanks!

title: RI_HFX| Simplification/optimization of trace for force evaluations
description:
This is a simplification of a part of the RI-HFX force code, which also happens to bring performance benefits.

Some contributions to the forces stem from a sum over the 3-indices of 3D tensors: `F = sum_ijk A_ijk * B_ijk`

Before this PR, these contraction were handled with an intermediate 2-center tensor `T_kl = sum_ij A_ijk * B_ijl`, before the sum of the diagonal would be taken: `F = sum_kk T_kk`.

This resulted in unnecessary and expansive 3-center tensor contractions, which can be avoided by directly looping over the blocks of the two initial tensors.

title: Fix for fft performance regression
description:
This PR contains hopefully some improvements that fix the performance regressions observed after my last PR.
Changes are numerous but not difficult

- fft plans are reused when possible. The default number of saved planes is fixed to 8.
    device allocations / opposite are only made when necessary.
- use _OFFLOAD{CUDA,HIP} instead of _PW{CUDA,HIP}. We still need -D__PW{CUDA,HIP} for the offload module until the build system is cleaned up.
- some minor cleanups as well

title: Fft improvements
description:
This PR contains hopefully some improvements that fix the performance regressions observed after my last PR. 
Changes are numerous but not difficult
- fft plans are reused when possible. The default number of saved planes is fixed to 8.
- device allocations / opposite are only made when necessary.

title: Protect against when GPU compilation is requested, but no PW
IR Intensities from Vibrational Analysis module
description:
This is a follow up to the recent question from the Google group: "what is the unit for IR intensity using normal mode analysis in cp2k?"

IR intensities are defined as proportional to (dmu/dQ)^2. However, CP2K prints out an intensity value in the vibrational analysis module that is |dmu/dQ| in atomic units. The same value is also printed as "intensity" in the MOLDEN vib file. I think this is at best
misleading and probably leads to errors in the post processing analysis.
I propose to change the "Intensity" output by (dmu/dQ)^2 in some often used units, e.g. km/mole as in G16.

If there are analysis programs that rely on CP2K intensity to be |dmu/dQ| we could find another solution.

title: GPU options Cleanup
description:
- use `-D__FUNCTIONALITY_GPU` flags instead of `-D__FUNCTIONALITY_CUDA` or `-D__FUNCTIONALITY_HIP`
- specialization to dedicated hardware can be done with `-D__OFFLOAD_CUDA` or `-D__OFFLOAD_HIP `(or others). It is mandatory to specify which platform is needed. if not and one of the `-D__FUNCTIONALITY_GPU` is specified then compilation error should occur.
- dbm supports hip. Right now it is just cut and paste of cuda backend. Code **sharing** is exploited as much as possible using the offload api.
- Updated the INSTALL.md to reflect these changes.
- Updated the arch file for daint as well
- removed some compilation options that date back from cuda 7.0 (or so). 
- modified the toolchain accordingly

title: Integration of a single Gaussian with GPW
description:
For the RI and LRI method, there is the regular need to collocate and integrate a single Gaussian. Potentially, the implementation of this feature accelerates these kind of operations.

It is about the following files:
qs_integrate_potential_single.F (all routines)
mp2_eri_gpw.F (most routines)
qs_collocate_density.F (most routines)

Right now, there are implemented as an collocation/integration operation of a single Gaussian with one (exponent set to 1) or of a single Gaussian with itself (exponent is half of the actual one).

title: Problem with LIBXSMM back compatibility

```

/data/aubuss/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.16.2/include/libxsmm_macros.h:98:3: error: missing binary operator before token "("
   (((0x7F & (MAJOR)) << 24) | ((0x1F & (MINOR)) << 19) | ((0x1F & (UPDATE)) << 14) | (0x3FFF & (PATCH)))
   ^
/data/aubuss/cp2k/src/grid/cpu/utils.c:45:5: note: in expansion of macro ‘LIBXSMM_VERSION4’

title: CP2K fails with large cells and high number of OMP threads
description:
A question in the google group (https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/dfuWp6UaIOY) sparked an investigation into the behaviour of the wavelet Poisson solver. Running a calculation with a 50A x 50A x 50A cell and 36 OMP threads lead to non-physical energies and warnings by the wavelet solver. It turns out that **all** Poisson solvers yield wrong result in such conditions.

I tested the input below with the `ssmp` and `psmp`  (1 MPI rank) executables, with 16 and 32 OMP threads, for all Poisson solvers (making sure to change the periodicity when needed). Runs with 16 threads were all successful, and runs with 32 threads all failed. Reducing the box size to 40A x 40A x 40A and using 32 threads works as well. 

In the end it seems that the problem is independent the Poisson solver, contrary to the initial assumptions. I currently have no idea of what goes wrong.

```
&FORCE_EVAL                                                                                          
  METHOD Quickstep                                                                                   
  &DFT                                                                                               
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT                                                                 
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL                                                                    
                                                                                                     
    &POISSON                                                                                         
      PERIODIC NONE !XYZ                                                                             
      PSOLVER WAVELET !ANALYTIC IMPLICIT MT MULTIPLOE PERIODIC                                       
    &END                                                                                             
                                                                                                     
    &XC                                                                                              
      &XC_FUNCTIONAL PBE                                                                             
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL                                                                             
    &END XC                                                                                          
    &END DFT                                                                                         
  &SUBSYS                                                                                            
    &CELL                                                                                            
      ABC 50.0 50.0 50.0                                                                             
      PERIODIC NONE !XYZ                                                                             
    &END CELL                                                                                        
    &COORD                                                                                           
      O          0.00000        0.00000        0.11779                                               
      H          0.00000        0.75545       -0.47116                                               
      H          0.00000       -0.75545       -0.47116                                               
    &END                                                                                             
    &TOPOLOGY                                                                                        
      &CENTER_COORDINATES                                                                            
      &END CENTER_COORDINATES                                                                        
    &END TOPOLOGY                                                                                    
    &KIND H                                                                                          
      BASIS_SET SZV-MOLOPT-SR-GTH                                                                    
      POTENTIAL GTH-PBE                                                                              
    &END KIND                                                                                        
    &KIND O                                                                                          
      BASIS_SET SZV-MOLOPT-SR-GTH                                                                    
      POTENTIAL GTH-PBE                                                                              
    &END KIND                                                                                        
  &END SUBSYS                                                                                        
&END FORCE_EVAL                                                                                      
&GLOBAL                                                                                              
  PROJECT test                                                                                       
  PRINT_LEVEL MEDIUM                                                                                 
  RUN_TYPE ENERGY                                                                                    
&END GLOBAL
```

COSMA with OpenBLAS is 10x slower than ScalaPACK
description:
There seems to be a performance issue with COSMA (pdgemm) when built with OpenBLAS. Running the input file below with COSMA versus ScalaPACK exhibits a slowdown of ~10x. This is when installing CP2K with the `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` script and default parameters. 

The issue is most certainly related to OpenBLAS, because building COSMA with MKL (locally) or LIBSCI (on daint and eiger) leads to similar performance between COSMA and ScalaPACK.

The tests were run using the `psmp` executable and the latest CP2K git commit. The slowdown happens irrespective of the number OMP threads and MPI ranks.

Input file:

```
&GLOBAL
  PROGRAM_NAME TEST
  RUN_TYPE NONE
  &FM
    TYPE_OF_MATRIX_MULTIPLICATION COSMA !SCALAPACK
  &END
&END GLOBAL
&TEST
  &CP_FM_GEMM
    K 8512
    M 8512
    N 8512
    TRANSA FALSE
    TRANSB FALSE
    N_LOOP 1
  &END
&END TEST
```

add support for hip fft
description:
It is an attempt to make fft calls on GPU more generic than we had before. It is now possible to run ffts on both NVIDIA and AMD gpus more transparently, the specialization occurring at the lowest level. 

- Adding for instance intel support for their next gen HPC gpus reduces to one header file and writing the gather and scatter kernels.
- it would alsso be possible to use the same header file for the Nvidia and AMD kernels since they are actually the same. For now they are separated.
- NB : Hip fft supports both rocm_fft and cufft libraries.

title: Fix performance of COSMA with OpenMP
description:
The toolchain already enables [threading for OpenBLAS](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/cdcb2793be4c6302478245ba22b1e8c038bd7787/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage2/install_openblas.sh#L97), which [should](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/1826#issuecomment-999544877) do the trick.

However, for some reason our [performance test](https://dashboard.cp2k.org/archive/perf-openmp/index.html) still show that COSMA does not take advantage of threads.

Furthermore, the performance fluctuates by 2x:

![GW_PBE_4benzene_timings_32omp](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/241771/148387230-a8df3787-f168-4653-a0e1-1848cf1d182f.png)

title: Re-enable OpenMP for ELPA on Cray
description:
The toolchain currently builds [ELPA without OpenMP on Cray](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/cdcb2793be4c6302478245ba22b1e8c038bd7787/tools/toolchain/scripts/stage5/install_elpa.sh#L84). While this is ok for the current XC40/50 generation, we'll eventually need OpenMP support for EPYC and ARM based machines.

See also #1857.

title: Missing data-sharing clause in OMP PARALLEL region
description:
Hi,

in the file `src/qs_tensors.F` there are two `OMP PARALLEL` regions (lines 1354 and 1982). With the `DEFAULT(NONE)` clause it is necessary to specify the data shared attribute for every variable, which is accesses within the region. The variable `i` is omitted although it is referenced in both regions. once in line 1462 and once in line 2091. `i` is used in this context as an implicit loop variable, thus should be declared private, if I'm not mistaken.

title: OMP standard violation in grid_collocate_dgemm.c
description:
Hi,

the source file `src/grid/cpu/grid_collocate_dgemm.c` contains several OMP pragmas of the form `#pragma omp simd linear([list]) simdlen(X)`. In some instances the list in the `linear` clause contains pointers which are marked `const`. The first occurence is in line 221.
According to the OMP standard v5.0 section 2.19.4.6 "the *linear* clause  declares one or more list items to be private [...]". However Section 2.19.3 states in the restrictions for privatized variables "A variable that is privatized must not have a const-qualified type [...]".
Therefore, the code in `grid_collocate_dgemm.c` clearly violates the OMP standard.
This does not seem to matter for the gcc compiler, which simply accepts it, but more strict and standard compliant compilers abort with an error message at this point. Since these pointers seem to be local to the loops anyways, I don't see a reason why they need to be marked `const`.

There are memory leaks in Intel MPI 2019.x and 2021.3 which cause OOM errors in long MD simulations with CP2K. It was fixed in Intel MPI 2021.4, see this ticket on Intel forums:

https://community.intel.com/t5/Intel-oneAPI-HPC-Toolkit/Memory-leak-in-D-amp-C-eigensolver-parallel/m-p/1337226#M32303

You may want to include this information on [Compiler support](https://www.cp2k.org/dev:compiler_support) page.

title: RI-HFX| Revert some previous non-scalable changes + small fixes
New version of sirius
description: It should fix the dashboard issue reported on 12/15/2021

title: toolchain: verify checksum on already downloaded packages
description:
The toolchain should (like most package managers do) verify the checksum of the tarballs not only after download but before unpacking.
Commit 7b3f17ae0526bb7351f43a2626058fa726785bec updated the Sirius tarball hash (because upstream decided to repackage with changes). With the current logic a user who has the tarball of SIRIUS v7.2.8 already will never get the new one automatically, nor get an error message that it changed.

title: Fix mpi_grid_dims settings and valgrind warning
Keyword to enable explicit switching off of sTDA exchange
description: keyword to enable switching off sTDA exchange contribution

title: Toolchain: Update checksum of SIRIUS 7.2.8 tarball
Local resolution of the identity for excited states
description: Local resolution of identity based on LRIGPW from D.Golze for Coulomb kernel in TDDFT

title: Fix typo
description: Just a typo, because the actual argument value does not matter.

title: Fix toolchain for cuda+gcc11 and fix build without libint
RI-HFX| Finer tensor batching + uniform treatment of MEMORY_CUT
description: Some additional optimizations

title: NEWTONX interface - writing CP2K output
description: This is a small change to write output for the NEWTONX interface.

title: Update Cray arch file and rename it
SIRIUS: MPI_GRID_DIMS ignored
description: Currently the `MPI_GRID_DIMS` in `CONTROL` gets ignored, while it might be necessary to have this for cases with large number of planewaves. Relevant parties are already informed.

title: Symmetrize W_mp2
description: This PR fixes numerical issues with the energy-weighted density matrix leading to numerically unstable forces.

title: SIRIUS: elpa2 solver gets stuck
description:
Detailed configuration is attached as [Br-elpa-stuck.zip](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/7682787/Br-elpa-stuck.zip), but essentially when setting:

```
         GEN_EVP_SOLVER_NAME elpa2
         STD_EVP_SOLVER_NAME elpa2
         MPI_GRID_DIMS 2 2
```

the calculation gets reproducibly stuck at iteration 4, with the main processes spinning at 100%.

This is with CP2K 9f7a817, SIRIUS v7.2.8 and ELPA 2021.05.002_bugfix.



Also notable is that `MPI_GRID_DIMS` gets ignored completely.

title: bugfix/sirius fix atomic mass
description:
- libint: mark p_work_tmp as UNUSED if building without libint
- sirius_interface: fix mass, set symbol

title: Grid code does not work with large l quantum numbers, especially with stress tensor
description:
The grid code breaks on the GPU for larger l quantum numbers (especially with RI basis sets). In general, the grid code complains about not enough shared memory and crushes on purpose. Additional files on request. I work on Piz Daint, P100.
With stress tensor calculations, this issue occurs already with smaller angular quantum numbers.

title: New version of SIRIUS
description:
It may seems not a lot, but we redesigned the interface to better handle error and runtime options. Now each function can return and error code (I did not use it in cp2k since the error code is optional). Hopefully this will fix some of the issues that several people reported to us (thanks).
- Also fixed few warnings that popped up here and there
- next thing will be Hubbard U + V

title: NNP | feature improvement
description:
This PR adds
- Sigma scaling of the symmetry functions as available in n2p2
- Support of the analytical stress tensor
- Adjustments of control over the biasing of the committee disagreement

title: Setting any MAGNETIZATION breaks diagonalization when running PW_DFT calculations
description:
```
&FORCE_EVAL
   &DFT
      POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME POTENTIAL
      &XC
         &XC_FUNCTIONAL
            &GGA_C_PBE
            &END GGA_C_PBE
            &GGA_X_PBE
            &END GGA_X_PBE
         &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
      &END XC
   &END DFT
   METHOD SIRIUS
   &PW_DFT
      &CONTROL
         VERBOSITY 2
      &END CONTROL
      &ITERATIVE_SOLVER
         NUM_STEPS 20
      &END ITERATIVE_SOLVER
      &MIXER
         TYPE ANDERSON
      &END MIXER
      &PARAMETERS
         ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD pseudopotential
         GK_CUTOFF 6
         NGRIDK 2 2 2
         NUM_DFT_ITER 50
         NUM_MAG_DIMS 2
         PW_CUTOFF 12
         SMEARING_WIDTH 0.02
         USE_SYMMETRY .TRUE.
      &END PARAMETERS
   &END PW_DFT
   &SUBSYS
      &CELL
         A 4.3554087206434 0.0             0.0
         B 2.6701589597363e-16 4.3607005111918 0.0
         C 1.8421026947424 1.7261622759977e-16 4.2816912845655
         PERIODIC XYZ
      &END CELL
      &KIND O1
         ELEMENT O
         MAGNETIZATION 1.5
         POTENTIAL GTH GTH-PBE-q6
      &END KIND
      &KIND O2
         ELEMENT O
         MAGNETIZATION -1.5
         POTENTIAL GTH GTH-PBE-q6
      &END KIND
      &TOPOLOGY
         COORD_FILE_FORMAT XYZ
         COORD_FILE_NAME aiida.coords.xyz
      &END TOPOLOGY
   &END SUBSYS
&END FORCE_EVAL
&GLOBAL
   PROJECT aiida
   RUN_TYPE ENERGY
&END GLOBAL
```

When setting the `MAGNETIZATION` keyword I get:
```
=== Warning at line 366 of file /tmp/software/spack-stage/spack-stage-sirius-7.2.7-dwkfkgaagclxhuvxo5h3etr7eiygyd55/spack-src/src/linalg/eigenproblem.hpp ===
not all eigen-values are found
target number of eigen-values: 22
number of eigen-values found: 0
```

For `NUM_MAG_DIMS 2 ` [as documented](https://www.cp2k.org/howto:running_qe_computation) on the other hand:

```
Backtrace for this error:
  what():
=== Fatal error at line 215 of file /tmp/software/spack-stage/spack-stage-sirius-7.2.7-dwkfkgaagclxhuvxo5h3etr7eiygyd55/spack-src/src/hamiltonian/non_local_operator.cpp ===
wrong number of magnetic dimensions
```

with SIRIUS 7.2.7 and CP2K b48b44a.

title: remove unused variables as recognized by coding conventions on dashboard
Optionally change from accurate sum to standard sum in pw_integral_ab
description:
Hello,
This an update on the previous PR #1721 

The PR has been amended so that the standard intrinsic sum in pw_integral_ab is called optionally, with the accurate sum being the default. The standard sum is used when pw_integral_ab is called through pw_restrict or pw_prolongate (via find_coeffs). This improves the performance for qmmm calculations and the regression tests now all pass with this change. OpenMP has also been added for the standard sum.

I did not yet add the option to use the standard sum dynamically based on the number of entries but this could be added now or later on after some discussion.

Thanks,
Holly

title: EC| Add print section for EC properties, and Voronoi dipoles
improvements of periodic GW
description:
- regularized RI for periodic GW, enables use of large RI basis sets together with the minimum image convention (could be also interesting for periodic RI-Hartree-Fock)
- diagonalization + inversion instead of Cholesky decomposition + inversion
- still to do: k-points for self-energy for computing band structures

title: Segfault when calculating Hirshfeld charges in collocate_pgf_product with high cutoff

Running with: `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 mpirun -np 96 cp2k.psmp ...`.
