title: Switch to new GNU arch file
Catastrophic errors in new build on ORNL Summit, last commit built 4 months ago still builds and runs fine
description:
Hi all- I am rebuilding CP2K on Summit after our OS upgrade. In August, I had a well running build using git:678222b. Now during the build, I error out with `Error: Unused variable 'p_work_tmp' declared at (1) [-Werror=unused-variable]` but can build if I remove that flag from the arch files. However, even with a totally serial, no GPU test I get rapid NaNs and infinity for all regtests I have tried. When I check out that previous commit, everything is fine and the tests pass.

Details:
GCC: I  tried 9, 10, and 7. I used 7 for the one that worked with `git:678222b`
CUDA: 11.0.3
FFTW: I used both system and local build through the toolchain. The final build that works used system
Openblas: Tried 0.3.5 (this one is built with no threading) from system, local build with toolchain, final working build used openblas/0.3.17-omp (with threads) which is the default system version
I used no other libraries, built with no packages except DBCSR
Scalapack and lapack are the netlib refs

It seems to me that it's not an issue with the libraries on Summit, based on the fact that `678222b` is fine.

arch file is this (with the `-Werror=unused-variable` flag removed from `WFLAGS` for the current version)
```
CC          = mpicc
CXX         = mpicxx
AR          = ar -r
FC          = mpif90
LD          = mpif90
#
DFLAGS      = -D__parallel -D__DBCSR_ACC -D__PW_CUDA  -D__FFTW3  -D__SCALAPACK -D__ACC 
#
WFLAGS      = -Werror=aliasing -Werror=ampersand -Werror=c-binding-type -Werror=intrinsic-shadow -Werror=intrinsics-std -Werror=line-truncation -Werror=tabs -Werror=target-lifetime -Werror=underflow -Werror=unused-but-set-variable -Werror=unused-variable -Werror=unused-dummy-argument -Werror=conversion -Werror=zerotrip -Wno-maybe-uninitialized -Wuninitialized -Wuse-without-only 
#
FCDEBFLAGS  = -fbacktrace -ffree-form -fimplicit-none -std=f2008 
CFLAGS      = -mtune=native -fno-omit-frame-pointer -O3 -funroll-loops $(PROFOPT) -fopenmp  -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -Werror $(DFLAGS)
FCFLAGS     =  -O2 -funroll-loops  -fopenmp  $(FCDEBFLAGS) $(WFLAGS) $(DFLAGS)
#
LDFLAGS     =  $(FCFLAGS) -Wl,--enable-new-dtags -L${OLCF_FFTW_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_NETLIB_LAPACK_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_NETLIB_SCALAPACK_ROOT}/lib -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/lib64 -L${OLCF_OPENBLAS_ROOT}/lib -L'/usr/lib64' -Wl,-rpath='/usr/lib64'
LIBS     = -L${OLCF_FFTW_ROOT}/lib -lfftw3 -lfftw3_threads \
           -L${OLCF_NETLIB_LAPACK_ROOT}/lib  -llapack \
           -L${OLCF_NETLIB_SCALAPACK_ROOT}/lib -lscalapack \
           -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/lib64 -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/include -L${OLCF_CUDA_ROOT}/bin -lcudart -lcuda -lstdc++ -lnvrtc \
           -L${OLCF_OPENBLAS_ROOT}/lib -lblas -lfftw3_mpi -lfftw3 -lfftw3_omp  -lopenblas -lstdc++  -lcufft -lcublas -lrt 
#
GPUVER        = V100
OFFLOAD_CC    = nvcc
OFFLOAD_FLAGS = -g -arch sm_70 -O3 -Xcompiler='-fopenmp' --std=c++11 $(DFLAGS) 
#
FYPPFLAGS   = -n --line-marker-format=gfortran5
```

description:
Latest updates and features concerning RI-HFX:
- Some contractions involve dense tensors (especially for the forces). Those are now done with much larger block sizes, enabling better GPU acceleration
- Hybrid TDDFPT forces are now possible with RI-HFX
- Automatic RI_FLAVOR switching (from RHO to MO and vice-versa) is now possible. It is called when post-SCF calculations do not have MOs available, but the SCF was converged with the MO flavor (e.g. MP2, RPA, TDDFPT)

title: SIRIUS: wrong type of electronic structure method: FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
description:
In version 8.2 the following worked:

```
    &PARAMETER
      ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
    ...
```

with SIRIUS 7.2.7 and CP2K 4ef9989 I get now:

```
SIRIUS: run-time error

=== Fatal error at line 236 of file /tmp/software/spack-stage/spack-stage-sirius-7.2.7-2onvcagphket6ecprltlo67h3ywpxue7/spack-src/src/context/simulation_parameters.cpp ===
wrong type of electronic structure method: FULL_POTENTIAL_LAPWLO
```

and have to correct to

```
    &PARAMETER
      ELECTRONIC_STRUCTURE_METHOD full_potential_lapwlo
    ...
```

... and the manual doesn't list allowed values anymore.

The second issue might already be fixed with one of the commits of @mtaillefumier in SIRIUS already.
Would be good to have a new SIRIUS release before the next CP2K release.

title: RI-HFX| Fixed a bug leading to wrongly conditioned RI basis sets
description: A bug in the RI-HFX code lead to wrongly conditioned RI basis sets. In particular, the routine `init_orb_basis_set` was called twice on the RI_HFX basis (once during the initialization of `qs_env`, once during the initialization of `ri_data`). This leads to wrong conversion matrices from primitive to spherical Gaussian functions. As a result, all RI-HFX calculations thus far have been run with basis sets slightly less accurate (and different) than the basis specified in the input file. This PR fixes this issue  and updates the regtests. The RI-HFX total energies are now typically an order of magnitude (one extra decimal point) closer to reference standard HFX calculations, as tested on the regtests and a handful of other systems.

title: Analytical derivatives of the MO coefficients wrt nuclear coordinates and the evaluation of Atomic Polar Tensors
description:
Dear CP2K developers,

We used the code in this pull request for the calculations in this publication: [doi.org/10.1063/5.0041056](doi.org/10.1063/5.0041056).
We added another response calculation called `dcdr_linres` to `qs_linres_module`. The purpose is the analytical evaluation of the derivatives of the MO coefficients with respect to nuclear coordinates. The routine `dcdr_response_dR` calculates `$dCR = dC^0/dR^lambda_beta$`, where `lambda` enumerates the atom indices and `beta` the cartesian coordinates. The coefficient derivatives are then used to calculate the Atomic Polar Tensors (APTs) `$P^lambda_alpha,beta = d<mu_alpha>/dR^lambda_beta$`, where `mu_alpha` are the components of the electric dipole operator. The routine `apt_dR_localization` calculates the APTs using the localized dipole operator and localized orbitals.

This pull request touches quite a few files in the core of CP2K to evaluate integral derivatives with respect to nuclear coordinates - instead of electronic coordinates. I hope that I didn't incur significant performance issues in the standard ground-state calculations.

This input: https://gist.github.com/edditler/97d8a3d2b8f23eb2bb0bcbee3bacfcfc results in this output: https://gist.github.com/edditler/0b517b67bd01346f5c6ad7674c5dc21c on my machine, using the sdbg, ssmp, and psmp binaries.

I ran the regression tests using the arch files generated by the toolchain with the command `tools/regtesting/do_regtest -arch local -version psmp -maxtasks 10 -mpiranks 2 -ompthreads 2`

One can also use the implementation to evaluate the analytical Hessian matrix and improve geometry optimizations, but we didn't consider the needed interface.

Best regards,

Edward

title: Docker: Switch Gromacs to use pkgconfig
RI-HFX forces
description:
Implementation of RI-HFX forces based on sparse DBCSR tensor contractions. The new code is an extension of the work by @pseewald. New features cover:
- RI-HFX forces in the RHO and MO flavors
- Possibility of using a shorter range RI metric (for both flavors)
- Stress tensor

Additionally, the option of using RI-HFX was generalized throughout the code. It is now available for (although only in the RHO flavor):
- RPA
- MP2 (including gradients)
- TDDFPT (not including forces)
- LINRES

Some of the SCF RI-HFX routines where refactored for performance. In particular, `alloc_block_3c` now avoids allocating tons of arrays and `build_3c_integrals` was OMP parallelized.

Relevant regtests were added.

title: Enable different BD values for BMHFTD potential
No DBCSR submodule available
description:
Hi all

I tried to install cp2k 8.2 with openmpi 4.1.1, gcc 10.3 and cuda 11.4.

I had finished toolchain installation. I also cloned dbcsr to `exts/dbcsr`  and used `git submodule update --init --recursive` in the directory to check again that every package is ready.

However, when I start make cp2k, it still gave me the error:

```
Discovering programs .../opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/exts/Makefile.inc:2: *** "No DBCSR submodule available, please run 'git submodule update --init --recursive'".  Stop.
make[1]: *** [ssmp] Error 2
make: *** [all] Error 2
```

I checked the file `exts/Makefile.inc:2`, and found it checked whether `$(EXTSHOME)/dbcsr/.cp2k/Makefile` existed. The dbscr cloned from github does not have the .cp2k directory and that's why I got the error.

I tried to fix this problem: 
First, I created the .cp2k directory in `$(EXTSHOME)/dbcsr` and used cmake to generated the Makefile, and everything was fine.

Then I tried to make cp2k again. 
It gave another error:
```
Discovering programs ...
Removing stale archives for ssmp ... 
Resolving dependencies for ssmp ... 
make -C /opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/exts/dbcsr -f .cp2k/Makefile \
   INCLUDEMAKE=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/arch/local.ssmp \
   LIBDIR=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/lib/local/ssmp/exts/dbcsr \
   OBJDIR=/opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/obj/local/ssmp/exts/dbcsr \
   ACC="" \
   ACCFLAGS=""
-- Found OpenMP_C: -fopenmp  
-- Found OpenMP_CXX: -fopenmp  
-- Found OpenMP_Fortran: -fopenmp  
-- Found OpenMP: TRUE   
-- Using BLAS for Small Matrix Multiplication
-- A library with LAPACK API found.
-- GPU target architecture: V100
-- GPU architecture number: 70
-- GPU profiling enabled: OFF
-- FYPP preprocessor found.
Tests will run with 20 MPI ranks and 2 OpenMP threads each
-- Configuring done
-- Generating done
-- Build files have been written to: /opt/src/cp2k/cp2k_yshen/cp2k-8.2.0/exts/dbcsr/.cp2k
make[4]: CMakeFiles/Makefile2: No such file or directory
make[4]: *** No rule to make target `CMakeFiles/Makefile2'.  Stop.
make[3]: *** [all] Error 2
make[2]: *** [dbcsr] Error 2
make[1]: *** [ssmp] Error 2
make: *** [all] Error 2
```

I checked that the missing `CMakeFiles/Makefile2` is in `exts/dbcsr/.cp2k/CMakeFiles`, I don't know why the program can't find it.

I'm confused and need some suggestions. Thanks.

title: Docker: Exclude SIRIUS from farming test
description: While SIRIUS and [farming](https://dashboard.cp2k.org/archive/farming/index.html) never really [got along well](https://github.com/electronic-structure/SIRIUS/issues/520), since #1680 it causes a dead lock.

title: Fix flush for non-i/o ranks
description: I forgot to call m_flush with a valid unit_nr only.

title: Add time estimates for RI-MP2 calculation
Fix initialization of string variables
description:
- string variable are C initialized which make FORTRAN unhappy
- explicitly set pseudo_potential or full_potential

title: Add pkg-config and test for Gromacs QM/MM
TDDFT Forces (beta version)
description:
This is the current version of the TDDFT forces/properties code. Debug output is still activated.
Works for most combinations of GGA/Hybrid(ADMM) ground state and GGA/Hybrid(ADMM)/sTDA kernel
combinations. xTB/sTDA forces are also possible. Also provides dipole moments (isolated systems).

title: Remove one call to 2nd order kernel in MP2
description: Some simplifications (unused para_env_sub_RPA, removal of one call to 2nd order kernel, CPABORT, scaling factor of cphf_like_update)

title: Feature request: Conjugate gradient diagonalization
description:
Hello devs,

Would it be possible to add conjugate gradient as method for solving diagonalization-based SCF? CP2K farms this step to programs like lapack, so it doesn't seem like it would be hard, but I'm not sure.

Thanks.
-Nick

title: Fixes for the Intel compiler related to #1664
description:
- Using the `INTENT(OUT)` attribute for `POINTER` arguments is delicate as the status of the pointer might become undefined after the routine is left. At least this happens with the Intel compiler. Therefore all `INTENT(OUT)` attributes for `POINTER` arguments have been replaced by `INTENT(INOUT)` attributes.
- Added routine name to `END SUBROUTINE` where missing
- Replaced `ENDIF` by `END IF` and `ENDDO` by `END DO`

title: Add a key to print the atom name instead of the element symbol
Adding density matrix based conjugate-gradient linear response solver.
description:
* Controlled by RESPONSE_SOLVER environment in Harris functional

 * Preconditioner (MULTI_LEVEL) for AO_ORTHO solver (cheaper CG solver without XC-kernel calculation)

Adding new Harris functional solvers:

 * Linear scaling purification methods (SIGN_MATRIX, TC2, TRS4)

 * OT-Diagonalization (Intitial guess by Atomic block or ground-state density guess)

Stress tensor for Harris functional, response solver, and subsystem DFT (embedding and atomic potential) for LDA and GGA functionals.

title: Adding densitry matrix based conjugate-gradient linear response solver.
description:
* Controlled by RESPONSE_SOLVER environment in Harris functional

 * Preconditioner (MULTI_LEVEL) for AO_ORTHO solver (cheaper CG solver without XC-kernel calculation)

Adding new Harris functional solvers:

 * Linear scaling purificatio methods (SIGN_MATRIX, TC2, TRS4)

 * OT Diagonalization (Intitial guess by Atomic block or ground-state density guess)

Stress tensor for Harris functional, response solver, and subsystem DFT (embedding and atomic potential). Works for LDA and GGA for all these methods

title: ocl: support OpenCL devices in DBCSR
description:
* Slightly revised CP2K's installation manual and added section about OpenCL Devices.
* Support OFFLOAD_TARGET=opencl and added ARCH-file for GNU Fortran and OpenCL.
* Map GPUVER to a file with tuned parameters (to be embedded into the build).

title: Add compiler flag only if available
Fix #1653
description: COLLAPSE(3) clause removed, because it might be risky given the inner loop structure.

title: Intel regression tests are failing since #1647
description: All Intel regression tester list roughly 60 failing tests since #1647. Some errors, the segfaults, can be resolved by increasing the OMP_STACKSIZE but most of the wrong results remain. Many tests abort in [qs_linres_methods](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/05cc75664f36af52834c7b1fc1586686d8f9b89e/src/qs_linres_methods.F#L487) which is most likely a follow-up error.

title: Fix thresholds
description: This PR increases the thresholds of some tests to let more Dashboard tests become green.

title: MP2-based Double-Hybrid Gradients and Stress Tensors
description:
This PR enables Gradients and Stress Tensor for MP2-based Double-Hybrids. The SCF-part may contain
-HF (as before)
-DFT (up to GGAs with numerical or analytical 2nd derivatives)
-ADMM (some exchange corrections may require numerical 2nd derivatives)
-dispersion corrections
From the MP2-side, it contains
-additional interaction operators w/ stress tensor
-mixed metric /potential (w/o SVD)

Misc:
-Refactoring of modules and regtests directories of MP2/RPA
-Removal of old dead code
-Refactoring of Linres modules (kernel routines)

title: Feature request: support for the .magres file format
description:
Hello! 

Would it be possible to add support for outputting NMR-related quantities from cp2k into the standardised .magres file format as specified here:

https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/output/magres/magres-format.pdf 

This would enable users to take advantage of the magres-capable post-processing tools such as [MagresView](https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/magresview/magresview/magres_view.html) as well as being able to contribute to the [NMR database](https://www.ccpnc.ac.uk/database/). 

I hope that this would be a relatively straightforward feature to add.

Many thanks,
Kane

title: Specify that versioned tarballs should be used instead of source code.
description: This is mentioned in the [download page](https://www.cp2k.org/download) but not in `INSTALL.md`. Might be better to mention here also? This is the second time I faced #1302.

title: "SCF_GUESS restart" does not work with ELPA
description:
I updated cp2k to ca5cdc8c5 at two different workstations and found that
mpirun -n N cp2k ... with N>3
can not read saved wavefunctions in some cases. The code simply stops, all cores are busy and nothing happens.
If N=1,2,3 it works.
*With older versions, at least 153f2687a, it works.*
toolchain was used to update all including gcc and openmpi

psmp  version, OMP_NUM_THREADS can be different, nothing helps

I have no idea. An example of simple input with this problem is below.

`@SET Project        Li28_energy

@SET basis_set_path BASIS_MOLOPT
@SET potential_path GTH_POTENTIALS
@SET basis_set_li   SZV-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
@SET potential_li   GTH-PADE-q3
!GTH-PBE-q3 GTH-PADE-q3

@SET xc_functional  pade
!PBE pade

@SET cutoff         300
@SET added_mos      10
@SET max_scf        50
@SET eps_scf        1e-6

@SET lx             6.0
@SET ly             6.0

!---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

&GLOBAL
  PROJECT               ${Project}
  RUN_TYPE              ENERGY 
  !NEGF
  PRINT_LEVEL           LOW #MEDIUM #SILENT #MEDIUM #HIGH #DEBUG 
  EXTENDED_FFT_LENGTHS  .true.
&END GLOBAL

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD Quickstep

    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME ${basis_set_path}
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME ${potential_path}
    &MGRID
      CUTOFF ${cutoff}
    &END MGRID
    &SCF
      MAX_SCF   ${max_scf}
      EPS_SCF   ${eps_scf}
      SCF_GUESS restart
      !atomic
      ADDED_MOS ${added_mos}
      &SMEAR
        ELECTRONIC_TEMPERATURE [K] 10
        METHOD fermi_dirac
      &END SMEAR
      &MIXING
        ALPHA   0.3
        METHOD  broyden_mixing
        NBUFFER 8
      &END MIXING
      &PRINT
        &RESTART

      &END PRINT
    &END SCF
    &POISSON
      PERIODIC xyz
    &END POISSON
    &XC
      &XC_FUNCTIONAL ${xc_functional}

    &END XC
  &END DFT

  &SUBSYS
    &CELL

      !83.720
      PERIODIC xyz
    &END CELL
    &COORD
      Li            0.00000000           0.00000000           0.00000000 L2

title: Control also MOLDEN basis set output by NDIGITS
offload+grid: Change license to BSD-3-Clause
description:
We're switching to a permissive license for our newly written GPU code to make it easier for vendors to contribute. 

The vast majority of CP2K's code base will of course remain under the GPL, which we should not and cannot change.

Besides myself, the following people contributed to the new grid code: @mtaillefumier, @hfp, @mkrack, @dev-zero, @ltalirz. Could each of you please state that you agree to this license change.

title: USPEX 10.5: issue with @INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex' in CP2K
description:
Hello,

Used with CP2K, USPEX creates a file containing the cell parameters and atom positions named "subsys.uspex", which can conveniently be included in the Specific files cp2k_options_1 with the line:
  @INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex'

The problem is that the section &SUBSYS in my cp2k_options_1 already contains info about atom types like:
&SUBSYS
    &KIND C
      BASIS_SET DZVP-MOLOPT-SR-GTH
      POTENTIAL GTH-NLCC-PBE-q4
    &END KIND
&END SUBSYS
@INCLUDE 'subsys.uspex'

and the keyword &SUBSYS cannot be used more than once, whereas it is also present in subsys.uspex:
&SUBSYS
&CELL
ABC [angstrom] 4.0505422775 5.829809608 2.280013981
ALPHA_BETA_GAMMA [deg] 90 90 90
PERIODIC XYZ
&END
&COORD
SCALED
C 0.3181270957 0.5 0
C 0.6818729043 0.5 0.5
C 0.6818729043 0.5 0
C 0.3181270957 0.5 0.5
C 0.725584507 0.1219883189 0.25
C 0.274415493 0.8780116811 0.75
C 0.725584507 0.8780116811 0.75
C 0.274415493 0.1219883189 0.25
&END
&END SUBSYS

I tried to find the source file to remove the lines &SUBSYS and &END SUBSYS created by USPEX, but there is none. My guess is it's embedded in the MATLAB file, which cannot be openly modified. So I have no way to make USPEX work properly with CP2K. Besides it should rather be a 'cell.uspex' file rather than a 'subsys.uspex' file which is created by USPEX.

Please advise.

Thank you,
Xavier

title: Add Deep Potential plugin for CP2K
description:
Deep Potential (DP) is a machine learning potential based on deep neural network, first proposed by Zhang L. et al. (Doi: [10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cpc.2018.03.016)) By learning from coordinates as well as energy and forces of different structures from DFT calculations, a potential describing the whole system could be frozen to a file. By reading the file, program could yield energy, forces etc. from structures, and then MD simulation with DFT accuracy could be fastened greatly. This PR add Deep Potential support for CP2K, with potential file (e.g. `graph.pb`) supplied, CP2K could run MD simulation. 

**Only DeePMD-kit >= 2.0.0 is supportted.**

To enable the DeePMD-kit feature, `--with-deepmd` as well as `--with-tfcc` flag in toolchain should not be set to `no`. The following ways installing has been added.

 - Please set `--deepmd-mode` to `gpu` if GPU support is needed, and load CUDA environment before installing.
 - From exist DeePMD-kit C++ interface, with`$deepmd_root` and `$tensorflow_root` (Described [here](https://docs.deepmodeling.org/projects/deepmd/en/latest/install.html#install-the-deepmd-kit-s-c-interface) in detail), by setting `--with-deepmd=$deepmd_root` and `--with-tfcc=$tensorflow_root`.
  - From source code and library from Github and Anaconda Cloud, by setting the both flags to `install`. For now, DeePMD-kit v2.0.0.b1 and Tensorflow C++ interface 2.3.0 will be installed, and only CUDA Toolkit 10.1 is OK from this way. 
  - From system, by adding the header files and library to where `ld` command could read directly.

The result from CP2K has been tested to be the same with which from Lammps, supportted by Deepmodeling.

title: Try out ELPA's new redistribution feature
description:
During yesterday's ELPA meeting it was suggested that we try its new redistribution feature. This could have several advantages:

 - we could remove our [own redistribution code](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/fm/cp_fm_diag_utils.F)
 - hopefully resolve  [#1444](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/1444) as it's presumably caused by bugs in our redistribution
 - always arrive at a block size that is GPU compatible 
 - overall better performance because ELPA picks more optimal distributions

Unfortunately, I could not find any documentation on this. @marekandreas, maybe you could help?

Do not compile fourth derivatives in LibXC
description: We do not use fourth derivatives in our code.

title: Barostat issues with frozen atoms
description:
Hello,

Trying some MD runs, I have found that the coordinate scaling induced by NPT ensemble usage does affect the position of frozen atoms, and therefore, inter-atomic distances that should be fixed as well. It does not seem to be the desired behaviour in my opinion. 

I have worked on a fix for my own runs but it seems to provoke numerous failures in the test cases. My question is the following: should the npt_i and npt_f be properly fixed (which would probably imply more changes than what I did, as well as modifying some test cases), should I just implement my current version as a new keyword, or should I simply keep my version for myself ?

In any case, I will be happy to provide my working materials but my development time is limited since my PhD is about to come to an end.

Let me know which path you prefer !

Cheers,

Paul Clabaut

title: sirius: configuration params were not recognized and ignored
description:
With the current sirius version from the toolchail (v7.2.5):

```
=== Warning at line 104 of file /data/tiziano/cp2k/tools/toolchain/build/SIRIUS-7.2.5/src/context/simulation_parameters.cpp ===
The following configuration parameters were not recognized and ignored: potential_tol
```

I don't have the output ready, but from earlier tests I remember there were also more, like the evp_solver_type.

title: Bump to LibXC 5.1.5
description: Can any of the admins add the new tarball please?

title: libint: use the libint_build_2eri/3eri from libint_f rather than the forward declared ones
description:
For some reason the forward declarations result in nullptrs on some platforms like Cray Shasta with Zen2 and AArch64-based architectures, fixes #1576

With this patch the segfaults on Eiger are fixed, but some tests report wrong results (so far only one in MP2, regtests still running).

It would be good to verify this a bit more in-depth before merging.

title: Offload: Add timing ranges and mem_info
input: lone_keyword_c_vals does not pass values along with a lone keyword
description:
I've defined a new keyword as follows:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/55d3731eea70b965a4015ad0e31caf946183de86/src/input_cp2k_dft.F#L1325-L1332

yet when I tried to parse the values I obtained here:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/55d3731eea70b965a4015ad0e31caf946183de86/src/qs_scf_output.F#L239-L247

Specifying a lone keyword always gave me an array of size 1 with the first argument empty rather than the value given as `lone_keyword_c_vals`.

title: HIP grid integrate incorrect results on Mi50 (rocm 4.0.1)
description:
Hi,

We are experiencing incorrect **grid integrate** computation results on Mi50 (rocm 4.0.1), running the unit test `sample_tasks/general_tau.task`

## Environment

We are using commit b5a86d8dab03ee640791d3f22f2dca67a4a43bb1:
```
commit b5a86d8dab03ee640791d3f22f2dca67a4a43bb1
Author: Juerg Hutter <hutter@chem.uzh.ch>
Date:   Fri Jul 9 15:47:06 2021 +0200

    Coding conventions (#1581)
    
    * Coding conventions
    
    * Update DZ/DZP ADMM Basis for S/Cl
    
    * Adjust regtest tolerance
```

ROCm environment:

```
HIP version: 4.0.20496-4f163c68
clang version 12.0.0 (1100ebe275a9dcc79a9abbda902b6f10738f2f5d)
Target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
```


## How to reproduce

In `cp2k/src/grid`, we have modified `Makefile` a bit to make it work with HIP. Then we just `make` to make the two executables.

```diff
--- a/src/grid/Makefile
+++ b/src/grid/Makefile
@@ -5,9 +5,10 @@ all: grid_miniapp.x grid_unittest.x
 clean:
        rm -fv *.o */*.o *.x ../offload/*.o

-CFLAGS := -fopenmp -g -O3 -march=native -Wall -Wextra
-NVFLAGS := -g -O3 -lineinfo -arch sm_70 -Wno-deprecated-gpu-targets -Xcompiler "$(CFLAGS)" -D__GRID_CUDA                                                                              
-LIBS := -lm -lblas
+CFLAGS := -fopenmp -g -O3 -march=native -Wall -Wextra -D__HIP_PLATFORM_HCC__ -Wno-unused-parameter                         
+#NVFLAGS := -g -O3 -lineinfo -arch sm_70 -Wno-deprecated-gpu-targets -Xcompiler "$(CFLAGS)" -D__GRID_CUDA                                                                             
+NVFLAGS := -x hip -g -O3 $(CFLAGS) -D__GRID_HIP -D__HIP_PLATFORM_HCC__
+LIBS := -lm -lblas -llapack -llapacke -lcblas -lgfortran                                                                     

 ALL_HEADERS := $(shell find . -name "*.h") $(shell find ../offload/ -name "*.h")
 ALL_OBJECTS := ../offload/offload_buffer.o \
@@ -33,25 +34,31 @@ ALL_OBJECTS := ../offload/offload_buffer.o \
                cpu/tensor_local.o

 # Enable Cuda when nvcc compiler is present.
-NVCC := $(shell which nvcc)
+NVCC := $(shell which hipcc)
 ifneq ($(NVCC),)
-LIBS += -lcudart -lcuda -lcublas -L${CUDA_PATH}/lib64
-CFLAGS += -I${CUDA_PATH}/include -D__GRID_CUDA
+LIBS += -L$(ROCM_PATH)/lib -lamdhip64 -lrocblas -lhipblas
+CFLAGS += -I$(ROCM_PATH)/include -I$(ROCM_PATH)/hip/include -D__GRID_HIP
 ALL_OBJECTS += gpu/grid_gpu_task_list.o \
                gpu/grid_gpu_collocate.o \
-               gpu/grid_gpu_integrate.o
+               gpu/grid_gpu_integrate.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_context.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_collocate.o \
+               hip/grid_hip_integrate.o

 %.o: %.cu $(ALL_HEADERS)
        cd $(dir $<); $(NVCC) -c $(NVFLAGS) $(notdir $<)
+
+%.o: %.cc $(ALL_HEADERS)
+       cd $(dir $<); $(NVCC) -c $(NVFLAGS) $(notdir $<)
 endif

 %.o: %.c $(ALL_HEADERS)
        cd $(dir $<); $(CC) -c -std=c11 $(CFLAGS) $(notdir $<)

 grid_miniapp.x: grid_miniapp.o $(ALL_OBJECTS)
-       $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)
+       $(NVCC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)

 grid_unittest.x: grid_unittest.o $(ALL_OBJECTS)
-       $(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)
+       $(NVCC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^ $(LIBS)

 #EOF
```

## Failed test
In `grid` dir, running batched integration with `sample_tasks/general_tau.task` gives the following output:

```
$ ./grid_miniapp.x --integrate --batch 1 1 sample_tasks/general_tau.task                                                                                     

forces 0 0 ref: 2.859785e-05 test: 1.459657e+01 diff:1.459654e+01 rel_diff: 1.459654e+01
forces 0 1 ref: -1.651097e-05 test: 1.459625e+01 diff:1.459627e+01 rel_diff: 1.459627e+01
forces 0 2 ref: -3.698257e-02 test: 1.433749e+01 diff:1.437447e+01 rel_diff: 1.437447e+01
forces 1 0 ref: 2.859785e-05 test: 1.459657e+01 diff:1.459654e+01 rel_diff: 1.459654e+01
forces 1 1 ref: -1.651097e-05 test: 1.459625e+01 diff:1.459627e+01 rel_diff: 1.459627e+01
forces 1 2 ref: -3.698257e-02 test: 1.433749e+01 diff:1.437447e+01 rel_diff: 1.437447e+01
virial 0 0 ref: 8.805353e-01 test: 2.076011e+01 diff:1.987958e+01 rel_diff: 1.987958e+01
virial 0 1 ref: -7.457423e-10 test: 1.459637e+01 diff:1.459637e+01 rel_diff: 1.459637e+01
virial 0 2 ref: -3.557349e-06 test: 1.459634e+01 diff:1.459635e+01 rel_diff: 1.459635e+01
virial 1 0 ref: -7.457423e-10 test: 1.459637e+01 diff:1.459637e+01 rel_diff: 1.459637e+01
virial 1 1 ref: 8.805353e-01 test: 2.076011e+01 diff:1.987958e+01 rel_diff: 1.987958e+01
virial 1 2 ref: 2.053836e-06 test: 1.459638e+01 diff:1.459638e+01 rel_diff: 1.459638e+01
virial 2 0 ref: -3.557349e-06 test: 1.459634e+01 diff:1.459635e+01 rel_diff: 1.459635e+01
virial 2 1 ref: 2.053836e-06 test: 1.459638e+01 diff:1.459638e+01 rel_diff: 1.459638e+01
virial 2 2 ref: 9.507904e-01 test: 2.125190e+01 diff:2.030111e+01 rel_diff: 2.030111e+01
Task: sample_tasks/general_tau.task                             Integrate Batched   Cycles: 1.000000e+00   Max value: 1.549947e+00   Max rel diff: 2.030111e-03   Time: 2.430098e-02 sec

 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 -                                                                             -
 -                                GRID STATISTICS                              -
 -                                                                             -
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 LP    KERNEL             BACKEND                              COUNT     PERCENT
 5     integrate general  GPU                                      1     100.00%
 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Error: Maximal difference is too large.
```

Could you please take an eye on this problem?
Thanks!

title: Fix DBCSR HIP build to use CC and not OFFLOAD_CC
description:
When linking with OpenBLAS that is built with g++ with OpenMP support and enabling DBCSR's HIP backend, if hipcc is used to build DBCSR's cpp files, then in the final CP2K executable, we see two OpenMP runtimes linked. One (libgomp.so) that OpenBLAS pulled in, and another (libomp.so) that DBCSR pulled in. To avoid this, it is best to build DBCSR using the same host compiler as CP2K and add a flag to indicate that the HIP_PLATFORM is AMD. This commit and an equivalent commit to DBCSR will rely on the setting of USE_ACCEL to determine which compiler and flags to use for compiling DBCSR's cpp files for AMD devices.

I tested on AMD and NVIDIA devices, but not with the OpenCL backend. Also, I am not sure if other parts of CP2K need to be modified, I only fixed INSTALL.md and the generate_arch_files.sh script. Please test and let me know if more modifications are necessary.

title: toolchain: user env var filtering done in subshell
description: fixes #1567, obviously some shells started to use line continuations in export -p, and we broke those when using sed to filter env vars

title: fm: Upgrade to ELPA 2021.05.001 and use GPU kernel by default
[grid] Add hip backend
description:
- Add hip backend for AMD GPU support. The same code can also be used for NVIDIA
- simple template programming used for the hip kernels. The interface is agnostic (use of template functions for cuda/hipMemcpy etc...) and the task list data structure can be reused across backend.
- cross platform compatible since there are no hardware specific (such as inline asm) code.
- algorithm are tailored for minimal use of atomic operations (not possible to avoid them for collocate) at the prize of more memory use. It means in particular that calculating the hab or the coefficients for collocate is split from the main collocate and integrate kernels. It improves occupancy and has no drawbacks in performance.
- does not assume specific device_id. If cp2k provides one then it will take it otherwise defaulting to 0 (since the backend is not mpi aware).
- Minimal changes to the Makefile (it is hacky) -> cmake
- single kernel for orthorhombic and non orthorhombic cases. Orthorhombic case consider the generic case of a orthogonal basis for the grid lattice vectors (the dh is not necessarily diagonal).  
- Performances : H2O-32.inp (benchmarks/QS) in seconds. Results taken from cp2k timings

| V100                            |                  |           |           |             |              |             |
|------------------------------ | -------------- | ------- | -------- | --------- | ---------- | --------- |
| grid_integrate_task_list |           116 | 12.3  |  4.437 |   4.437 |   4.437 |   4.437 |
| grid_collocate_task_list |           116 | 9.6  |  2.614  |  2.614  |  2.614  |  2.614 |

| Mi100                         |                   |           |             |         |            |        |
| ----------------------------- | -------------- | -------- | --------- | ------ | -------- | ------ |
|grid_integrate_task_list     |      116| 12.3 |   4.140  |  4.140  |  4.140 |   4.140|
|grid_collocate_task_list     |      116|  9.6  |  4.459  |  4.459   | 4.459  |  4.459 |

title: specifying ndigit on output position/velocity/forces or output in scientific notation
description:
I'm currently running some simulations where the output does not come out with enough significant digits (position/velocity/forces), part of the reason for this is that I have chosen to output non-standard units.
Hence forces for instance comes out as:

 H         0.0104393281        0.0054219586       -0.0006507595

The big problem here is that these number are not precise enough to replicate the results to a reasonable degree, but there doesn't seem to be anyway to increase the number of digits, or ensure that the output is generated in scientific notation, which would ensure that digits are at least not lost.

There might be a way to do this that I just haven't found yet, but from reading the documentation I didn't find anything about this.

title: Recover line lost during cleaning of USE section
Add keyword EPS_CHECK_DIAG to global section
description:
- The keyword `EPS_CHECK_DIAG` was added to the  `&GLOBAL` section. It specifies the requested numerical accuracy for the orthonormality of the eigenvectors obtained after a diagonalization.
- A negative value for `EPS_CHECK_DIAG` disables the orthonormality check which is the default (`EPS_CHECK_DIAG = -1`)
- If the compilation flag `-D__CHECK_DIAG` has been used, the orthonormality check is always performed using the default threshold value of `5.0E-14` unless a different threshold value has been defined by the keyword `EPS_CHECK_DIAG`.
- If available, ELPA is again the default diagonalization library

title: Parallelize the calculation of 2c integrals for RI with GPW using OpenMP
Restart from calculation with &EXT_RESTART and &PERIODIC_EFIELD fails with CPASSERT
description:
When trying to use the restart file written by CP2K for a simulation with the following `EXT_RESTART` part AND a `&PERIODIC_EFIELD`:

```
&EXT_RESTART
        RESTART_FILE_NAME   ${RESFILE}-1.restart
        RESTART_COUNTERS T
        RESTART_POS T
        RESTART_BAND T
&END
```

CP2K immediately crashes with:

```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/                             CPASSERT failed                          *
 *    |                                                                        *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                                  cp_control_utils.F:424 *
 *******************************************************************************
```

The culprit is the internal generation of the `REAL_TIME_PROPAGATION` section in case `&EXT_RESTART` is used and `RESTART_RTP` is not explicitly set the `.FALSE.`
This then triggers the assertion which checks that RTP and the periodic efield are not enabled at the same time.

The user can salvage the restart file by explicitly setting `RESTART_RTP .FALSE.`.

title: Results from periodic GW should be taken with care
description: Periodic GW calculations in CP2K (https://journals.aps.org/prb/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevB.95.235123) have recently been detected to be numerically unstable when using large, diffuse basis sets. We are working on a replacement of the current periodic GW implementation by a more reliable implementation that includes k-point sampling of the density response and the screened Coulomb interaction.

Make doesn't work with paths containing white space
description:
The `make prettyclean` and also `make pretty` don't work if there are white spaces in the path. I'm a bit scared to run `make clean` on this machine right now, but the result is probably similar.

The line https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/Makefile#L339 leads to the execution of e.g. `rm -rf ~/00 ` if the code is in `~/00\ cp2k/` which could lead to accidental removal of unrelated files.

I'm not sure if this is just a feature of make. Maybe the whole process could be aborted after the first command fails like doing `set -e` in a shell script.

title: Feature request: auto-adjust EPS_PGF_ORB
description:
Hello devs,

When running hybrid calculations, especially with ADMM, its common to run into the following warning:

>  *** WARNING in hfx_energy_potential.F:592 :: The Kohn Sham matrix is not  ***
>  *** 100% occupied. This may result in incorrect Hartree-Fock results. Try ***
>  *** to decrease EPS_PGF_ORB and EPS_FILTER_MATRIX in the QS section. For  ***
>  *** more information see FAQ: https://www.cp2k.org/faq:hfx_eps_warning    ***

This warning comes before the HFX ERIs are calculated, and so evaluating it must be fairly quick.

I believe it would be especially useful to have an auto-converge setting for EPS_PGF_ORB. Something like `while warning exists; set EPS_PGF_ORB = EPS_PGF_ORB / 10 `. This way, you can ensure that the calculation is reasonably accurate and numerically stable (the latter being more sensitive to EPS_PGF_ORB in my experience). I have looked into the source, but having no idea of the structure of CP2K and not coded in FORTRAN before, I didn't get very far, and so I'd like to ask how hard it would be to build this into the dev branch of CP2K?

title: ELPA: memory leaks, wrong results and crashes when using small matrices
description:
as mentioned in #1428 

We currently see the following issues on our machines with ELPA:

* memory leaks (4TB RES+)
* hangs in MPI calls issued by ELPA (discovered by attaching gdb to running process and triggering a backtrace, maybe waiting for memory allocation?)
* sudden crashes of CP2K without any message (my guess is that MPI crashes, hence no processing of any I/O)
* wrong results in diagonalization

It is not related to CUDA or OpenMP (`OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` triggers it nonetheless), but most likely related to small matrices since it can be more easily triggered by small systems and when I looked into some of the hanging processes by attaching gdb and going up the stack matrix dim was < 100.

Also, while it got more problematic with the latest ELPA and CP2K releases, I observed similar problems with small matrix sizes with CP2K 7.1 (and the ELPA we used back then) but didn't have enough time to come up with a minimal reproducer to report to upstream.

title: Bump jinja2 from 2.11.2 to 2.11.3 in /tools/precommit
description:
Bumps [jinja2](https://github.com/pallets/jinja) from 2.11.2 to 2.11.3.
Release notes

Sourced from jinja2's releases.

2.11.3

This contains a fix for a speed issue with the urlize filter. urlize is likely to be called on untrusted user input. For certain inputs some of the regular expressions used to parse the text could take a very long time due to backtracking. As part of the fix, the email matching became slightly stricter. The various speedups apply to urlize in general, not just the specific input cases.

  • PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/Jinja2/2.11.3/
  • Changes: https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/2.11.x/changelog/#version-2-11-3
Changelog

Sourced from jinja2's changelog.

Version 2.11.3

Released 2021-01-31

  • Improve the speed of the urlize filter by reducing regex backtracking. Email matching requires a word character at the start of the domain part, and only word characters in the TLD. :pr:1343
Commits
  • cf21539 release version 2.11.3
  • 15ef8f0 Merge pull request #1343 from pallets/urlize-speedup
  • ef658dc speed up urlize matching
  • eeca0fe Merge pull request #1207 from mhansen/patch-1
  • 2dd7691 Merge pull request #1209 from mhansen/patch-3
  • 4892940 do_dictsort: update example ready to copy/paste
  • 7db7d33 api.rst: bugfix in docs, import PackageLoader
  • 9ec465b fix changelog header
  • See full diff in compare view

title: grid: Move tests from LIBTEST into QS/regtest-grid
call dgemv_, etc... fortran functions instead of cblas equivalent
description:
the code uses the cblas interface of MKL (openblas has it as well). When MKL is not used, then there is a fallback to hand written implementations of the functions. The problem comes from here and the only way to fix it without modifying the toolchain is to call the fortran routines with appropriate parameters. 

This fix is not portable since the fortran function mangling can be different from one compiler to another (the underscore at the end). The proper way to fix this is to use the cblas interface provided by lapack, openblas (amd BLIS also has this interface) but it means changing the toolchain so that liblapacke (that is compiled) is added to the libraries to link to *and* to add the include directory in the list of compilations options.

XAS: CP2K won't complete the XAS calculation
description:
I want to calculate XAS spectra by Gaussian and Augmented Plane Wave Method. I found [official tutorial] (https://www.cp2k.org/exercises:2017_uzh_cp2k-tutorial: gapw) and run cp2k with the input files of * Task 2 : compute the XAS spectrum of ice-1h *, which is not needed to edit. However, cp2k couldn't complete the calculation. Here is what I did.
## comment out `MAP_CONSISTENT`
Without any editing of the input files, cp2k said it didn't know `MAP_CONSISTENT` and aborted the calculation. So I commented out` MAP_CONSISTENT`.
## Aborted with segmentation fault error
Next, it stopped with segmentation fault error. In my workstation, stack size is limited within 8MB by default. So next, I made the stack size unlimited, and run again.
Then, it didn't stop with the error. However, it didn't finish the calculation even after 1 day progress. The system showed no error messase in the log and did not sleep. It had been calculationg sometiong.
According to the output of cp2k, it calculated up to `XAS_TP_SCF WAVEFUNCTION OPTIMIZATION`. The output was finished with following line:
`Calculation of 7 additional virtual states of the subspace complementary to the lowest 160 states`.
`ice1h-xas_at * .spectrum` files did not be generated.

I think this was caused by some cp2k's bug. Please verify this error, please.

title: Improve OpenMP performance of collocate and integrate
Rewrite m_memory with POSIX getrusage instead of reading /proc
description:
`m_memory` is currently not thread-safe (for example when sampling memory inside a bigger OMP loop) and does not support macOS. While we could make it threadsafe, it might be a good opportunity to switch over to the POSIX API `getrusage` (same for the more extended one).

This can happen in 2 ways:

* remodel the `timeval` and `rusage` structs in Fortran and call the function directly. Advantage: no additional wrapper. Disadvantage: might break if the structs don't match what the system provides (unsure up to which part POSIX defines it) or if the datatypes don't match exactly.
* write a small wrapper in C which extracts the necessary part and returns them in a struct we define. Advantage: the C side will transparently handle order of fields, type conversion, verifies that we have a proper definition. Disadvantage: additional complexity and possible source of error (on the other hand, breakages are less likely to go unnoticed).

This is somewhat orthogonal to what #630 proposes

title: Error when restarting calculations with MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL with COORD_FILE
description:
Using `EXT_RESTART` together with `MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL` and `COORD_FILE` results in a CPASSERT with a somewhat minimal error message
```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/                             CPASSERT failed                          *
 *    |                                                                        *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                                       atoms_input.F:122 *
 *******************************************************************************


 ===== Routine Calling Stack =====

            5 read_atoms_input
            4 coordinate_control_READ_COORDINATE
            3 coordinate_control
            2 topology_control
            1 CP2K
```
The line is
https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/a130d97bd8bbaf62cd271d8502577b480fef7b04/src/atoms_input.F#L122

Input file (based on [Si example](https://www.cp2k.org/howto:static_calculation))
```
&GLOBAL
  PROJECT Si_bulk8
  RUN_TYPE GEO_OPT
  PRINT_LEVEL LOW
&END GLOBAL
&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD Quickstep
  &SUBSYS
    &KIND Si
      ELEMENT   Si
      BASIS_SET DZVP-GTH-PADE
      POTENTIAL GTH-PADE-q4
    &END KIND
    &CELL
      A     5.430697500    0.000000000    0.000000000
      B     0.000000000    5.430697500    0.000000000
      C     0.000000000    0.000000000    5.430697500
      MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL 1 1 2
    &END CELL
    #&COORD
    #  Si    0.000000000    0.000000000    0.000000000
    #  Si    0.000000000    2.715348700    2.715348700
    #  Si    2.715348700    2.715348700    0.000000000
    #  Si    2.715348700    0.000000000    2.715348700
    #  Si    4.073023100    1.357674400    4.073023100
    #  Si    1.357674400    1.357674400    1.357674400
    #  Si    1.357674400    4.073023100    4.073023100
    #  Si    4.073023100    4.073023100    1.357674400
    #&END COORD
    &TOPOLOGY
      MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL 1 1 2
      COORD_FILE si.xyz
      COORD_FILE_FORMAT XYZ
    &END TOPOLOGY

  &END SUBSYS
  &DFT
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME  BASIS_SET
    POTENTIAL_FILE_NAME  GTH_POTENTIALS
    &QS
      EPS_DEFAULT 1.0E-10
    &END QS
    &MGRID
      NGRIDS 4
      CUTOFF 300
      REL_CUTOFF 60
    &END MGRID
    &XC
      &XC_FUNCTIONAL PADE
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
    &END XC
    &SCF
      SCF_GUESS ATOMIC
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-7
      MAX_SCF 300
      &DIAGONALIZATION  ON
        ALGORITHM STANDARD
      &END DIAGONALIZATION
      &MIXING  T
        METHOD BROYDEN_MIXING
        ALPHA 0.4
        NBROYDEN 8
      &END MIXING
    &END SCF
  &END DFT
  &PRINT
    &FORCES ON
    &END FORCES
  &END PRINT
&END FORCE_EVAL
&EXT_RESTART
 RESTART_FILE_NAME Si_bulk8-1.restart
&END EXT_RESTART
```
si.xyz
```
       8
 i =        1, E =       -31.2978853728
 Si         0.0000000020        0.0000000019        0.0000000023
 Si        -0.0000000011        2.7153487322        2.7153487326
 Si         2.7153487327        2.7153487332       -0.0000000013
 Si         2.7153487326       -0.0000000012        2.7153487331
 Si         4.0730230974        1.3576743672        4.0730230973
 Si         1.3576743706        1.3576743704        1.3576743702
 Si         1.3576743662        4.0730230969        4.0730230967
 Si         4.0730230974        4.0730230969        1.3576743676
```

In the restart file, the `MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL 1 1 2` calculation is translated into a `MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL 1 1 1` calculation with a doubled cell, which likely causes the issue.

Interestingly, the issue is *not* raised (i.e. I can restart without problems) when specifying the coordinates directly in the CP2K input file like so:
```
    ...
    &COORD
      Si    0.000000000    0.000000000    0.000000000
      Si    0.000000000    2.715348700    2.715348700
      Si    2.715348700    2.715348700    0.000000000
      Si    2.715348700    0.000000000    2.715348700
      Si    4.073023100    1.357674400    4.073023100
      Si    1.357674400    1.357674400    1.357674400
      Si    1.357674400    4.073023100    4.073023100
      Si    4.073023100    4.073023100    1.357674400
    &END COORD
    &TOPOLOGY
      MULTIPLE_UNIT_CELL 1 1 2
    &END TOPOLOGY
   ...
```

It would be nice if

 1. A descriptive error message was shown
 2. The number of atoms in the system was computed consistently, such that the restart of the calculation works in both cases

Adding Gaussian / Methfessel-Paxton / Marzari-Vanderbilt smearing to the toolbox
description:
Particularly in high-throughput studies, where one does not know a priori whether a given structure is metallic or insulating, it can be useful to use diagonalization with smearing as a fallback if orbital transformation does not converge.

Fermi-Dirac smearing is known to introduce rather strong deviations in e.g. the atomic forces compared to other smearing schemes (see e.g. Figure 7 of [this paper](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6424870/)).

To your knowledge, has there been any work in the direction of extending the [smearing schemes available in cp2k](https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/FORCE_EVAL/DFT/SCF/SMEAR.html#list_METHOD) (forks, ...)?
If not, would it be possible to get some pointers on where/how to best get started; any details one should pay attention to?
I guess the place to start would be [here](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/5a03381abe24376cf018f40887736cdc95d3b0a6/src/qs_mo_occupation.F#L557).

Apropos: @flavianojs from our group just [added to Quantum Espresso](https://gitlab.com/QEF/q-e/-/merge_requests/1294) an implementation for reliably computing Fermi energies for Methfessel-Paxton and Marzari-Vanderbilt smearing (which produce non-monotonous occupations) and might be able to help a bit with this as well.

title: toolchain: COSMA picks up system-MPI instead of builtin/provided one
description:
a user reported the following failure when building COSMA with the toolchain:

```
[...]
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:

In static member function ‘static ompi_datatype_t*
grid2grid::mpi_type_wrapper<std::complex<double> >::type()’:
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:24:41:

error: ‘MPI_CXX_DOUBLE_COMPLEX’ was not declared in this scope; did you
mean ‘MPI_C_DOUBLE_COMPLEX’?
    24 |     static MPI_Datatype type() { return
MPI_CXX_DOUBLE_COMPLEX; }
       |                                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
       |                                         MPI_C_DOUBLE_COMPLEX
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:

In static member function ‘static ompi_datatype_t*
grid2grid::mpi_type_wrapper<std::complex<float> >::type()’:
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:29:41:

error: ‘MPI_CXX_FLOAT_COMPLEX’ was not declared in this scope; did you
mean ‘MPI_C_FLOAT_COMPLEX’?
    29 |     static MPI_Datatype type() { return MPI_CXX_FLOAT_COMPLEX; }
       |                                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
       |                                         MPI_C_FLOAT_COMPLEX
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:

In static member function ‘static ompi_datatype_t*
grid2grid::mpi_type_wrapper<bool>::type()’:
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/build/cosma-2.2.0/libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/mpi_type_wrapper.hpp:64:41:

error: ‘MPI_CXX_BOOL’ was not declared in this scope; did you mean
‘MPI_C_BOOL’?
    64 |     static MPI_Datatype type() { return MPI_CXX_BOOL; }
       |                                         ^~~~~~~~~~~~
       |                                         MPI_C_BOOL
make[2]: ***
[libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/CMakeFiles/grid2grid.dir/transform.cpp.o]
Error 1
make[2]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
make[1]: *** [libs/grid2grid/src/grid2grid/CMakeFiles/grid2grid.dir/all]
Error 2
make[1]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
[ 28%] Linking CXX static library liboptions.a
[ 28%] Built target options
make: *** [all] Error 2 
```

This error gets triggered by an older MPI which doesn't have the MPI_CXX_BOOL (check the MPI standards/OpenMPI errata). The interesting thing is that the user actually provided a newer OpenMPI release:

```
user@host:~> which mpicc
/home/user/Software_2021/openmpi_4.1.0/bin/mpicc 
```

And looking in the `cmake.log` reveals:

```
-- The CXX compiler identification is GNU 9.3.0
-- Detecting CXX compiler ABI info
-- Detecting CXX compiler ABI info - done
-- Check for working CXX compiler:
/home/user/Software_2021/gcc_9.3.0/bin/g++ - skipped
-- Detecting CXX compile features
-- Detecting CXX compile features - done
-- Found MPI_CXX: /usr/lib64/mpi/gcc/openmpi/lib64/libmpi_cxx.so (found
version "2.1")
-- Found MPI: TRUE (found version "2.1") 
```

For some reason not all packages (in this case COSMA) in the toolchain pick up an MPI implementation just from the `$PATH`. This can potentially be very bad as packages may enable different kind of codepaths for different MPI implementations and versions, leading to link- or runtime failures (at best) or data corruption (at worst) when built against one MPI version but link against a different one.

We need to:

* [ ] investigate why COSMA in this case doesn't pick up the MPI from `$PATH`, likely it is because CMake requires to explicitly have CC/FC/CXX set to ensure it picks up the right MPI implemention, at least that's what we figured out for [DBCSR Cmake](https://cp2k.github.io/dbcsr/develop/page/2-user-guide/1-installation/1-cmake-build-recipes.html)
* [ ] whether other packages in the toolchain suffer from the same issue
* [ ] check in the respective MPI install scripts that the vars are set and the provided implementation is usable, if the user needs to set other paths as well

This may be related to other build issues we see, like #1274, #1275

title: regtest: use TEST_DIRS for unittests to avoid running leftover tests and support labels
description:
I get in a `make test` run:

```
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
[...]
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
/data/tiziano/cp2k.clean/regtesting/local/sdbg/TEST-local-sdbg-2021-02-03_14-22-31/UNIT/grid_collocate_unittest.out
Could not open task file: /data/tiziano/cp2k.clean/regtesting/local/sdbg/../../../src/grid/sample_tasks/collocate_ortho_density_l0000.task
EXIT CODE:  1  MEANING:  RUNTIME FAIL
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
[...]
```

That binary was renamed some time ago. Unfortunately contrary to our other regtests, unittests are just discovered by convention rather than by explicitly registering them.

This breaks the workflow for people switching branches regularly and makes for example a `git bisect` more difficult since you have to explicitly clean the exe-dir.

```
/usr/bin/ld:
/home/user/Software_2021/Source/cp2k-8.1/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.16.1/lib/libxsmm.a(libxsmm_timer.o):
undefined reference to symbol 'clock_gettime@@GLIBC_2.2.5'
/usr/bin/ld: note: 'clock_gettime@@GLIBC_2.2.5' is defined in DSO
/lib64/librt.so.1 so try adding it to the linker command line
```

Even though the POSIX standard does not say what to link to get `clock_gettime` it seems to be found in `-lrt` even for BSD-systems (macOS not included).

title: toolchain: building libint 2.6.0 fails on CPUs with FMA4 due to wrong/missing intrinsics
description:
from the `make.log`:

```
[...]
CXX
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__d__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.lo
CXX
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__f__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.lo
CXX
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__g__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.lo
CXX
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__h__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.lo
CXX
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__i__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.lo
In file included from ./include/libint2/util/vector.h:186,
                 from ./include/libint2_types.h:24,
                 from ./include/libint2/util/generated/libint2_types.h:29,
                 from ./include/libint2.h:35,
                 from
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__g__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.cc:21:
./include/libint2/util/vector_x86.h: In function
‘libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat
libint2::simd::fma_plus(libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat,
libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat, libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat)’:
./include/libint2/util/vector_x86.h:1106:11: error: ‘_mm256_facc_ps’ was
not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘_mm256_macc_ps’?
 1106 |     d.d = _mm256_facc_ps(a.d, b.d, c.d);
      |           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |           _mm256_macc_ps
In file included from ./include/libint2/util/vector.h:186,
                 from ./include/libint2_types.h:24,
                 from ./include/libint2/util/generated/libint2_types.h:29,
                 from ./include/libint2.h:35,
                 from
src/CR_DerivGaussP0InBra_aB_d001__0__f__1___TwoPRep_s__0__s__1___Ab__up_0.cc:21:
./include/libint2/util/vector_x86.h: In function
‘libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat
libint2::simd::fma_plus(libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat,
libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat, libint2::simd::VectorAVXFloat)’:
./include/libint2/util/vector_x86.h:1106:11: error: ‘_mm256_facc_ps’ was
not declared in this scope; did you mean ‘_mm256_macc_ps’?
 1106 |     d.d = _mm256_facc_ps(a.d, b.d, c.d);
      |           ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |           _mm256_macc_ps 
[...]
```

Inspecting `vector_x86.h` reveals that the stated intrinsics are used when `__FMA4__` is defined, which is most likely only the case on AMD Bulldozer/Piledriver (Opteron).
Unfortunately I couldn't find any references to the specified intrinsics so far.

Furthermore (and here is the problem): it seems that FMA4 on Zen is still available, but seems to be broken and while it doesn't get exposed via `cpuid` there are some binutils around which still enable it. See the [wikipedia entry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FMA_instruction_set) for an overview and the respective links.

While it currently seems that when `__FMA4__` is set, the build will fail, there is also a chance that it could compile but return wrong results when running on a Zen, hence we should add `-mno-fma4` if we detect that `-fma4` is added as part of `-march=native` to prevent that from happening and fix the build on platforms which would actually support FMA4.
As we have seen in libvori with adding `-mno-avx` we can't simply add `-mno-fma4` all the time.

* [ ] fix the issue in the toolchain
* [ ] report to libint upstream
* [ ] fix/report in Spack (maybe not affected since they maintain their own list of flags for each microarch) and Easybuild

title: Tansformation of MO coefficients from spherical to Cartesian basis
description:
Seems to me that there is a bug in transforming the MO coefficients from a spherical to a Cartesian basis. The orbital transformation matrix "s2c" is used in two places in the code to accomplish this transformation. However, this matrix transforms the spherical functions (not the coefficients) to Cartesian ones. The correct transformation for the coefficients should be the "c2s" matrix. The transformation matrices are defined in aobasis/orbital_transformation_matrices.F

This is done twice in the code:
1. In qs_mo_io::write_mo_set_to_output_unit
2. In molden_utils::write_mos_molden, might be relate to the issue here: https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/JlJuAz8belA/m/Pgd8dWzpCgAJ

In the attached file I have written down my reasoning regarding the correct transformation. I would appreciate if this would either be confirmed or corrected.

In case this turns out indeed to be a bug, I can provide a patch.

[sph_to_cart.pdf](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/5886362/sph_to_cart.pdf)

The make.log of elpa says:

Why dose it access nonexistent file/directory? And how to avoid it?

And also note that even editing 'remove_xcompiler' may not make sence since it will regenerate when installing, please.

title: toolchain: elpa fails to build without `python` binary
description:
I tried to install cp2k to our workstation.

The problem happend when I install elpa and other libraries by toolchain with following command:

`./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --math-mode=mkl --mpi-mode=intelmpi --with-cmake=install --with-libxsmm=install --with-mkl=system --with-fftw=install --with-reflapack=no --with-scalapack=no --with-sirius=no --with-cosma=no --with-plumed --with-gsl=install --with-hdf5=install --with-superlu=install --with-libvori=no -j 4`

'male.log' of elpa said `/usr/bin/env: ‘python’: Permission denied` because my workstation has python at `/usr/bin/python3`, and  it works only with`python3` command, not `python` command.

It seems that it comes from './build/elpa-2020.05.001/remove_xcompiler'.

To recognize the python path, I tried: 
- run `export PYTHON="/usr/bin/python3"` before install: failure. 
- change script in 'remove_xcompiler': failre because it generates every time.
- add `export PYTHON="/usr/bin/python3"` to configure of elpa: failure because it also regenerate.
- add `export PYTHON="/usr/bin/python3"` to install_elpa.sh: failure.
- add `export PYTHON="/usr/bin/python3"` to install_cp2k_toolchain.sh: failure.

Dose anybody know how to set python path when installing elpa by toolchain?

title: Remove POINTER spec from dummy arguments where possible
description:
While a POINTER can be passed as non-POINTER actual argument, it does not work the other way around. This makes it impossible to call any of the `get_*`, `set_*` functions with anything else than a pointer.

Also, an INTENT(IN) spec on a POINTER dummy argument acts on the
POINTER, meaning that while the POINTER can not be changed, its contents still can.

The only thing lost is that we can not check explicitly whether an actual POINTER argument is ASSOCIATED, but we have to rely on the compiler to provide those checks (-fcheck with gcc). Given that those assertions have been missing more often than not we will not lose much here while making it possible to switch to ALLOCATABLEs.

One note of caution: passing an unassociated pointer to such a function may work with GCC (w/o -fcheck), while code generated with Intel or Cray will reliably produce segfaults.

title: Toolchain: Split into stages to simplify Dockerfile
Performance decrease on avx512 instruction set extensions
description:
Recently installed Cp2k-8.1 on a HPC cluster.

Firstly i built the cp2k on a AVX128 machine and compiled according to its AVX128 architecture.
And sent my jobs to a bigger pool which has AVX512 supported CPUs. CP2K handed me a hint telling me 

>  *** HINT in environment.F:802 :: The compiler target flags (x86_avx) used ***
>  *** to build this binary cannot exploit all extensions of this CPU model  ***
>  *** (x86_avx512). Consider compiler target flags as part of FCFLAGS and   ***
>  *** CFLAGS (ARCH file).                                                   ***

After i saw it, i've started building CP2K from scratch with AVX512 compatibility on the AVX512 machine. 
Now it doesn't hand me down that hint and in the "GLOBAL" section of output file it says it is the right build for the CPU.

But unexpectedly,
AVX128 build running on an AVX512 machine,
 MD| CPU time per MD step [s]                                   0.614059 (avg.)
 
AVX512 build running on an AVX512 machine
 MD| CPU time per MD step [s]                                   0.633807 (avg.)
 
That's a %3.2 performance decrease in average. Is it somehow expected or might be a minor inconvience and should be neglected ?

title: Slower performance in cp2k-8.1
description:
I built the newest cp2k-8.1 and previous cp2k-7.1 on my cluster using intel-2018-update3 compiler (including icc, ifort, intel-mpi and MKL). Their porformances on a simple BOMD task are tested, and timing information are attached. It seems version 8.1 is slower than 7.1. I also noticed that in version 8.1, the grid part in src is rewritten in C, and the SUBROUTINEs **grid_integrate_task_list** and **grid_collocate_task_list** take up a lot of time, while in version 7.1 the SUBROUTINEs **integrate_v_rspace** and **calculate_rho_elec** are faster. 

Is there any suggestions that the performance of version 8.1 can be improved?

[8.1_timing.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/5854234/8.1_timing.txt)

[7.1_timing.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/5854233/7.1_timing.txt)

BTW, the arch files for compiling 8.1 and 7.1 are almost the same, except that in 8.1 arch file, the following line should be added accounting for grid part written in C.

CFLAGS += -std=c11 -qopenmp -I$(LIBXSMM)/include

description: PLUMED automatically links against a GSL when found (check `install/plumed-2.6.1/lib/libplumedKernel.so`), even if disable in the toolchain. If GSL is disabled in the toolchain, then PLUMED should not link against a GSL.

title: Limit veclib threads for executables using accelerate framework on MacOS?
description:
The [default ssmp arch file for Macos](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/arch/Darwin-gfortran.ssmp)  uses the `-D_ACCELERATE` option and links against the threaded `-framework Accelerate`.

This results in calls to BLAS and LAPACK potentially using threads, whose number is not controlled by `OMP_NUM_THREADS` but by `VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS`.
For example, using `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` on my macbook running MacOS 10.15.7 results in the cp2k.ssmp executable (e.g. the one from conda-forge) anywhere from 2-9 threads (but always <= 100% of a core).

In a small test, the timings of the cp2k executable with `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1` and `VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS=1` are about 13% faster than without limiting the number of veclib threads. I could test more with >1 OpenMP thread as well, but it somehow sounds reasonable that  running 9 threads is not very efficient when all of them are full-on computing and have to share the same resources.

As mentioned by @dev-zero in https://github.com/conda-forge/cp2k-feedstock/pull/16#issuecomment-760998951 it might be useful to check the `VECLIB_MAXIMUM_THREADS` env var when `__ACCELERATE` is defined and inform the user to set it to 1 if not done already.

title: toolchain: do not specify unused preprocessor/include flags in generated arch file
description:
From a standard run of the toolchain I get the following the `arch/local.psmp` file:

```
DFLAGS      = -D__LIBXSMM  -D__parallel  -D__FFTW3  -D__SPFFT -D__LIBINT -D__LIBXC -D__SCALAPACK -D
__COSMA -D__ELPA -D__GSL -D__SPGLIB -D__HDF5 -D__LIBVDWXC -D__SIRIUS
```

of which `__SPFFT`, `__COSMA`, `__GSL`, `__HD5`, `__LIBVDWXC` are not used by CP2K. The same goes for the respective include flags.

title: Possible parallel build issue: Error: Unclassifiable statement at (1)
description:
With the following arch file as my `Darwin-gfortran.ssmp` on a MacBook Air with an M1 CPU:

```
CC       = gcc-10
FC       = gfortran
LD       = gfortran
AR       = ar -r
RANLIB   = ranlib
DFLAGS   = -D__NO_STATM_ACCESS -D__ACCELERATE -D__LIBXC -D__FFTW3
CFLAGS   = -std=c11 -fopenmp -O2
FCFLAGS  = -I$(LIBXC_INCLUDE_DIR) -fopenmp -funroll-loops \
           -ftree-vectorize -ffree-form -O2 $(DFLAGS)
LDFLAGS  = $(FCFLAGS)
LIBS     = -framework Accelerate \
           -L$(LIBXC_LIB_DIR) -lxcf03 -lxc -lfftw3_omp -lfftw3
```

I could trigger the issue reported in the Google Groups here:

https://groups.google.com/g/cp2k/c/GJYQd6kh0g4/m/xbkVuPwoDQAJ
([direct link to log file](https://04325810545079678207.googlegroups.com/attach/d28fcb815b9c5/log?part=0.1&view=1&vt=ANaJVrHjmCEGwym-utixsOulhnEcoMbQmhHWZMmJX5KFSieOBZa_F7W7Qv55XIFnsBT5BH3TzJPQ55pIlloUUWIty4o8q8dzbXtUjTqlzdp0SF8T5MlwtjY))

```console
$ make ARCH=Darwin-gfortran VERSION=ssmp -j
[...]
gfortran -c -I/opt/homebrew/include -fopenmp -funroll-loops -ftree-vectorize -ffree-form -O2 -std=f2008 -D__NO_STATM_ACCESS -D__ACCELERATE -D__LIBXC -D__FFTW3 -D__COMPILE_ARCH="\"Darwin-gfortran\"" -D__COMPILE_DATE="\"Mon Jan 18 13:00:51 CET 2021\"" -D__COMPILE_HOST="\"Administrators-MacBook-Air.local\"" -D__COMPILE_REVISION="\"git:42512f7\"" -D__DATA_DIR="\"/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/data\"" -D__SHORT_FILE__="\"semi_empirical_int_ana.F\"" -I'/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/' -I'/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/obj/Darwin-gfortran/ssmp/exts/dbcsr' semi_empirical_int_ana.F90 
/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/semi_empirical_int_debug.F:19:1:

   19 | SUBROUTINE check_rotmat_der(sepi, sepj, rjiv, ij_matrix, do_invert)
      | 1
Error: Unclassifiable statement at (1)
/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/semi_empirical_int_debug.F:21:22:

   21 |    USE kinds, ONLY: dp
      |                      1
Error: USE statement at (1) cannot follow PRIVATE statement at (2)
/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/semi_empirical_int_debug.F:22:45:

   22 |    USE semi_empirical_int_utils, ONLY: rotmat
      |                                             1
Error: USE statement at (1) cannot follow PRIVATE statement at (2)
/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/semi_empirical_int_debug.F:27:54:

   27 |                                    se_int_control_type
      |                                                      1
Error: USE statement at (1) cannot follow PRIVATE statement at (2)
/Users/admin/Desktop/cp2k/src/./base/base_uses.f90:13:53:
```

title: CP2K terminates with "Number of threads has changed! " when built without __FFTW3 and OMP_NUM_THREADS /= 1
description:
I built CP2K on macOS Big Sur/XCode 12 with `gcc-10` and `gfortran` from Homebrew with the following arch file:

```
CC       = gcc-10
FC       = gfortran
LD       = gfortran
AR       = ar -r
RANLIB   = ranlib
DFLAGS   = -D__NO_STATM_ACCESS -D__ACCELERATE -D__LIBXC
CFLAGS   = -std=c11 -fopenmp -O2
FCFLAGS  = -I$(LIBXC_INCLUDE_DIR) -fopenmp -funroll-loops \
           -ftree-vectorize -ffree-form -O2 $(DFLAGS)
LDFLAGS  = $(FCFLAGS)
LIBS     = -framework Accelerate \
           -L$(LIBXC_LIB_DIR) -lxcf03 -lxc
```

This triggered a

```
 *******************************************************************************
 *   ___                                                                       *
 *  /   \                                                                      *
 * [ABORT]                                                                     *
 *  \___/                     Number of threads has changed!                   *
 *    |                                                                        *
 *  O/|                                                                        *
 * /| |                                                                        *
 * / \                                         dbcsr_iterator_operations.F:179 *
 *******************************************************************************
```

in all runs I've tried (for example the `benchmark/QS`).

Installing fftw3 and building with `-D__FFTW3` solved the issue as did setting `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`.

title: elpa: enable OMP threading when called from non-parallel regions
grid/hybrid: memalign -> aligned_alloc in CUDA path
Compiling cp2k 8.1 on macOS
description:
We're trying to build cp2k 8.1 on macOS as part of Homebrew: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/pull/68951

We are seeing several failures:

- `malloc.h` header, fixed by https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/4139ab6cd2d60c112964a6c75c5d52576ac49e6b (but not part of the released version)
- the latest stable libint version for CP2K (`libint-v2.6.0-cp2k-lmax-4`) fails to build with GCC 10: `Error: missing '(' before "__has_include" operand`)
- and if we fix that by moving to the libint-cp2k beta version, `make ARCH=Darwin-IntelMacintosh-gfortran VERSION=ssmp` fails with:

```
/private/tmp/cp2k-20210116-20292-1c8dsfw/cp2k-8.1/src/grid/common/grid_library.c:28:2:
error: #error "OpenMP is required. Please add -fopenmp to your C compiler flags."
```

I do not see the reason why `-fopenmp` was not added to `CFLAGS` in the darwin arch files (or BSD ones, for that matter). Are we doing something wrong, or is it a bug?

description:
There was a very subtle bug in the spin-orbit coupling treatment of open-shell system in XAS LR-TDDFT. It would only appear when using CUDA or a large number of OMP threads, as mentioned in #1243.

This PR should fix it.

title: grid: use posix_memalign instead of deprecated memalign
description:
"modern" systems like macOS BigSur do not provide malloc.h anymore,
this is required for CP2K to build on macOS

This could potentially break the build on older systems and may require
additional #ifdef logic.

Besides, it seems there are some hardcoded alignments to 4096 which should
probably be replaced by `sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE)` or `4*sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE)`.
As an alternative one could use `aligned_alloc` (from C11), but that has
additional restrictions on the size.

title: buildsystem: build fails on macOS BigSur: ld: archive has no table of contents file
description:
When trying to build CP2K v8.1 with `ARCH=minimal VERSION=sdbg` (arch-file patched to use `gcc-10` from homebrew due to OpenMP-requirement), building fails at the linker step:

```console
Discovering programs ...
Removing stale archives for sdbg ... 
Resolving dependencies for sdbg ... 
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/make -C /Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/exts/dbcsr -f .cp2k/Makefile \
	  INCLUDEMAKE=/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/arch/minimal.sdbg \
	  LIBDIR=/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/lib/minimal/sdbg/exts/dbcsr \
	  OBJDIR=/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/obj/minimal/sdbg/exts/dbcsr \
	  ACC="" \
	  ACCFLAGS=""
Removing stale archives ... 
Resolving dependencies ... 
gfortran -fopenmp   -L/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/lib/minimal/sdbg -o /Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/exe/minimal/grid_miniapp.sdbg grid_miniapp.o  -lcp2kgrid -lcp2kgridhybrid -lcp2kgridcpu -lcp2kgridref -lcp2kgridcommon -lcp2ksubsys -lcp2kfm -lcp2kao -lcp2kinput -lcp2kpw -lcp2kfft -lcp2kcommon -lcp2kmpiwrap -lcp2kfpga -lcp2kbase -L/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/lib/minimal/sdbg/exts/dbcsr -ldbcsr -llapack -lblas
ld: archive has no table of contents file '/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/lib/minimal/sdbg/libcp2kgridhybrid.a' for architecture x86_64
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
make[3]: *** [/Volumes/data/tiziano/cp2k/exe/minimal/grid_miniapp.sdbg] Error 1
make[2]: *** [all] Error 2
make[1]: *** [sdbg] Error 2
make: *** [all] Error 2
```

Since there's no CUDA support, the `.a` files of `grid/hybrid` have actually no symbols:

```console
$ nm lib/minimal/sdbg/libcp2kgridhybrid.a
lib/minimal/sdbg/libcp2kgridhybrid.a(grid_collocate_hybrid.o):
no symbols

lib/minimal/sdbg/libcp2kgridhybrid.a(grid_integrate_hybrid.o):
no symbols
```

... and as it seems `ar` (respectively `ranlib`) from Xcode has a problem with linking empty `.a` files: adding a dummy function which is not "ifdefed" out to `hybrid/grid_collocate_hybrid.c` makes the error go away.

The same applies for `libcp2kfpga.a`.

title: make test returns exit code 0 if tests fail
description:
For automated cp2k builds and runs of the test suite it would be useful, if `make test` would only return exit code 0 if all tests pass (and otherwise return a nonzero exit code).

See e.g. [these logs](https://dev.azure.com/conda-forge/feedstock-builds/_build/results?buildId=261784&view=logs&j=656edd35-690f-5c53-9ba3-09c10d0bea97&t=e5c8ab1d-8ff9-5cae-b332-e15ae582ed2d&l=23316) from the conda-forge build where we explicitly ask for `set -e` in the [build script](https://github.com/conda-forge/cp2k-feedstock/blob/52438996ebcf2d79d13711c4b0b025f62ac07f59/recipe/build.sh#L2).
