title: Typo in install_fftw.sh leads to build failures
description:
There is an error in the install_fftw.sh script:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/552c70dd21f9941e515a971f7079262164def7ba/tools/toolchain/scripts/install_fftw.sh#L100

The call to prepend-path is incorrect because of "=", I believe it should be
`prepend_path PKG_CONFIG_PATH "$pkg_install_dir/lib/pkgconfig"`

This results in install_fftw.sh overriding PKG_CONFIG_PATH instead of prepending to it.

title: f77_interface checking for convergence of HF calculation
description:
I have been using the f77_interface to interface an MD code to CP2K with great success so far.
The only issue I have run into is that currently I cannot detect if the HF calculation converged or not. Meaning that I usually have to babysit the first couple of iterations + check the whole calculation in the end to make sure that all steps taken were valid.

Is it possible to add a logical or similar that one can pull out of the interface to check if the HF calculation has actually converged, as currently the logical that checks for this is confined to be local in the HF loops?

title: toolchain: Track GPL compatibility for all dependencies
description: I've stopped pushing images to Dockerhub, because I lost track of our dependencies and was afraid to violate the GPL. We should track [GPL compatibility](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-faq.html#WhatDoesCompatMean) as metadata in the toolchain. Then we could add a flag `--gpl-compliant` which limits the installation such that the resulting binaries can be freely shared.

title: cp2k shell binary missing from 7.1 release artifacts
description:
The [latest release](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/releases/tag/v7.1.0) no longer includes a binary of the cp2k shell.

Is this on purpose? If not, could it be added?

Mentioning @oschuett

title: Add implementation of submatrix method
description:
This pull request adds an implementation of the submatrix method, as described in:

> Michael Lass, Stephan Mohr, Hendrik Wiebeler, Thomas D. Kühne, and Christian Plessl. 2018. A Massively Parallel Algorithm for the Approximate Calculation of Inverse p-th Roots of Large Sparse Matrices. In Proceedings of the Platform for Advanced Scientific Computing Conference (PASC ’18). Association for Computing Machinery, New York, NY, USA, Article 7, 1–11. DOI:https://doi.org/10.1145/3218176.3218231

The submatrix method allows to approximately solve matrix functions for large, sparse matrices by solving the same function on a set of smaller, dense matrices. While the original publication mainly targets inverse p-th roots, the method can similarly applied to other matrix functions.

As part of this pull request, an option is added to calculate the sign function within dm_ls_scf using the submatrix method. Solving the sign function for the submatrices can be either done using a Newton-Schulz iteration or a direct approach based on an explicit eigendecomposition.

title: Regtests in QS/regtest-corr_dipm take too long
description:
The [newly added](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/5ffbcad9ea8227fd8543f587798aec8a93f0d613) regests in [QS/regtest-corr_dipm](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/tree/master/tests/QS/regtest-corr_dipm) take quite long, which occasionally leads to [timeouts](https://dashboard.cp2k.org/archive/minimal-sdbg/commit_5bb94ebccdf5243fb3bec20cb3c479a0f89e5718.txt).

@hforbert, could you please have a look? Ideally, tests shouldn't run longer than [30 seconds](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/issues/174).

low-scaling RPA/GW: optimizations and refactoring
description:
- better heuristics for block sizes based on basis set sizes and sort basis sets for more sparsity
- batched contraction optimizations (load balancing and use of
  DBCSR-internal optimizations)
- unifying routines to create tensors
- reduce memory bottleneck of 3-center integral calculation

title: Adapt testcode as regtest-driver
description:
[testcode](https://testcode.readthedocs.io) is a more generalized Python-based implementation of our regtest script. Wannier90 for example adopted it for their testing.

What they're lacking is support for farming (used for the many short tasks we have), but this could be implemented as an extension of `testcode` by either using a helper doing `MPI_Comm_spawn` (preferred if supported by popular implementations) or by wrapping tests inside generated CP2K farming inputs.

title: Update DOI resolver
description: https://www.doi.org/doi_handbook/2_Numbering.html#htmlencoding

title: Improved time/frequency treatment for low-scaling GW
description: The LUMOs computed with the low-scaling GW algorithm of all 100 molecules from the GW100 set deviate now on average 0.007 eV from the reference data. HOMO is slightly worse due to multiple GW solutions. HOMOs in practice are expected to be as well behaved as LUMOs in GW100 because in larger molecules and materials, the self-interaction is less severe than in small molecules as in GW100. High accuracy is also gained using RI with truncated Coulomb metric as implemented by Patrick Seewald. In the long term, high accuracy is especially important for applying and benchmarking the k-point low-scaling GW code for periodic systems.

title: do_regtest: Fix miscount of unit tests
do_regtest: Make reference values mandatory
description:
Since have now have reference values for 99.8% of our tests, I believe it's time to drop support for tests without reference value. This will greatly simplify the reimplementation of do_regtest in Python, which in turn will enable many other things including e.g. efficient GPU testing. 

Unfortunately, I had to ''sacrifice'' three tests by converting them to test type zero, ie. we'll only check for runtime failures.

title: elpa cuda build
description:
Hi, I'm trying to build the recent master version with Cuda 10.1.105 support for V100, gcc 7.3.0. After ELPA's configure, I need to manually modify Makefile for both _obj_no_thread_ and _obj_thread_ builds. I need to change the NVCCFLAGS variable - copy the content of the LDFLAGS to NVCCFLAGS, but modify:
-Wl,--enable-new-dtags  --> -Xlinker=--enable-new-dtags
-Wl,-rpath='/path.../'   -->  -Xlinker=-rpath,'/path.../'

and then, everywhere where the NVCCFLAGS and LDFLAGS are combined, remove the LDFLAGS, i.e.

    .cu.lo:
            NVCC="$(NVCC)" libtool --mode=compile --tag=CC $(top_srcdir)/nvcc_wrap $(NVCCFLAGS) $(LDFLAGS) -I$(top_builddir)/ -I$(top_srcdir)/  -c $< -o $@

to

    .cu.lo:
            NVCC="$(NVCC)" libtool --mode=compile --tag=CC $(top_srcdir)/nvcc_wrap $(NVCCFLAGS) -I$(top_builddir)/ -I$(top_srcdir)/  -c $< -o $@

as far I could find, this should not be a problem of CP2K, but of the CMake. On some places, they report that Cmake 3.12+ should make the trick, unfortunately, it does not seem to be the case here - I use the toolchain provided 3.15.1

title: Fix Periodic RTP (issue 187)
description:
Hotfixes issue #187 187
The problem:
If a delta pulse for RTP is applied periodically the inital MOs for the perturbation are calculated via first order perturbation theory on the ground state MOs.
For calculations employing either GAPW or hybrid functionals the qs_env did not contain the correct KS matrix used as starting point for the PT.
This seems to be fixed by the code provided.

However, for a more elegant solution the interface between RTP and a usual ground state SCF run if INITIAL_WFN is set to SCF_WFN should consider all these cases.

title: Drop support for non-OpenMP builds
description:
We now got CPUs with [64 cores](https://www.amd.com/de/products/cpu/amd-ryzen-threadripper-3970x) per socket. Hence, running serial binaries will become very rare. On the other hand supporting non-OpenMP binaries has a real maintenance cost to it, in particular for the toolchain. 

Users who still want to run with a single thread, e.g. the high throughput folks,  can use `OMP_NUM_THREADS=1`. We know from our [scaling tests](https://dashboard.cp2k.org/archive/scaling/index.html) that the performance overhead for OpenMP is negligible.

title: SIRIUS: Switch to CP2K's parser for UPF files.
description:
I think there are currently two problems with the way SIRIUS reads pseudo potentials:

1.  SIRIUS uses a custom json format while UPF files are [commonly](https://www.materialscloud.org/discover/sssp) distributed in the [XML format](https://www.quantum-espresso.org/pseudopotentials/unified-pseudopotential-format). For the conversion users have to run the [upf_to_json.py](https://github.com/electronic-structure/SIRIUS/blob/develop/apps/upf/upf_to_json.py) tool, which is an unnecessary burden.

2. SIRIUS handles the parsing [all by itself](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/atom_upf.F#L120), which leaves the CP2K datastructures uninitialized, including e.g. `pot%zion`. We [once](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/595591190969e81cdeaa0688b21cdc2aab1c7497) parsed the json file twice, which was just a brittle workaround.

In my opinion, the clean solution is to use [CP2K's UPF parser](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/atom_upf.F#L180) and then pass the values on to SIRIUS. While CP2K's parser is probably not yet up to the task, we can look at Quantum ESPRESSO's [implementation](https://github.com/QEF/q-e/blob/master/Modules/read_upf_v2.f90) for inspiration.

title: How should we detect left-hand-side (lhs) reallocations ?
description:
We want to avoid using lhs-reallocs because they can introduce hidden copies, which can be hard to debug performance issues. Furthermore, it can hide out-of-bounds bugs. Generally, most Fortran developers are not aware of the feature and expect allocatables to behave like pointers.

Recently, our toolchain tests missed the [introduction](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/commit/63f2e15396cd4d5323c594035dca6ba31437099) of reallocs in an `OMP REDUCTION`. To catch those in the future, I'm considering to add `-Werror=realloc-lhs` to all toolchain arch files. Unfortunately, for newer GCC versions one then also has to use `-finline-matmul-limit=0`, which is why we current only have it in `sdbg` and `pdbg`. While inlining helps the performance, the potential reallocation will hurt it. So, maybe disabling inlines is still a net-win?

Alternatively, we could just enable OpenMP for `sdbg` and `pdbg`.

RTP velocity representation
description:
Include expectation value calculation of
- electric dipole moment
- electric quadrupole moment
in their velocity representation for RTP.

In the presence of pseudo potentials additional commutator terms are included for
- the velocity representation of the electric dipole moment
- the velocity representation of the electric quadrupole moment
- the magnetic dipole moment.

The input includes in DFT%PRINT%MOMENTS new keywords
- VEL_REPRS to switch on velocity representations
- COM_NL to include the commutator terms

additionally a second reference point can be included via the keywords
- SECOND_REFERENCE_POINT (logical)
- REF_2 (COM, COAC, ZERO, USER_DEFINED)
- REF_POINT_2 (x y z)

In DFT%REAL_TIME_PROPAGATION the new keyword
- COM_NL to include the commutator term for a delta pulse in the velocity representation
was added.

title: Inconsistencies when reading reference trajectories in MD
description:
I have been made aware of problems regarding the parsing of reference trajectories in `MOTION%MD%REFTRAJ`. I had a look hoping for a quick fix but the issue is actually quite complex.

In `*.xyz` files, the second line of each frame is reserved for a description. In principle anything could be written there, in any format. As we can see at line 1538 of `motion/integrator.F` (development version of CP2K), the program expects a well defined format, `FMT="(T6,I8,T23,F12.3,T41,F20.10)"`, from which it extracts frame index, time and potential energy. If this returns an error, then the frame index is read from the `md_env` and time and energy are set to zero.

This may fail in different ways:
- If the frame description starts with `#` or `!` the line is simply ignored and the first atomic position is interpreted as the description instead (general property of `cp_parser_type`)
- Because of `T6` in the expected format, any description of 5 characters or less will not cause an error at reading time. Hence CP2K interprets this as a frame with index, time and energy zero
-  The `STRIDE` keyword combined with non CP2K-format descriptions results in frames indices with increments of one, and not the value of stride

This can be problematic for users analyzing trajectories coming from different programs or homemade scripts.

I am not sure how this should be tackled. Issuing a warning in the manual could be a start.

I played around with `tests/QS/regtest-sparsity/Ar-ref-1.inp` to produce these errors.

title: prettify: properly define our own logger
description:
Previously we partially re-used the `fprettify-logger` tag, which would
have led to problems of missing extra attributes if the corresponding
logger calls would have ever been called (luckily we don't do proper
unittesting) and for the cases of `fprettify-logger` tag re-use we
had to define a line number attribute we didn't have (so, set to 0).

Instead we now proudly and properly define our own logger tag.

title: Enabled ADMM with GAPW
description:
This PR aims at enabling ADMM together with the GAPW method.

The main challenge was to define, compute and store the `AUX_FIT` atomic soft and hard densities. This was achieved  with minimal refactoring by storing alternative types (*e.g.* a new `qs_kind_set`) in the `admm_env` and generalizing GAPW routines to accept external variables (for example by using an external `qs_kind_set` and not the default `qs_env%qs_kind_set`). See `admm_types.F` for the alternative types and the `calculate_vxc_atom` subroutine in `qs_vxc_atom.F` for an example of generalized GAPW routine.

The impact on the rest of the code was kept low. Only an additional (automatically generated) `AUX_FIT_SOFT` basis type and a `exc1_aux_fit` energy contribution were added.

The new feature was thoroughly tested, including for forces (in geometry optimization and MD). Relevant tests were added in `tests/QS/regtest-admm-gapw`

**Notes:** 
- Real time propagation and `admm_dm` methods are not supported
- ADMM as a whole could use some refactoring. I think that all relevant variables (such as `matrix_ks_aux_fit` or `rho_aux_fit`) should be part of the `admm_type` and not the more general `qs_ks_env_type`. Similarly, the calculation of  the aux_fit neighbor lists, task_list, etc. should take place in ADMM specific routines and not the general QS ones. This is material for another PR though.

title: Ready multi-GPU support
description:
There is a clear trend towards having multiple GPUs per node and users start asking for it.

While DBCSR and SIRIUS each on their own support multi-GPU, there is anecdotal evidence of [interference](https://github.com/cp2k/dbcsr/issues/205) between them.

Unfortunately, we don't have a multi GPU dashboard test yet. The reason is that I first want to finish rewriting the [do_regtest](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/tools/regtesting/do_regtest) script in order to mitigate the startup overhead for libcusmm's JIT compilation.

In the mid-term we should also add support for multiple GPUs per MPI rank.

Reproducer:

```
$ cd cp2k/tools/toolchain

$ cd ..

collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
```

title: obscure Make errors when .git directory is missing
description:
Building CP2K from a recent git checkout, without the .git subdirectory (in my case because I was using [EasyBuild](https://github.com/easybuilders/easybuild)) results in obscure Make errors that took me a long time to figure out.

The cause is the following: `tools/build_utils/get_revision_number` exits with return code 1, but does not print an error message.   As a result, the build fails somewhere in the middle of compilation, Make reports "Error 1", but it's not clear what caused the error.

One of the following changes to `get_revision_number` or the Makefile could make life easier:
- print (on stderr) an error message that explains the problem if the .git directory is missing, or
- keep the script as it is, but use `exit 0` after echoing `<unknown revision>`, so the build can continue (instead of current `exit 1`). Perhaps print a warning on stderr just to inform the user.
- allow the user to proviide GIT_REVISION as a Make variable, and don't run `get_revision_number` if that variable is set.

title: Cubic scaling RPA/GW/SOS-MP2: remove legacy code
description:
This removes all code that has been replaced with a new implementation
based on DBCSR tensors. The following features have not been adapted to
the new implementation and are removed:
- Modified Atomic Orbitals (MAOs)
- full k-points for GW

The input section `IM_TIME` has been simplified and the number of
keywords/parameters has been reduced. Input for the RI metric has
been moved to a section `RI_METRIC`.

Using the cubic scaling variant of RPA is now as simple as that:
```
&WFC_GPW
   EPS_FILTER 1.0E-09 ! DBCSR filtering parameter affecting accuracy/performance
&END WFC_GPW
&IM_TIME
   MEMORY_CUT 4 ! memory reduction by factor 4
&END IM_TIME
```

title: Cubic scaling RPA/GW: different operators for RI metric
description: Supported RI metrics: Coulomb / truncated / short-range / long-range

title: XAS_TDP| RI metric + refactoring
description:
An additional feature was added to speed-up the computation of 3-center exchange integrals, which in some situations turned out to be a computational bottleneck. Before, the V-approximation was exclusively used. Now, additional metrics (SR, TC and overlap) can be invoked (note that using the overlap as a metric is known as the SVS-approximation).

Some refactoring also took place. All 2-center integrals needed for the RI are now computed with the libint library and the SYEVR diagonalization option has been removed. For the latter, tests have shown that although usually faster than SYEVD, SYEVR is systematically **way** outperformed by ELPA. Hence, for the sake of input simplicity  and code stability, the option and all related code were removed.

title: Add srLDA
description:
This PR adds a short-ranged LDA functional plus some regtests with range-separated Double-Hybrids.

This implementation is based on code by Paola Gori-Giorgi (in F77). I improved its performance. Second derivatives will be added later.

title: prettify: make sure we prefer the included fprettify over any other
Libint 2- and 3-center refactoring + 4-center fix
description:
This PR can be seen as an extension of #634 

The 2- and 3-center integrals in `libint_2c_3c.F` have been slightly refactored. Changes include:
- passing a `hfx_potential_type` instead of a bunch of different arguments (omeg, cutoff_radius, etc.)
- primitive gaussian radius based screening for 2-center integrals
- addition of the identity operator (overlap)

The overlap integrals have been carefully tested with `ab_overlap` and `overlap3` as references. 

This development lead to the discovery that the 4-center overlap integrals in the HFX code were wrong, as something else than the (ss|ss) overlap was provided to libint as a starting point. It has been fixed using the knowledge gained through 2- and 3-center overlaps.

Note that this correction does not affect the regtests and should have no effect on previous results since (practically?) nobody actually uses the identity potential for HFX calculations

Unfortunately, it requires ruby and might therefore not be suitable for pre-commit. Nevertheless, running it in the CI should be fine.

Furthermore, it would be nice to check our docs for broken links. Since external sites might be flaky we could restrict the check to cp2k.org and intra-repro links.

Use VmHWM from /proc/self/status to measure max_memory.
description:
Currently, [m_max_memory](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/src/base/machine.F#L93) is determined by taking the maximum across all calls to `m_memory()`. Therefore, it's noisy and we've got several calls to `m_memory()` [scatter](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/20fe0099bce4444784cb4990292e1d9e993a51dd/src/mp2_ri_gpw.F#L417-L418) throughout the code base.

Instead we should read `VmHWM` from [/proc/self/status](http://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/proc.5.html), which directly gives us the _Peak resident set size ("high water mark")_.

title: Feature/curate benchmarks
description:

title: [input] unit specification is case sensitive
description:
initially reported by @rybkinjr:

The documentation for units for example in `&COORD` says: `ANGSTROM`, but in fact it has to be all lowercase `angstrom`, otherwise one gets the CPABORT `Unknown unit: Angstrom` (or `Bohr`, ...).

Possible fixes:

* fix the documentation (disable the auto-capitalization of default options)
* make the thing case insensitive (but at which point of the parsing)
* add specific aliases

description:
### As mentioned:
 ###  First of all, fftw-3.3.8 was successfully installed. Can someone give me some advice.

```
[root@master toolchain]# ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh -j --with-cmake=install --with-libsmm=install --with-libxsmm=install --with-elpa-install
MPI is detected and it appears to be MPICH
nvcc not found, disabling CUDA by default
Compiling with --with-cmake=install processes.
==================== Finding binutils from system paths ====================
path to ar is  /usr/bin/ar
path to ld is  /usr/bin/ld
path to ranlib is  /usr/bin/ranlib
==================== Finding make from system paths ====================
path to make is  /usr/bin/make
==================== Finding GCC from system paths ====================
path to gcc is  /usr/bin/gcc
path to g++ is  /usr/bin/g++
path to gfortran is  /usr/bin/gfortran
Found include directory /usr/include
Found lib directory /usr/lib64
==================== Getting proc arch info using OpenBLAS tools ====================
OpenBLAS detected LIBCORE = haswell
OpenBLAS detected ARCH    = x86_64
==================== Finding MPICH from system paths ====================
path to mpirun is  /home/group_huang/software/mpich-install/bin/mpirun
path to mpicc is  /home/group_huang/software/mpich-install/bin/mpicc
path to mpif90 is  /home/group_huang/software/mpich-install/bin/mpif90
path to mpic++ is  /home/group_huang/software/mpich-install/bin/mpic++
libmpi is found in ld search path
libmpicxx is found in ld search path
Found lib directory /home/public/intel/compilers_and_libraries_2019.3.199/linux/mpi/intel64/lib/release
==================== Installing LAPACK ====================
lapack-3.8.0 is already installed, skipping it.
==================== Finding MKL from system paths ====================
MKLROOT is found to be /home/public/intel/compilers_and_libraries_2019.3.199/linux/mkl
libm is found in ld search path
libdl is found in ld search path
==================== Installing FFTW ====================
fftw-3.3.7.tar.gz is found
Installing from scratch into /home/public/cp2k/cp2k-6.1/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.7
**ERROR: (/home/public/cp2k/cp2k-6.1/tools/toolchain/scripts/install_fftw.sh, line 39) Non-zero exit code detected.**
```
And **install_fftw.sh, line39:**
```
#!/bin/bash -e
[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" ] && SCRIPT_NAME="${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" || SCRIPT_NAME=$0
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$SCRIPT_NAME")" && pwd -P)"

source "${SCRIPT_DIR}"/common_vars.sh
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}"/package_versions.sh
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}"/tool_kit.sh
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}"/signal_trap.sh

with_fftw=${1:-__INSTALL__}

[ -f "${BUILDDIR}/setup_fftw" ] && rm "${BUILDDIR}/setup_fftw"

FFTW_CFLAGS=''
FFTW_LDFLAGS=''
FFTW_LIBS=''
FFTW_LIBS_OMP=''
! [ -d "${BUILDDIR}" ] && mkdir -p "${BUILDDIR}"
cd "${BUILDDIR}"
case "$with_fftw" in
    __INSTALL__)
        echo "==================== Installing FFTW ===================="
        pkg_install_dir="${INSTALLDIR}/fftw-${fftw_ver}"
        install_lock_file="$pkg_install_dir/install_successful"
        if [ -f "${install_lock_file}" ] ; then
            echo "fftw-${fftw_ver} is already installed, skipping it."
        else
            if [ -f fftw-${fftw_ver}.tar.gz ] ; then
                echo "fftw-${fftw_ver}.tar.gz is found"
            else
                download_pkg ${DOWNLOADER_FLAGS} \
                             https://www.cp2k.org/static/downloads/fftw-${fftw_ver}.tar.gz
            fi
            echo "Installing from scratch into ${pkg_install_dir}"
            [ -d fftw-${fftw_ver} ] && rm -rf fftw-${fftw_ver}
            tar -xzf fftw-${fftw_ver}.tar.gz
            cd fftw-${fftw_ver}
            ./configure  --prefix=${pkg_install_dir} --libdir="${pkg_install_dir}/lib" --enable-openmp > configure.log 2>&1
            **make -j $NPROCS > make.log 2>&1**    **#line39**
            make install > install.log 2>&1
            cd ..
            touch "${install_lock_file}"
        fi
        FFTW_CFLAGS="-I'${pkg_install_dir}/include'"
        FFTW_LDFLAGS="-L'${pkg_install_dir}/lib' -Wl,-rpath='${pkg_install_dir}/lib'"
        ;;
    __SYSTEM__)
        echo "==================== Finding FFTW from system paths ===================="
        check_lib -lfftw3 "FFTW"
        [ $ENABLE_OMP = "__TRUE__" ] && check_lib -lfftw3_omp "FFTW"
        add_include_from_paths FFTW_CFLAGS "fftw3.h" $INCLUDE_PATHS
        add_lib_from_paths FFTW_LDFLAGS "libfftw3.*" $LIB_PATHS
        ;;
    __DONTUSE__)
        ;;
    *)
        echo "==================== Linking FFTW to user paths ===================="
        pkg_install_dir="$with_fftw"
        check_dir "${pkg_install_dir}/lib"
        check_dir "${pkg_install_dir}/include"
        FFTW_CFLAGS="-I'${pkg_install_dir}/include'"
        FFTW_LDFLAGS="-L'${pkg_install_dir}/lib' -Wl,-rpath='${pkg_install_dir}/lib'"
        ;;
esac
if [ "$with_fftw" != "__DONTUSE__" ] ; then
    FFTW_LIBS="-lfftw3"
    FFTW_LIBS_OMP="-lfftw3_omp"
    if [ "$with_fftw" != "__SYSTEM__" ] ; then
        cat <<EOF > "${BUILDDIR}/setup_fftw"
prepend_path LD_LIBRARY_PATH "$pkg_install_dir/lib"
prepend_path LD_RUN_PATH "$pkg_install_dir/lib"
prepend_path LIBRARY_PATH "$pkg_install_dir/lib"
prepend_path CPATH "$pkg_install_dir/include"
EOF
        cat "${BUILDDIR}/setup_fftw" >> $SETUPFILE
    fi
    # we may also want to cover FFT_SG
    cat <<EOF >> "${BUILDDIR}/setup_fftw"
export FFTW_CFLAGS="${FFTW_CFLAGS}"
export FFTW_LDFLAGS="${FFTW_LDFLAGS}"
export FFTW_LIBS="${FFTW_LIBS}"
export FFTW_LIBS_OMP="${FFTW_LIBS_OMP}"
export CP_DFLAGS="\${CP_DFLAGS} -D__FFTW3 IF_COVERAGE(IF_MPI(|-U__FFTW3)|)"
export CP_CFLAGS="\${CP_CFLAGS} ${FFTW_CFLAGS}"
export CP_LDFLAGS="\${CP_LDFLAGS} ${FFTW_LDFLAGS}"
export CP_LIBS="${FFTW_LIBS} IF_OMP(${FFTW_LIBS_OMP}|) \${CP_LIBS}"
EOF
fi
cd "${ROOTDIR}"


# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Suppress reporting of known leaks
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
cat <<EOF >> ${INSTALLDIR}/valgrind.supp
{
   <BuggyFFTW3>
   Memcheck:Addr32
   fun:cdot
   ...
   fun:invoke_solver
   fun:search0
}
EOF
```

description:
When building the toolchain with `--math-mode=mkl` the SpFFT library does not pick up MKL.
This is why the CUDA Pascal test is [currently failing](https://dashboard.cp2k.org/archive/cuda-pascal/index.html).

Later when compiling CP2K one gets the following warnings:
```
/usr/bin/ld: warning: libmkl_intel_lp64.so, needed by /opt/cp2k-toolchain/install/SpFFT-0.9.8/lib/../lib/libspfft.so, not found (try using -rpath or -rpath-link)
```

title: Silent fallback to internal mass definition for elements when loading from PDB
description:
When loading a structure from a PDB file it seems that CP2K silently ignores a given [MASS keyword](https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/FORCE_EVAL/SUBSYS/KIND.html#MASS) in the `KIND` section for the matched chemical element, while still using other values set in the `KIND` section.
While a resulting `RESTART` file contains the coordinates of the atoms copied from the input PDB, the initial `MASS` in the `KIND` section is preserved, making it completely non obvious that effectively the mass either from the PDB and/or the default mass for the element have been used (have to investigate further which one it is).
This could be related to the fact that in the tested PDB the name of the atoms were given as `HW1`, `HW2`.

More details will follow...

title: Making XAS_TDP regtests compatible for libint compiled with maxl=4
description:
XAS_TDP regtests and more specifilcally `Ne-HF-extRI_XAS.inp` are causing some  failures in the case where libint is compiled with maxl=4. The issue arises in the initialization of the `HFX` environment which also sets up libint for derivatives.

To solve that problem without asking everyone to recompile libint with maxl=5, I switched from a HF  to a LDA calculation. The regtest still fulfills its purpose of including an external RI basis set while avoiding the problematic routine call.

title: [toolchain] broken with macOS
description:
The toolchain seems to be largely untested with macOS.

The setup:

* macOS Mojave (10.14.6)
* XCode 11 + matching Command Line Tools
* Homebrew-based gfortran/gcc

Using simply `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` will not work: `gcc` points to `clang`, which doesn't support `-fopenmp`. First one to complain is OpenBLAS, which can be made to build with `--with-openmp=no`, but our `fftw` script does not support building without OpenMP-support in the compiler.

Using `CC=gcc-9 CXX=g++-9 ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` fails in OpenBLAS since those vars are inconsistently forwarded: CMake picks them up correctly, OpenBLAS does not.

Using `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --with-gcc=/usr/local/Cellar/gcc/9.2.0_1/` fails since it always assumes a multilib-system (where `lib64` is available):

```
==================== Linking GCC to user paths ====================
Found directory /usr/local/Cellar/gcc/9.2.0_1/bin
Found directory /usr/local/Cellar/gcc/9.2.0_1/lib
ERROR: (./scripts/install_gcc.sh) Cannot find /usr/local/Cellar/gcc/9.2.0_1/lib64
ERROR: (./scripts/install_gcc.sh, line 21) Non-zero exit code detected.
```

title: Changes to the LibXC API
description:
Compiling the current development version of cp2k with the current development version of LibXC showed some API changes. For example the function `xc_f03_mgga(...)` from LibXC seems to provide third derivatives (and requires the corresponding arguments). It is also possible that the order of arguments might have changed slightly.

The functions get called in `xc/xc_libxc.F` from cp2k and is implemented in `libxcf03.f90`.

title: Run formatting tests only on changed files (determined by Git)
Xas tdp libint2
description:
This pull request is for a new feature/method in CP2K named XAS_TDP (X-ray Absorption Sepctroscopy based on Time-Dependent density functional Perturbation theory). It allows for computation of core level excitation energies and oscillator strengths up to the hybrid-GGA TDDFT level of theory, for both molecules and solids. A RI scheme exploiting the locality of the core donor states is used, which should make calculation for large system possible and scalable (benchmarks are yet to be done though).

This method is quite independent from the rest of the code and thus, most of the new code is contained in the following new files: 

- `xas_tdp_types.F` definition of the types used throughout the method
- `xas_tdp_methods.F` general routines, I/O, etc.
- `xas_tdp_utils.F` the more computationally intensive routines 
- `xas_tdp_atom.F` where the numerical XC kernel integration takes place (on atomic grids)
- `xas_tdp_kernel.F` where the kernel matrices are built, the RI 3-center integrals are contracted, and generally all that is kernel related takes place (except for XC kernel integration)

Note that although TDDFT implementations where already around, it made sense to start from scratch as a lot of case specific optimization can be done (due to the nature of XAS). 

Along the way, some other part of the code were modified to fit the need of the method. In particular, the following features were added:
- 3-center integrals from libint2 (new file: `libint_3center.F`, modifications in `hfx_libint_wrapper.F` and `qs_o3c_methods.F` )
- 2-center Obara-Saika  integrals for the truncated Coulomb operator (modifications in `generic_os_integrals.F`, `ai_operators_r12.F`, moved `t_c_g0.F` to common/)
- Special neighbor lists where centers b of a,b pairs only cover a subset of all atoms (modifications in `qs_neighbor_lists.F`) 

The rest of the modifications to the main CP2K code are rather small and mostly involve making some routines public or the infrastructure needed for a new `RI_XAS` basis.

The code was successfully tested for consistency versus other programs (mostly Orca and some Turbomole) on small molecules.

Edit: the commit history was cleaned-up via git rebasing and regtests were added

title: Fix for sirius options. More tests second try
description:
Something is fishy with the CI. The CI wants to compile thing during the convention check

this pull request contains a correction to an error introduced in the last SIRIUS pre-release. An include file was mistakenly included in the release which corrupted the list of options. Unfortunately, I realized the error too late and by snowball effect landed in cp2k.

- Updated the toolchain to the new SIRIUS prerelease version.

- cp2k now support full potential computations (through sirius). I had to force cp2k not to interpret the json potential file all the time.

- some improvements in the autotools build (well generate only the arch files)

- Added more tests for the SIRIUS backend. But we need more tests especially for magnetic materials and molecular dynamics computations.

title: Longrange potential for MP2 and RPA
description:
This PR enables ...
- ... longrange potentials for MP2 and RPA. It is a first step to enable range-separated double-hybrid functionals with range-separation within the correlation part.
- ... Eigenvalue decompositions as an alternative for the Cholesky decomposition in RI. (necessary for longrange potentials)
- ... lots of regtests.

Misc:
Refactoring and Splitting of some modules in the MP2 code

title: Verify recent versions of spglib with CP2K
description:
Current master of CP2K has the following check:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/5566d17405ab6f351561817a83675b3344ac2a5a/src/cryssym.F#L168-L170

with spglib-1.9.9 being released in 2016 and the toolchain shipping 1.12.2.

Counterintuitively I get this warning even with `FORCE_EVAL/DFT/KPOINTS/SYMMETRY` set to `.FALSE.`

Do we have a test set for spglib-based functionality?

title: Create package pw_env
description: This is a preparation for creating a gpw_acc package (see #458).

title: Segmentation fault when using Hirshfeld in CDFT
description:
Hi,

I got a seg fault when running CDFT using Hirshfeld while Becke is totally fine. The problem is in both cp2k/5.1 and cp2k/6.1 but not cp2k/7.0. 

I believe the seg fault comes from line 856 of qs_scf_output.F of branch cp2k/5.1 where you try to access the constraint_type from group section which does not exist for Hirshfeld input structure. The suspicious usage of "group % constraint_type" for Hirshfeld happens many times elsewhere in the code.

Best,
Chenghan

title: too many communicators
description:
I am getting the following crashes reproducibly after several thousand time steps:

 *******************************************************************************
 *             MPI error 672742415 in mpi_cart_sub @ mp_cart_sub : Other MPI   *
 *   ___         error, error stack:
PMPI_Cart_sub(228)..................:     *
 *  /   \        MPI_Cart_sub(comm=0xc4040010, remain_dims=0x7f200ce27e80,     *
 * [ABORT]                       comm_new=0x2b5549318b64)                      *

PMPI_Cart_sub(167)..................:            *
 *    |                  
MPIR_Comm_split_impl(253)...........:               *
 *  O/|       
MPIR_Get_contextid_sparse_group(604): Too many communicators   *
 * /| |                 (0/32768 free on this process; ignore_id=0)            *
 * / \                                                    dbcsr_mpiwrap.F:1098 *
 *******************************************************************************

           14 mp_cart_sub
           13 dbcsr_complete_redistribute
           12 copy_dbcsr_to_fm
           11 cp_dbcsr_sm_fm_multiply
           10 make_basis_sm
            9 reorthogonalize_vectors
            8 wfi_extrapolate
            7 scf_env_initial_rho_setup
            6 init_scf_run
            5 qs_energies
            4 qs_forces
            3 velocity_verlet
            2 qs_mol_dyn_low
            1 CP2K

It seems that there is a communicator leak--more communicators are created than destroyed. This is using the latest code for github . I am using 2019 intel compilers and mkl ,
but it seems to be a problem in cp2k dbscr. I am running psmp hybrid with 384 cores and OMP_NUM_THREADS=4 . I am stuck until I can get pas this.

Thanks!

Ron

CPP in arch file
description:
If I do set (to gcc) or no declared CPP in the arch file, I get the following error:
```
*** The MPI_Comm_f2c() function was called before MPI_INIT was invoked.
*** This is disallowed by the MPI standard.
*** Your MPI job will now abort.
```
whenever I run PSMP version.
The only way to get it to work is to use
```
CPP =
```
in the arch file. From what I see, we only test this case in the dashboard, so my conclusion is that there is something wrong and untested with CPP. Should we remove this variable at all?

title: CP2K prints wrong source code revision number
description:
Steps to reproduce:

1. compile cp2k development version
2. run cp2k executable with `--version`
3. change and `git commit` any file in `src`
5. recompile cp2k
6. run cp2k executable with `--version`

The last step shows that the version number has not been updated.

Probably [this line](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/Makefile#L431) does not work as intended.

parallel_rng_types: refactoring and "objectification"
description:
This `rng_stream_type` is one of the most low-level/basic objects I found. To see how a complete OO approach could look like, I converted it to Modern Fortran.

Points to discuss:

* object initialization: while it looks like a constructor, there are differences to what people usually call a constructor. With that in mind, it might be clearer to create a type-bound procedure `init(...)` instead.
* finalization: this object does not have any pointer components which would require special care, which is why there is no `final` routine
* since I didn't want to adapt the whole code yet, I've added a separate `..._TEST` program to test this part without most of the other infrastructure

To build and test:

```
make ARCH=... VERSION=... parallel_rng_types_TEST
./exe/$ARCH/parallel_rng_types_TEST.popt
```

There is not much here yet, except for the syntactic sugar. More to come when adapting the code to the changes and when going up the "foodchain" (tackling the globenv).

Generalize mpirank contraints in TEST_DIRS
description:
Several directories in [TEST_DIRS](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/master/tests/TEST_DIRS) have a list of hard-coded permissible mpiranks, e.g.:
```
mpiranks==2||mpiranks==4||mpiranks==6||mpiranks==16||mpiranks==24
```

These expressions are evaluated via awk, which has the full suite of [arithmetic operatores](https://www.gnu.org/software/gawk/manual/html_node/Arithmetic-Ops.html) available. Hence, we should generalize these expressions, e.g. like this:
```
mpiranks%2==0
```

Toolchain: lapack compilation error with INT_ETIME
description:
`cat /etc/os-release`
```
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:7"
HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"
```
Tried installing all with the main installer script, but failed with a generic error on the lapack installation, so I drilled down to the lapack installer.
The lapack installer in `tools/toolchain/scripts/install_reflapack.sh` tries to compile lapack with the value `TIMER = INT_ETIME` in the generated `make.inc` file, and I had to change to `TIMER = INT_CPU_TIME` to compile successfully. 
Can this variable be set based on the fortran compiler found or make this the default?

title: Toolchain: ELPA download fails
description:
The download of ELPA in the toolchain fails, at least on my Ubuntu 18.04.2. The reason is certificate verification:

```
$ wget https://elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de/html/Releases/2017.05.003/elpa-2017.05.003.tar.gz
--2019-06-26 17:18:49--  https://elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de/html/Releases/2017.05.003/elpa-2017.05.003.tar.gz
Resolving elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de (elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de)... 130.183.17.20
Connecting to elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de (elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de)|130.183.17.20|:443... connected.
ERROR: cannot verify elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de's certificate, issued by ‘CN=MPG CA - G02,O=Max-Planck-Gesellschaft,L=Muenchen,ST=Bayern,C=DE’:
  Unable to locally verify the issuer's authority.
To connect to elpa.mpcdf.mpg.de insecurely, use `--no-check-certificate'.
```

As per the error message, adding `--no-check-certificate` helps. Could that be added to the toolchain at least as an option, given that checksums are calculated anyway?

Improve CPUID hint for cases where CFLAGS are not in sync with FCFLAGS
description:
The translation unit base/machine_cpuid.c relies on the (same) target flags as the Fortran code, otherwise it is impossible (with the current implementation) to determine which target flags have been given at compile-time of CP2K.

If m_cpuid_static() returns MACHINE_CPU_GENERIC, then it is likely CFLAGS did not carry the same target flags as FCFLAGS. Instead of hinting "The compiler target flags used to build this binary are insufficiently exploiting the extensions which are available for this CPU model", the hint "Consider compiler target flags as part of FCFLAGS and CFLAGS (ARCH file)" is given.

Prettified the source code.

title: Script for planning MPI/OMP-hybrid execution

The material is taken from the XCONFIGURE project (https://github.com/hfp/xconfigure/) and hence it is keeping the copyright banner and license as it is used there (BSD 3-clause).

Do you think, we should further add a hint to INSTALL.md?

title: libxc warning "unstable" functional for untested
description:
libxc introduced the functionality to get a "stable/unstable" flag per functional.
Unfortunately this seems to be a tri-state at the moment: "stable/unstable/unknown", with almost all functionals being "unknown". In CP2K we're treating "unknown" (the absence of "stable") as "unstable", leading to the simplest (and most likely) stable functionals being reported as "unstable".
We must handle this differently. I see two possibilities at the moment:

1. remove the check again
2. treat "unknown" as "stable"

@fstein93 more ideas?

title: Initial version of the autotools arch generation
description:
This pull request does not change anything in the build system whatsoever and is **not** intended to replace the toolchain to build cp2k dependencies. Instead, it use autotools (a simple generated configure shell script) to detect all dependencies installed on the machine. It generates the different versions of the arch files according to the libraries found on the machine. It is possible to start from these generated arch files and teak them (with optimizations parameters mostly)

It is not perfect but works on fedora, rhel, summit (ornl), and basically should work on any descent unix systems with bash. The configure script will use pkg-config (if it finds it) or rely on the good old fashion way to detect libraries. Note that the arch files are **not** copied in the arch directory to avoid confusion. 

You will need automake, autoconf, m4 and autoconf-archives If you want to generate the configure script but you need to do it only one time. Dependencies can be deactivated with the without-dependency paradigm. CUDA, mkl, and fftw have additional options accessible with a simple

./configure --help

Comments are most welcome.

NB : it is under GPL by default

title: Regtest script, split runs and collect results
description:
Currently, the regtest script runs the collection of results at the end, just after the run of the results:

https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/blob/20ad1a1b303df6bd121f68b2a6f3c6a576bba03e/tools/regtesting/do_regtest#L1140

It turns out that this procedure is very inefficient on Daint since we run in batch and it is sequential (a slow because of disk access). Therefore I would propose to have two phases:

1) running of tests in parallel (i.e. in batch)
2) collection of results in sequential (i.e. on the login node)

This can be achieved by adding a new option to the regtest script, something like:

default (no option): runs tests and collect results
-collect=no: runs only tests
-collect=yes: only collect results

Any opinion?

title: libint2 in Debian and Fedora lack Fortran interface
description:
It seems the libint2-dev packages in [Debian](https://packages.debian.org/stretch/amd64/libint2-dev/filelist) and [Fedora](https://www.rpmfind.net/linux/RPM/fedora/devel/rawhide/x86_64/l/libint2-devel-2.1.0-9.fc30.x86_64.html) are lacking the Fortran interface. Since building libint from scratch takes quite a bit of time, maybe the toolchain could install the Fortran interface separately?

In the long run we should of course try to convince the package maintainers to include the Fortran API.

title: wavelet solver wrong with lengths > 640
description:
Wavelet solver with 0D boundary conditions gives incorrect results for FFT lengths 648, 675, 720.

Box of 37x37x37 works 38x38x38 gives incorrect Hartree energy in the file below:

&FORCE_EVAL
  METHOD QS
  &DFT
    UKS T
    BASIS_SET_FILE_NAME BASIS_MOLOPT
    &MGRID
      CUTOFF 800
      REL_CUTOFF 60
    &END MGRID
    &QS
       METHOD GPW
       EPS_DEFAULT 1.0E-12
       EPS_PGF_ORB 1.0E-15
    &END QS
    &SCF
      EPS_SCF 1.0E-07
      MAX_SCF 1
      SCF_GUESS RESTART
      &OT ON
        MINIMIZER DIIS
        PRECONDITIONER FULL_ALL
        ENERGY_GAP 0.005
      &END OT
      &OUTER_SCF
        EPS_SCF 1.0E-07
        MAX_SCF 0
      &END OUTER_SCF
    &END SCF
   &XC
      &XC_FUNCTIONAL PBE
      &END XC_FUNCTIONAL
   &END XC
   &POISSON
     PERIODIC NONE
     POISSON_SOLVER WAVELET
   &END

  &END DFT
  &SUBSYS
    &COORD
      H 0.5 0.5 0.5
      SCALED
    &END
    &CELL

&END GLOBAL

Furthermore, this might be an on-ramp for new developers.

title: Linking issue libxc
description:
Dear CP2K developers,

I'm new using CP2K and I'm having issues compiling it. The problem that I'm facing is linking it against libxc. I get the following error,

    ~/cp2k/src/xc/xc_libxc_wrap.F:33:23:
    xc_f03_func_init, &
    Error: Symbol ‘xc_f03_func_set_ext_params’ referenced at (1) not found in module ‘xc_f03_lib_m’

and others of the same kind. I tried with my on installation of libxc (which worked well with other ab initio programs) and the automatic installation from the `./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` script.

Running `grep` to check if the routine `xc_f03_func_init` is in libxc, I do find it in the library, but not in the module files present in the include directory

    grep -nri "xc_f03_func_init" ~/libxc-4.3.4/
    Binary file ~/libxc-4.3.4/lib/libxcf03.so.5.3.4 matches
    Binary file ~/libxc-4.3.4/lib/libxcf03.a matches

Do you have an idea of what goes wrong in my linking?

I'm trying to compile the master branch. I used the following option with the `install_cp2k_toolchain.sh` script

    ./install_cp2k_toolchain.sh --with-libxsmm=install --with-fftw=system --enable-cuda --enable-omp --gpu-ver=P100 --mpi-mode=no --with-libxc=~/libxc-4.3.4 --with-cmake=system

Below are the arch files

title: Allow global keywords to be overridden by environment variables
description:
I looked at `keyword_create` (perhaps there is better place?) and wanted to allow the environment to supply the value of a keyword (environment variable). To confirm, the value of a keyword should be overridden (rather than the default value). The parser infrastructure of CP2K may already allow an implementation, however it is not completely clear how to implement this when looking at the various candidate functions.

This enhancement can be used e.g., for benchmarks/competitions where modifying the workload/input is not allowed. More general, such a feature allows to quickly tryout a modification and to experiment with different settings. This feature might also enable automated interaction e.g., auto-tuning the performance of a workload.

The following code may illustrate the idea:

```fortran
CHARACTER(LEN=default_string_length) :: env
INTEGER :: env_stat

CALL GET_ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLE("CP2K_"//name, env, STATUS=env_stat)
IF (0 .EQ. env_stat) THEN
  ! parse value of "env" according to keyword-type
END IF
```

The prefix `CP2K_` for the keyword's name can sufficiently ensure an intentional interaction.

title: toolchain: Error linking ELPA with MKL
description:
I'm using CP2K 6.1. When building ELPA using the toolchain script, it fails to link with MKL, reporting as follows:

```
  FCLD     test_real_double_hermitian_multiply_1stage_all_layouts
/opt/spack/spack-avx512/opt/spack/linux-debian9-x86_64/gcc-8.2.0/intel-mkl-2019.1.144-a4wlgyiw6bj7f3too3q6wf4ly3n5mjc4/                compilers_and_libraries_2019.1.144/linux/mkl/lib/intel64/libmkl_sequential.a(dlaed3_seq.o): In function `mkl_lapack_dlaed3':
dlaed3_omp_gen.f:(.text+0x3): undefined reference to `mkl_lapack_xdlaed3'
/opt/spack/spack-avx512/opt/spack/linux-debian9-x86_64/gcc-8.2.0/intel-mkl-2019.1.144-a4wlgyiw6bj7f3too3q6wf4ly3n5mjc4/                compilers_and_libraries_2019.1.144/linux/mkl/lib/intel64/libmkl_sequential.a(dlaed2_seq.o): In function `mkl_lapack_dlaed2':
dlaed2_omp_gen.f:(.text+0x3): undefined reference to `mkl_lapack_xdlaed2'
/opt/spack/spack-avx512/opt/spack/linux-debian9-x86_64/gcc-8.2.0/intel-mkl-2019.1.144-a4wlgyiw6bj7f3too3q6wf4ly3n5mjc4/                compilers_and_libraries_2019.1.144/linux/mkl/lib/intel64/libmkl_sequential.a(dgeqrf_pf_seq.o): In function `mkl_lapack_dgeqrf_pf':
dgeqrf_pf_omp.c:(.text+0xa3): undefined reference to `mkl_lapack_xdgeqrf_pf'
dgeqrf_pf_omp.c:(.text+0xd9): undefined reference to `mkl_lapack_xdgeqrf_pf'
```

The error message is long, so I only pasted the first few errors.

I dag into this error, and found it caused by the lost of  `-Wl,--start-group` and `-Wl,--end-group` pair. `install_mkl.sh` prepares the library string with `-Wl,--start-group` and `-Wl,--end-group`, so that MKL libraries can be linked in any order. But when configuring ELPA, it lost this pair somewhere. According to the `config.log` generated by ELPA, it kept `-Wl,--start-group` and `-Wl,--end-group` for the first few command, until configuring `ac_cv_fc_libs`.

I searched around the master branch and found this issues seems not be fixed yet.

title: Rely on CONTIGUOUS keyword in MPI wrapper
description: Since CP2K moved to F08, the CONTIGUOUS keyword can be used. MPI requires contiguous buffers hence the wrapper can safely make that statement. In fact, this fixes occurrences of "created temporary array" (unveiled with related GNU Fortran flags). This change can be taken further by additing more uses of the CONTIGUOUS even in the MPI wrapper module.

title: Rely on mpi_comm_set_errhandler instead of mpi_errhandler_set
description: Rely on `mpi_comm_set_errhandler` instead of `mpi_errhandler_set`. The conditional compilation checks for MPIv3 (`__MPI_VERSION`) which is reasonable but may be not exactly related (see for instance [here](https://www.open-mpi.org/faq/?category=mpi-removed)). However, it is reasonable to assume that MPI function names are standardized since MPIv3 (portable MPI-ABI between different implementations of MPI). Fixed a few source code comments (typos).

title: Fpga interface for pw fft computations
description:
[summary.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/3202563/summary.txt)
[timings.txt](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/3202564/timings.txt)

Added APIs for computing single and double precision forward and backward fft3d
with intel fpgas set using `-D__PW_FPGA` and `-D__PW_FPGA_SP` flags. Tested for
FW_R3DC1D and BW_C1DR3D calculations for sizes 16^3, 32^3, 64^3 fft3d. If sizes
not found, falls back to CPU FFT computations.
Included host and kernel code that work with uniform fft3d sizes.

Attached files show the summary of regressions tests executed using the double 
precision fpga build.

title: [RFC] OO Implementation of SCF Inner Loop/Diagonalization
description:
This is a Request for Comments and while it compiles it likely contains bugs and subtle changes.

The idea here is to try an Object-oriented approach to structure part of the SCF Inner Loop with the goal to increase cohesion of code, meaning that everything related to a diagonalization method (or concretely the `new_mos` routine) is isolated in a single class, rather than distributed between multiple functions (resulting in a single `CASE`-switch before entering the SCF cycle).

In principle a `new_mos` class has to bring the following:

* a `init` routine, which is currently called before entering the outer SCF
* a `pre` routine which gets called at the beginning of each inner SCF and can be used to (re-)generate the preconditioner (hence `pre`)
* a `run` routine which does the actual diagonalization

New GCC versions break toolchain builds
description:
I'm using GCC 7.40 with CUDA 10.0 to build the main branch on a system. Everything proceeds smoothly with the toolchain and then when I go to build cp2k using the generate setup file and the generated arch file, it breaks with the following error:

```
gfortran -c -march=native -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g  -O1  -fopenmp  -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/fftw-3.3.8/include' -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libint-1.1.6/include' -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxc-4.3.4/include' -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/libxsmm-1.10.0/include' -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/gsl-2.5/include' -I/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/spglib-1.12.2/include -I/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/hdf5-1.10.4/include  -I'/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/tools/toolchain/install/json_fortran-7.0.0/include' -ffree-form -std=f2008 -fimplicit-none -fsanitize=leak -fcheck=all -ffpe-trap=invalid,zero,overflow -finit-derived -finit-real=snan -finit-integer=-42 -Werror=realloc-lhs-all -finline-matmul-limit=0 -Werror=aliasing -Werror=ampersand -Werror=c-binding-type -Werror=intrinsic-shadow -Werror=intrinsics-std -Werror=line-truncation -Werror=tabs -Werror=target-lifetime -Werror=underflow -Werror=unused-but-set-variable -Werror=unused-variable -Werror=unused-dummy-argument -Werror=conversion -Werror=zerotrip -Werror=uninitialized -Wno-maybe-uninitialized -Wuse-without-only  -D__ACC -D__DBCSR_ACC -D__PW_CUDA -D__CUDA_PROFILING -D__LIBXSMM  -D__FFTW3  -D__LIBINT -D__LIBINT_MAX_AM=6 -D__LIBDERIV_MAX_AM1=5 -D__LIBXC  -D__SPGLIB   -D__JSON -D__HAS_IEEE_EXCEPTIONS -D__CHECK_DIAG  -D__COMPILE_ARCH="\"kodiak-gcc-cuda\"" -D__COMPILE_DATE="\"Fri May 10 16:46:52 MDT 2019\"" -D__COMPILE_HOST="\"ko-fe1.lanl.gov\"" -D__COMPILE_REVISION="\"git:158053f\"" -D__DATA_DIR="\"/turquoise/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/data\"" -D__SHORT_FILE__="\"xc/xc_rho_cflags_types.F\"" -I'/turquoise/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/src/xc/' -I'/turquoise/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/obj/kodiak-gcc-cuda/sdbg/exts/dbcsr' xc_rho_cflags_types.F90 
/turquoise/usr/projects/icapt/dog/benchmarking/cp2k/src/xc/xc_rho_cflags_types.F:61:0:

    SUBROUTINE xc_rho_cflags_setall(cflags, value)
 
Error: ‘xc_rho_cflags_type.0’ is used uninitialized in this function [-Werror=uninitialized]
f951: some warnings being treated as errors
make[3]: *** [xc_rho_cflags_types.o] Error 1
make[2]: *** [all] Error 2
make[1]: *** [sdbg] Error 2
make: *** [all] Error 2
```

The problem is that the `-finit-derived` flag combined with the `-Werror-uninitialized` flag are causing subroutines to be flagged if uninitialized variables are passed into them, even though the variables are marked as `INTENT(IN)`.

The work-around for now is to add the flag `-finit-local-zero`, which "instructs the compiler to initialize local INTEGER, REAL, and COMPLEX variables to zero, LOGICAL variables to false, and CHARACTER variables to a string of null bytes."

Can this be added as part of the routine build process?

title: Build DBCSR via the toolchain
description:
Currently, DBCSR is built inside CP2K and their two build systems are very much intertwined. As a result, issues such as #338 can arise, and some fixes inside DBCSR's build system https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/pull/345#issuecomment-491226981 require a new DBCSR release before they can be reflected in CP2K. 

What is more, DBCSR maintains two build systems (Makefile to be compiled by cp2k and cmake to compile DBCSR as a standalone). with all the errors this could cause.

--- 

What about delegating the build of DBCSR to CP2K's toolchain (i.e. treat DBCSR like all other CP2K dependencies), letting the toolchain use DBCSR's cmake for building, but still keeping DBCSR inside CP2K's tree as a git submodule as is the case now? 

The main motivations for this suggestion are:
- Removes from CP2K the burden of compiling DBCSR correctly and having intimate knowledge of DBCSR's Makefile as well as the variables it needs. The consistency between DBCSR's compilation flags would be handled by DBCSR's cmake
- Avoids having to maintain two build systems in DBCSR (cmake & make) as is currently the case
- DBCSR is kept in CP2K's tree as a git submodule for those who want to develop DBCSR & CP2K together (if I recall correctly, this was the original motivation for having git as a submodule?)
- This suggestion would effectively be a step towards making DBCSR a fully external library, but still allows keeping DBCSR as a git submodule inside CP2K.

If you think this is a useful step, I would be happy to open a PR with a prototype.

title: [exts/dbcsr] remove CXXOMPFLAGS upon updating DBCSR to new release
description:
DBCSR's build system is slightly changed with the introduction of https://github.com/cp2k/dbcsr/pull/184. 

When upgrading DBCSR to the newest release, the variable `CXXOMPFLAGS` should be removed from the arch files and from `tools/toolchain/scripts/generate_arch_files.sh` (essentially reversing PR #345). 

Instead, the variable `CXXFLAGS` should contain the OpenMP compilation flags.

title: m_cpuid_static (C impl.): there seems to be no way to determine target flags in Fortran code
description:
* Similar to other Fortran compilers, `gfortran -E -dM -mavx - < /dev/null | grep AVX` defines a variety of predefined macros (also similar to C).
* However, with a Fortran translation unit only a subset of these definitions disappears (`gfortran -E -dM -mavx my.F | grep AVX`).

To demonstrate and use `m_cpuid_static()` and `m_cpuid()`, a new (useful) CPWARN is introduced: "_the compiler target flags used to build this binary are insufficiently exploiting the extensions which are available for this CPU model_". The warning is shown after presenting the "CPU model name" (global environment output during CP2K's startup).

Given that we gain more options over time (and the combinatorial complexity they introduce) I think it is crucial to cut-down on old library support and make sure that what we provide is also tested.

base/machine: m_cpuid() function to test cpu/architecture/features
description:
Implemented m_cpuid() function, which allows to test for CPU/architecture/features. Instead of testing for certain bits using bit-ops, a range is checked. This is more manageable also in the light of multiple different CPU architectures supported by CP2K (e.g., ARM, Power, etc.).

A fully fledged CPUID functionality is typically based on feature bits for a single vendor. However, this would quickly exhaust the number of bits of an integer number (or make the signature of such function complicated aka vendors-id, etc). Though, CP2K needs to support multiple CPU architectures/vendors. Moreover, such fine granular feature bits would be too much for a scientific code to specialize for. Hence this PR proposes an easy to use enumeration that can be used for CPU, architecture as well as features such as instruction set extensions. A feature would be tested on a range given by lower/upper bound.

title: Feature/pre-commit
description:
To use the pre-commit hooks install `pre-commit` with the following
instructions:

  https://pre-commit.com/#install

then run ONCE in your CP2K clone:

    pre-commit install

After that - before every commit - all the scripts configured in
`.pre-commit-config.yaml` will run on the files added in that
commit.

If any file changed (for example because they were modified by the
`prettify` script), the commit will be interrupted for you to
investigate and adding the prettyfied file to the commit if
necessary.

title: Feature/update sirius
description: @juerghutter did you plan on using `kgrid`/`kshift` in `pwdft_env_type` later on or is it OK if I drop them?

title: Enable ELPA by default if compiled into CP2K
description:
I will add the label "*Needs discussion*" since I can imagine that some objectives are not the same for others...

**This PR enables more reasonable defaults for ELPA** (*opinion*):

1. Enables ELPA by default (if compiled into the binary),
2. Select ELPA-kernel according to CPUID.

The latter point uses LIBXSMM's CPUID functionality to select the kernel.

**Further change** (*can be questioned*): Introduced CP2K_DIAG_LIBRARY environment variable to adjust default of PREFERRED_DIAG_LIBRARY without using the keyword in the input file (see also the note below). This environment variable allows to quickly disable using ELPA even if it is compiled into CP2K (e.g., CP2K_DIAG_LIBRARY=1 selects basic ScaLAPACK instead of ELPA).

**Comments**: CP2K's ELPA support until this patch is somwhat a deviation from how (optional) libraries are handled i.e., ELPA may be compiled-in but it is never used unless specified by a keyword. This is a surprise for the user who compiled with ELPA for performance reasons.

---

**Notes**

* One can imagine that global keywords in CP2K's input may have corresponding environment variables (could be even an option when registering a keyword).
* This change somewhat obscures the established ELPA-tests since now every test potentially exercises ELPA (if compiled into the binary).
* This PR passes regression tests. However, error margins have to be adjusted since ELPA is always used when compiled int the binary.

title: toolchain: set nvcc flags
description:
Adds `-O3` to the nvcc flags set by the toolchain script. 
Fixes #311.

title: Docker: Make build more quiet and resilient
[sirius] integration broken with SIRIUS-6.1.5
description:
I found the following issues so far:

- [x] keywords change: `K_GRID` → `NGRIDK`, `K_SHIFT` → `SHIFTK`, `&PARAMETER` → `&PARAMETERS`, `&SOLVER` → `&ITERATIVE_SOLVER`, ... and some more. Need adaptation in tests and the interface, see https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/compare/master...dev-zero:feature/update-sirius
- [x] `terminate called after throwing an instance of 'nlohmann::detail::type_error'`, `what():  [json.exception.type_error.302] type must be array, but is number` when specifying `NGRIDK` after adjusting the interface to the name change
- [ ] `terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::bad_alloc'`, `what():  std::bad_alloc` when omitting `NGRIDK` completely, respectively fixing the parser error (e92d5d1)

title: metadynamics quartic wall
description:
Dear all

There might be a typo in the code to calculate the wall potential (of a quartic wall)

see `efunc` - what is written there is effectively `diff_ss**5` instead of the expected `diff_ss**4`
the fact that the derivative of the energy `efunc` is correct i.e. `diff_ss**3` supports this conclusion.

As far as I see only the post-processing would be affected by this typo - but also only in a very limited number of cases as for example graph.F does not even utilize wallpotential of the COLVAR-1.metadynLog file

`SUBROUTINE meta_walls(meta_env)` (metadynamics_utils.F)
...
`CASE (do_wall_quartic)`
`diff_ss = colvars(ih)%ss0-colvars(ih)%walls(iwall)%pos0`
...
`efunc = colvars(ih)%walls(iwall)%k_quartic*diff_ss*diff_ss**4`
`dfunc = 4.0_dp*colvars(ih)%walls(iwall)%k_quartic*diff_ss**3`

Further the definition of the quartic wall potential in the manual (https://manual.cp2k.org/trunk/CP2K_INPUT/MOTION/FREE_ENERGY/METADYN/METAVAR/WALL/QUARTIC.html)

Does not correspond to the code in
`SUBROUTINE metavar_read(metavar, extended_lagrange, langevin, icol, metavar_section)` (metadynamics_utils.F)
` metavar%walls(i)%pos0 = metavar%walls(i)%pos+(0.05_dp/metavar%walls(i)%k_quartic**(0.25_dp))`

instead of 1/(K^0.25) as specified in the manual 0.05/(K^0.25) is calculated (which is unpleasant but not really critical just adjust the manual)

description:
When building CP2K with support for SIRIUS, I get the following when running `cp2k --help`:

```console
$ cp2k.popt --help

 cp2k.popt [-c|--check] [-e|--echo] [-h|--help] [--html-manual]
           [-i] <input_file>
           [-mpi-mapping|--mpi-mapping] <method>
           [-o] <output_file>

 starts the CP2K program, see <https://www.cp2k.org/>

 The easiest way is cp2k.popt <input_file>

 The following options can be used:

  -i <input_file>   : provides an input file name, if it is the last
                      argument, the -i flag is not needed
  -o <output_file>  : provides an output file name [default: screen]

 These switches skip the simulation, unless [-r|-run] is specified:

  --check, -c       : performs a syntax check of the <input_file>
  --echo, -e        : echos the <input_file>, and make all defaults explicit
                      The input is also checked, but only a failure is reported
  --help, -h        : writes this message
  --html-manual     : writes a HTML reference manual of the CP2K input
                      in the current directory. The file index.html is a good
                      starting point for browsing
  --license         : prints the CP2K license
  --mpi-mapping     : applies a given MPI reordering to CP2K
  --run, -r         : forces a CP2K run regardless of other specified flags
  --version, -v     : prints the CP2K version and the revision number
  --xml             : dumps the whole CP2K input structure as a XML file
                      xml2htm generates a HTML manual from this XML file
  

 *** WARNING in input/input_keyword_types.F:284 :: keyword XC_FUNCTIONALS ***
 *** assumed undefined type by default                                    ***
```

@mtaillefumier unfortunately this only happens when building CP2K with SIRIUS support.

title: update libint -> libint2
description:
After this PR, CP2K will use libint 2.5 (or upwards) instead of outdated libint 1. Since libint 2.5 comes with Fortran bindings, the compilation process of CP2K and the libint interface of CP2K become simpler. In particular the flags `-D__LIBINT_MAX_AM` and `-D__LIBDERIV_MAX_AM1` are no longer needed for CP2K.

After merging this PR, CP2K will no longer support libint 1.

Libint can be easily updated by running the `cp2k/tools/toolchain/install_cp2K_toolchain.sh` script of this pull request branch. If you use a CP2K that was not built with the toolchain script, see below for instructions on how to update libint manually.

## Instructions to compile a CP2K-compatible libint 2:

Download a libint generated for CP2K from [here](https://github.com/cp2k/libint-cp2k/releases/latest) (one of the libint tarballs, not the source code archive). The `lmax-5` library is recommended for most users/developers.

After extracting the tarball, run
```
./configure --prefix=[libint2 install directory] --enable-fortran \
    CXX=[C++ compiler] CXXFLAGS=[C++ compiler flags] FC=[Fortran compiler]

    make
    make install
```
For `CXXFLAGS` don't use `-g` flag since it will increase library size by a large factor (`-g1` seems to be ok though).

More detailed instructions for building libint can be found here:
https://github.com/evaleev/libint/wiki#compiling-libint-library

## Instructions to compile CP2K with Libint 2:

Change CP2K arch file: 
- remove the flags `-D__LIBINT_MAX_AM` and `-D__LIBDERIV_MAX_AM1`
- Add `-I'[libint2 install directory]/include'` to `FCFLAGS`
- Add `-L'[libint2 install directory]/lib'` to `LDFLAGS`
- Replace `-lint` by `-lint2` and remove `-lderiv`.

title: Cubic scaling RPA with DBCSR tensors
description:
This uses DBCSR tensors for cubic scaling RPA. This is not yet ready for merge, we need to wait for cp2k/dbcsr#149.

This does not change the input in `IM_TIME` section but parameters `EPS_FILTER_IM_TIME`, `EPS_FILTER`, `GROUP_SIZE_3C` and `GROUP_SIZE_P` need to be chosen differently for optimal performance and accuracy than with old implementation.

The following features are not yet implemented:
* Cubic scaling GW
* PAOs

This pull request will not remove the old implementation of cubic scaling RPA since Cubic scaling GW is still based on the old code. PAOs appear to be not useful in the current implementation so it should be removed at a later time.

title: cp2k output not printing but the job is running
description:
Dear developers,

I'm contacting you because I'm facing a problem with the program and I'm not able to find any solution for it.

I'm trying to run some B3LYP calculations with CP2K. However I noticed that while the job is running it is not writing anything on the output. 
 
Please see examples below:
Example 1:
 
rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18   3232 Mar 20 20:49 TMEDAC2H4CO2.inp
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 142632 Mar 20 20:49 TMEDAC2H4CO2-GSpace-SplInterp-
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 115876 Mar 20 22:19 TMEDAC2H4CO2-RESTART.wfn
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 796828 Mar 20 22:19 TMEDAC2H4CO2.out
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18   1354 Mar 20 22:49 slurm-12494799.out
 
It started to run at 20:49, stopped writing at 22:19 and the calculation finished at 22:49 due to time limit.
 
Example 2: 
 
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18   3485 Mar 21 13:04 TMEDAC2H4CO2.inp
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18     37 Mar 21 13:04 slurm-12505393.out
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18  15848 Mar 21 13:04 TMEDAC2H4CO2-GSpace-SplInterp-
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 115876 Mar 21 13:07 TMEDAC2H4CO2-RESTART.wfn
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18  98350 Mar 21 13:07 TMEDAC2H4CO2.out
 
It started to run at 13:04, the last update on the output file was at 13:07 and at 13:35 was still running without writing anything.
 
It finished at 15:04 but the last updated was at 14:33 and it finished at 15:04 due to time limit.
 
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18   3485 Mar 21 13:04 TMEDAC2H4CO2.inp
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18  15848 Mar 21 13:04 TMEDAC2H4CO2-GSpace-SplInterp-1.ddapcLog
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 115876 Mar 21 14:33 TMEDAC2H4CO2-RESTART.wfn
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18 110167 Mar 21 14:33 TMEDAC2H4CO2.out
-rw-r--r-- 1 gpareras sm18   1354 Mar 21 15:04 slurm-12505393.out
 
I'm wondering if this is normal or there is an error on the input, I've never seen that before, usually the output file is getting updated all the time while the job is running.

I've been in contact with my super computer provider and they couldn't find any explanation for this either.

Please find attached below a folder with: input, output and submission script. 

Any help would be really appreciated.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Gerard
[Test.tar.gz](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/files/3019461/Test.tar.gz)

title: mpich-3.3 fails to configure
description:
There seems to be bug in mpich's configure script. When they call configure in `test/mpi/dtpools` it fails because it can't find `mpicc` (see below). While dtpools seems to be a new feature `--disable-dtpools`  does not help either. Then it simply fails somewhere else in the test suite because it can't find `mpi.h`.

I guess, this problem did not appear in the CI because there mpich was installed a second time. I don't have more time to spend on this. So, maybe someone else could have a look, or we simply rollback to `mpich-3.2.1`.


```
$ grep -B 24  "more details" build/mpich-3.3/test/mpi/dtpools/config.log

./configure: line 2655: mpicc: command not found

| /* confdefs.h */

| #define PACKAGE_TARNAME "dtpools"
| #define PACKAGE_VERSION "0.0"
```

description:
Opefully this pull request will finally work with the CI and include modifications suggested in the diverse comments of my last closed pull request.

This pull request contains all necessary changes to configure SIRIUS using cp2k input files syntax. All  options are given through the interface which means that if new runtime options are added in sirius they will automatically be present without modification of cp2k code. At worst linking against SIRIUS is needed at best nothing should be done (if shared libraries are used).

- Sirius options are automatically documented. Documentation might be a bit simple but most parameters are self explanatory. 
- test cases are now following cp2k input standard. 
- Another important update is the presence of van der Waals corrections in sirius. The implementation uses libvdwxc (originally developed for octopus) as a backend. Forces and stresses are also there but test cases will be needed.

title: Slim down the toolchain
description: The aim of the [toolchain](https://github.com/cp2k/cp2k/tree/master/tools/toolchain) is to provide a simple reproducible way of building CP2K's dependencies. Naturally, the toolchain can not replace a full Linux distribution and has to have dependencies of its own. With fb915d00caf535890ffeaf89243b0f3f6474581a we added a few more of such dependencies. Hence, we should revisit if components like `make`, `cmake`, `binutils`, and `lcov` can also become external dependencies.

this pull request contains all necessary changes to configure SIRIUS using cp2k input files syntax. title: [regtesting] set CP2K_DATA_DIR environment variable to use data in source directory
description: Tests are usually run between the build and installation steps. If `CP2K_DATA` is set to point to the final location after installation but installation is not done already, regtests may fail to find referenced files and/or load files from the current installation dir.

title: Input preprocessor should export essential built-in settings as variables
description:
The input preprocessor should export built-in settings like the data directory (built-in, can be overwritten in the environment) and configuration flags as pre-defined `$VARIABLES`.

This could then be a more generic replacement for the `@XCTYPE` directive:

```
@INCLUDE "${_DATADIR}/xc_section/PBE.sec"
```

or allow for more generic input files.

Currently we're not injecting any variables.
